tests.name: Rational Numbers Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 8 Rational Numbers CBSE Online Test 01 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Find the reciprocal of -2.
questions.explanation:
The reciprocal of any number is given by:
{tex}\frac{1}{n u m b e r}{/tex}
So reciprocal of -2 = {tex}\frac{1}{-2}{/tex}

questions.question: Write the rational number that is equal to its reciprocal
questions.explanation: The answer is 1 because reciprocal of 1 is  {tex}1\over 1{/tex}which is equal to 1

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
The additive inverse of any given number is the same number with the opposite sign.
Here the given number is {tex}2\over 3{/tex}. So its additive inverse will be - {tex}2\over3{/tex}

questions.question: Find the multiplicative inverse of -13.
questions.explanation:
Multiplicative inverse or ( reciprocal ) of any given number is given by
{tex}1\over number{/tex}
Here, given number = -13 , so its multiplicative inverse = {tex}-1\over 13{/tex}

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex}(-5) = -5 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex} = 1
questions.explanation: Commutative property

questions.question: A number which can be written in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 is called a ________.
questions.explanation: Every rational number is in the form of {tex}p\over q{/tex}where q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0

questions.question: Sum of two rational numbers is a ________
questions.explanation: Sum of any two rational number is a rational number for example {tex}1\over2{/tex} + {tex}2\over3{/tex} = {tex}7\over6{/tex} which is a rational number, {tex}1\over2{/tex} + ({tex}—1\over2{/tex}) = 0 and 0 is an integer and all integers are rational number.

questions.question: For any three rational numbers a, b and c, a + (b + c) = ________.
questions.explanation:
The answer is ( a + b) + c ,
for example,
5 + ( 6 + 7 ) = 5 + 13 = 18 and
(5 + 6 ) + 7 = 11 + 7 = 18

questions.question: ________ = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} a = a for any rational number a.
questions.explanation: Any number multiplied with 1 is the number itself

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{7} + \left( { - \frac{6}{{11}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{8}{{21}}} \right) + \frac{5}{{22}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}3\over7{/tex} + (-{tex}6\over11{/tex}) + (-{tex}8\over21{/tex}) + {tex}5\over22{/tex}
=[({tex}3\over7{/tex})+(-{tex}8\over21{/tex})] + [(-{tex}6\over11{/tex}) + {tex}5\over22{/tex}]
=[{tex}3\times3-1\times8\over21{/tex}] +[{tex}-6\times2+1 \times5\over22{/tex}]
=[{tex}9-8\over21{/tex}  ]+ [{tex}-12+5\over22{/tex}]
={tex}1\over21{/tex}-{tex}7\over22{/tex}
=[{tex}1\times22-7\times21\over462{/tex}]
=[{tex}22-147\over462{/tex}]
= {tex}-125\over462{/tex}

questions.question: Find the reciprocal of {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{5}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
The reciprocal of any number is given by 
{tex}1\over number{/tex} Here given number is {tex}-1\over5{/tex}. So its reciprocal is {tex}\frac{1}{\frac{-1}{5}}{/tex} = -5

questions.question: {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = ________.
questions.explanation: Any number multiplied with 1 is the number itself, as 1 is the multiplicative identity of rational numbers.

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The additive inverse of any number is the same number with the opposite sign. Here given number is {tex}4\over5{/tex}, so its additive inverse is —{tex}4\over5{/tex}

questions.question: Find the multiplicative inverse of {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
The multiplicative inverse or reciprocal of any  number is given by {tex}1\over number{/tex}
Here the number is {tex}1\over4{/tex}, so its multiplicative inverse is {tex}\frac{1}{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}= 4

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation: Both 1st part i.e = {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 1 and 2nd part i.e = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex} the number( {tex}—1\over5{/tex})  is multiplied by 1 and 1 is said to be the multiplicative identity of rational numbers.

questions.question: A number which can be written in the form{tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are ________ and q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 is called a rational number.
questions.explanation: p and q can be any integer but q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0

questions.question: ________ are closed under addition.
questions.explanation: Rational numbers are closed under addition which means that if we add two rational numbers we will get a rational number as the sum.

questions.question: ________ is not associative for rational numbers.
questions.explanation:
Subtraction or division is not associative for rational numbers,
for example, {tex}7\over2{/tex}—{tex}3\over2{/tex}—{tex}2\over2{/tex}= 1, whereas
{tex}3\over2{/tex}—{tex}7\over2{/tex}—{tex}2\over2{/tex}= —3 here, both the results are not the same.
21 {tex}\div{/tex} 7 =3 and 7 {tex}\div{/tex} 21 = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}, here also both the results are not the same.

questions.question: 1 is the ________ for rational numbers.
questions.explanation: 1 is the multiplicative identity  for rational numbers as any number multiplied by 1 gives the same number

questions.question: Find: {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{5} \times \frac{3}{7} \times \frac{{15}}{{16}} \times \left( {\frac{{ - 14}}{9}} \right){/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}{-4\over5}\times{/tex} {tex}{3\over 7}\times{/tex}{tex}{15\over16}\times{/tex}({tex}-14\over9{/tex})

={tex}{-12\over35}\times{/tex}{tex}-210\over144{/tex}

={tex}6\over12{/tex}

={tex}1\over2{/tex}

questions.question: Find a rational number between {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
One rational number between {tex}1\over4{/tex}and {tex}1\over2{/tex}
LCM of 4 and 2 = 4
{tex}1\over 4{/tex} = {tex}1\times1\over1\times4{/tex} = {tex}1\over4{/tex}
{tex}1\over2{/tex} = {tex}1\times2\over2\times2{/tex} = {tex}2\over4{/tex}
So one rational number between {tex}1\over4{/tex}and {tex}2\over 4{/tex}
{tex}1\over4{/tex} = {tex}1\times2\over 4\times2{/tex} = {tex}2\over 8{/tex}
{tex}2\over 4{/tex} = {tex}2\times2\over 4\times2{/tex} = {tex}4\over 8{/tex}
So rational number between {tex}2\over 8{/tex}and {tex}4\over 8 {/tex} = {tex}3\over 8{/tex}

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex}.
questions.explanation: Commutative property as in both LHS and RHS have the same numbers after multiplied.

questions.question: {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = ________.
questions.explanation: Any number multiplied with 1 is the same number as 1 is the multiplicative identity of rational numbers.

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{6}{7}{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The additive inverse of any number is the same number with the opposite sign. Here the number is {tex}6\over 7{/tex} so its additive inverse is {tex}-6\over 7{/tex}

questions.question: Find the multiplicative inverse of {tex}\frac{2}{9}{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The multiplicative inverse or reciprocal of any number is given by {tex}1\over number{/tex}here, the number is {tex}2\over9{/tex} so its multiplicative inverse is {tex}\frac{1}{\frac{2}{9}}{/tex} = {tex}9\over2{/tex}

questions.question: ________ are the only rational numbers that are equal to their reciprocals.
questions.explanation: The answer is 1  and -1 because the reciprocal of 1 is {tex}1\over1{/tex} which is 1 and the reciprocal of -1 = {tex}1\over-1{/tex} = -1

questions.question: A number which can be written in the form, {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex} where p and q are integers and ________ is called a rational number.
questions.explanation: The answer is q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 as, if q becomes equal to 0 than the value will become undefined.

questions.question: ________ are closed under subtraction.
questions.explanation: Rational numbers are closed under subtraction which means if one rational number is subtracted from another rational number the answer will be a rational number.

questions.question: The product of two rational numbers is always a _______.
questions.explanation: The product of two rational numbers is always a rational number as, if we multiply any two rational numbers the product is a rational number ( with the exception of 0)

questions.question: Zero has ________ reciprocal
questions.explanation: Zero has no reciprocal because if we take the reciprocal of 0 that is {tex}1\over0{/tex} the value will become undefined and it will no longer be a rational number.

questions.question: Find: {tex}\frac{2}{5} \times \frac{{ - 3}}{7} - \frac{1}{{14}} - \frac{3}{7} \times \frac{3}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}{2\over5}\times{/tex}{tex}-3\over7{/tex}—{tex}1\over14{/tex}—{tex}{3\over7}\times{/tex}{tex}3\over5{/tex}
= {tex}3\over7{/tex}[{tex}-2\over5{/tex}—{tex}3\over5{/tex}]—{tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}3\over7{/tex}[{tex}-2-3\over5{/tex}] —{tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}3\over7{/tex}[{tex}-5\over5{/tex}]—{tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-3\over7{/tex}—{tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-3\times2-1\times1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-6-1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-7\over14{/tex}
= {tex}-1\over2{/tex}

questions.question: What should be subtracted from -5/4 to get -1?
Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{9}{8}{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The additive inverse of any number is the same number with the opposite sign. Here the number is {tex}9\over8{/tex} so its additive inverse is {tex}-9\over8{/tex}

questions.question: 0 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation: The answer is 0 because any number when multiplied by 0 the result becomes 0

questions.question: Find the multiplicative inverse of {tex} - \frac{9}{{13}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The multiplicative inverse or (reciprocal) of any number is given by {tex}1\over number{/tex}.Here the number is {tex}-9\over13{/tex}, so its reciprocal is {tex}\frac{1}{\frac{-9}{13}}{/tex}  = {tex}-13\over 9{/tex}

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation: The answer is commutative property as the same numbers are multiplied on both RHS and LHS.

questions.question: A number which can be written in the form ________, where p and q are integers and q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 is called a rational number.
questions.explanation: The answer is {tex}p\over q{/tex} as any number which is in this form is a rational number provided q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0

questions.question: For any two rational numbers a and b, a + b = ________.
questions.explanation: For any two rational numbers a and b, a + b = b+a. As rational numbers are commutative under addition

questions.question: For any three rational numbers a, b and c, a {tex}\times{/tex} (b {tex}\times{/tex} c) = ________.
questions.explanation: For any three rational numbers a, b and c, a {tex}\times{/tex} (b {tex}\times{/tex} c) = (a {tex}\times{/tex} b) {tex}\times{/tex} c. As rational numbers are associative under multiplication.

questions.question: Reciprocal of{tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex}, where x {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 is ________.
questions.explanation: Reciprocal of any number is given by {tex}1\over number{/tex}, here number is {tex}1\over x{/tex}, so its reciprocal is {tex}\frac{1}{\frac{1}{x}}{/tex}= x, so the answer is x

questions.question: Tell what property allows you to compute {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times \left( {6 \times \frac{4}{3}} \right)=\left( {\frac{1}{3} \times 6} \right) \times \frac{4}{3}{/tex}
questions.explanation: The answer is associative property of multiplication  as the product follows the associative property of multiplication rule which is  {tex}a\times (b\times c){/tex}= {tex}(a\times b)\times c{/tex}

questions.question: Write the rational numbers that are equal to their reciprocals.
questions.explanation:
The answer is 1 , -1
because the reciprocal of 1 is {tex}1\over1{/tex}=1 and the reciprocal of -1 is {tex}1\over -1{/tex}= -1

questions.question: Which of the following is the identity element?
Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{12}}{{11}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The additive inverse of any number is the same number with the opposite sign. Here the number is {tex}12\over11{/tex}, so its additive inverse will be {tex}-12\over 11{/tex}

questions.question: Find the multiplicative inverse of 25.
questions.explanation: The multiplicative inverse of any number is given by {tex}1\over number{/tex}, here the number is 25, so its multiplicative inverse is {tex}1\over 25{/tex}

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{5} \times \frac{1}{2}} \right) \times \frac{1}{3} = \frac{{ - 1}}{5} \times \left( {\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex}.
questions.explanation: The property used here is  Associative property, as the product here follows the rule. {tex}(a\times b)\times c{/tex} = {tex}a\times (b \times c){/tex}

questions.question: Which of the following is neither appositive nor a negative rational number?

options.html: Such a rational number does not exist

questions.question: In general, a {tex}\times{/tex} b = _________ for any two rational numbers a and b.
questions.explanation: For any two rational numbers a and b, a {tex}\times{/tex} b = b {tex}\times{/tex} a. As rational numbers are commutative under multiplication

questions.question: ________ is called the identity for the addition of rational numbers.
questions.explanation: 0 is the identity for addition of rational numbers. As for any rational number {tex}p\over q{/tex} ,where q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0, 0 + {tex}p\over q{/tex} = {tex}p\over q{/tex} + 0 = {tex}p\over q{/tex}

questions.question: The numbers ________ and ________ are their own reciprocals.
questions.explanation:
The answer is 1, -1
Because the reciprocal of 1 is {tex}1\over 1{/tex}=1 and reciprocal of -1 is {tex}1\over -1{/tex}= —1

questions.question: Find: {tex} - \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{3}{5} + \frac{5}{2} - \frac{3}{5} \times \frac{1}{6}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
{tex}-{2\over3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{3\over5}{/tex} + {tex}5\over2{/tex} - {tex}{3\over5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}1\over6{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex}[-{tex}2\over3{/tex} - {tex}1\over6{/tex}] + {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex}[{tex}—2\times2—1\times1\over6{/tex}] + {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex}[{tex}—4—1\over6{/tex}] + {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex}[{tex}—5\over6{/tex}] + {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}-{1\over2}{/tex} + {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}—1+5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}4\over2{/tex}
= 2

questions.question: Multiply {tex}\frac{3}{{11}}{/tex} by the reciprocal of {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{{11}}{/tex},
questions.explanation:
Reciprocal of {tex}-4\over11{/tex}is {tex}-11\over4{/tex}
Product of {tex}3\over11{/tex} and {tex}—11\over4{/tex} = {tex}3\over11{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}—11\over4{/tex}
= {tex}—3\over4{/tex}

questions.question: Tell what property allows us to compute {tex}\frac{1}{2} + \left( {\frac{1}{3} + \frac{1}{4}} \right) = \left( {\frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{3}} \right) + \frac{1}{4}{/tex}
questions.explanation: The answer is Associative property of addition. Because it follows the rule of  a + ( b + c) = ( a + b ) + c

questions.question: Tell which property allows us to compute {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times \left( {3 \times \frac{1}{4}} \right) = \left( {\frac{1}{2} \times 3} \right) \times \frac{1}{4}{/tex}?
questions.explanation: The answer is the associative property of multiplication. Because it follows the rule a {tex}\times{/tex} (b {tex}\times{/tex} c) = (a {tex}\times{/tex} b) {tex}\times{/tex} c

questions.question: Write the rational number that does not have a reciprocal.
questions.explanation: 0 does not have any reciprocal because the  reciprocal of 0 will be {tex}1\over 0{/tex} which will be undefined

questions.question: Name the property under addition used in{tex}\left( {\frac{{ - 1}}{5} + \frac{1}{2}} \right) + \frac{1}{4} = \frac{{ - 1}}{5} + \left( {\frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{4}} \right){/tex}.
questions.explanation: The property used here is associative property. As it follows the rule ( a + b ) + c = a + ( b + c )

questions.question: A number which cannot be written in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q {tex} \neq{/tex} 0 is called a ________.
questions.explanation: A number which cannot be written in the form of {tex}p\over q{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q is not equal to zero is called an irrational number for example {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}, {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}, ...

questions.question: In general, a + b = ________ for any two rational numbers a and b.
questions.explanation: For any two rational numbers a and b, a + b = b + a, as rational numbers follow commutative property under addition, so the answer is b + a.

questions.question: Zero is called the identity for the ________ of rational numbers.
questions.explanation: Zero is the identity for the addition of rational numbers, for example, any two integers p and q  such that q {tex}\neq{/tex} 0, 0 + {tex}p\over q{/tex} = {tex}p\over q{/tex} + 0 = {tex}p\over q{/tex}

questions.question: The numbers 1 and -1 are their own ________.
questions.explanation: 1 and -1 are their own  reciprocals as reciprocal of 1 = {tex}1\over 1{/tex}= 1 and reciprocal of -1 = {tex}1 \over -1{/tex}= -1

questions.question: Find the reciprocal of -4.
questions.explanation: The reciprocal of any number is given by {tex}1\over number{/tex}, here number is -4 , so its reciprocal is {tex}1\over -4{/tex}

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{5}{6}{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The additive inverse of any number is the same number with the opposite sign, here the number is {tex}5\over 6{/tex}, so its additive inverse is {tex}-5\over 6{/tex}

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{7}{8}{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The additive inverse of any number is the same number with the opposite sign. Here the number is {tex}7\over 8{/tex}, so its additive inverse is {tex}-7\over 8{/tex}

questions.question: Find the multiplicative inverse of -15.
questions.explanation: The multiplicative inverse or reciprocal of any number is given by {tex}1\over number{/tex}, here the number is -15  so its reciprocal will be {tex}1\over —15{/tex}

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{3} \times ( - 3) = (- 3) \times \frac{{ - 1}}{3} = 1{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The property used here is both reciprocal and commutativity under multiplication. Reciprocal because both (-3) and its reciprocal {tex}—1\over 3{/tex}are multiplied. Commutative under multiplication because it follows the rule: a {tex}\times{/tex} b = b {tex}\times{/tex} a

questions.question: Name the property under multiplication used in {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{3} \times ( - 3) = 1{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The answer is reciprocal. Because (-3) and its reciprocal {tex}—1\over 3{/tex}are multiplied to get the answer.

questions.question: Sum of two ________ is a rational number.
questions.explanation: Sum of two rational numbers is a rational number as, for any two rational number a and b, a + b = b + a

questions.question: For any three rational numbers x, y and z, x + (y + z) = ________.
questions.explanation: For any three rational numbers x , y and z,  x + (y + z) = (x + y) + z, as rational numbers are associative under addition.

questions.question: ________ = a for any rational number a.
questions.explanation: The answer is a {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = a, as 1 is the multiplicative identity of rational numbers that is, any number multiplied by 1 gives the same number as a product.

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{7} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{14}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{8}{{21}}} \right) + \frac{5}{{21}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}3\over 7{/tex} + ({tex}-5\over 14{/tex})] + [( {tex}-8\over 21{/tex}) + {tex}5 \over 21{/tex}]
= [{tex}3 \times2 + 1 \times (-5)\over 14{/tex}] + [{tex}-8 + 5 \over 21{/tex}]
= [{tex}1\over 14{/tex}] + [{tex}-3\over21{/tex}]
= {tex}3 \times1 + (-3) \times2\over 42{/tex}
= {tex}3 -6\over42{/tex}
= {tex}-3\over42{/tex}
= {tex}-1\over14{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{4} + \left( { - \frac{5}{2}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{8}{3}} \right) + \frac{5}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}3\over4{/tex} + ({tex}-5\over2{/tex}) + ({tex}-8\over3{/tex})] + {tex}5\over5{/tex}
= [{tex}3 \times3 + ( -5) \times6 + (-8 ) \times 4\over12{/tex}] + 1
= [{tex}9 -30-32\over 12{/tex}] + 1
= {tex}-53\over12{/tex} + 1
= {tex}-53 + 12 \over12{/tex}
= {tex}-41\over12{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{5}{7} + \left( { - \frac{6}{7}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{8}{{35}}} \right) + \frac{5}{{21}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}5\over7{/tex} + {tex}-6\over7{/tex})] + [({tex}8\over 35{/tex}) + {tex}5\over21{/tex}]
= [{tex}5-6\over7{/tex}] + [{tex}-8\times3 + 5\times5\over105{/tex}]
={tex}-1\over7{/tex} + {tex}1\over 105{/tex}
= {tex}-15+1\over105{/tex}
= {tex}14\over105{/tex}
= {tex}-2\over15{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{5} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{12}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{7}{{15}}} \right) + \frac{5}{{20}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left[\frac{3}{5}+\left(\frac{-5}{12}\right)\right]+\left[\left(\frac{-7}{15}\right)+\frac{5}{20}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\frac{3 \times 12+(-5) \times 5}{60}\right]+\left[\frac{-7 \times 4+3 \times 5}{60}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\frac{36-25}{60}\right]+\left[\frac{-28+15}{60}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{11}{60}+\left(\frac{-13}{60}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{11-13}{60}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-2}{60}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-1}{30}{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{7}{8} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{16}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{3}{{16}}} \right) + \frac{5}{8}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}7\over8{/tex} + ({tex}—5\over16{/tex})] + [({tex}—3\over16{/tex}) + {tex}5\over8{/tex}]
= [{tex}7 \times2 + ( —5) \times1\over16{/tex}] + [{tex}—3\times1 + 2 \times5\over16{/tex}]
= [{tex}14—5\over16{/tex}] + [{tex}—3+10\over16{/tex}]
= {tex}9\over16{/tex} + {tex}7\over16{/tex}
= {tex}16\over16{/tex}
= 1

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{5}{9} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{18}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{7}{{18}}} \right) + \frac{7}{9}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}5\over9{/tex} + ({tex}—5\over18{/tex})] + [({tex}—7\over18{/tex}) + {tex}7\over9{/tex}]
= [{tex}5\times2 + (—5) \times1\over18{/tex}] + [{tex}—7\times1 + 2 \times7 \over18{/tex}]
= [{tex}10—5\over18{/tex}] + [{tex}—7+14\over18{/tex}]
= {tex}5\over18{/tex} + {tex}7\over18{/tex}
= {tex}12\over18{/tex}
= {tex}2\over3{/tex}

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex} - \frac{{13}}{{14}}{/tex}.

questions.explanation: The multiplicative inverse or reciprocal of any rational number is given by {tex}1\over number{/tex}, here the rational number is {tex}—2\over3{/tex}, so its multiplicative inverse will be {tex}\frac{1}{\frac{-2}{3}}{/tex} = {tex}—3\over2{/tex}

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex} - \frac{{15}}{{17}}{/tex}.

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{13}}{{17}}{/tex}.

questions.question: Find the multiplicative inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{{21}}{/tex}.

questions.question: Write the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{16}}{{19}}{/tex}.

questions.question: Find the multiplicative inverse of -23.
questions.explanation: The multiplicative inverse or reciprocal of any rational number is given by {tex}1\over number{/tex}, here the rational number is -23, so its multiplicative inverse will be {tex}1\over —23{/tex}.

questions.question: {tex}\frac{{15}}{{17}} + \left( { - \frac{{15}}{{17}}} \right){/tex} = ________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}15\over17{/tex} + ({tex}—15\over17{/tex})
={tex}15\over17{/tex} - {tex}15\over17{/tex}
= 0

questions.question: {tex}\left( { - \frac{{16}}{{19}}} \right) + \frac{{16}}{{19}}{/tex} = ________.

({tex} -16\over19{/tex}) + {tex}16\over19{/tex}

questions.question: {tex} - \frac{2}{5} \times \left( { - \frac{5}{2}} \right){/tex} = ________.

questions.question: Find {tex} - \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{3}{5} + \frac{5}{2} - \frac{3}{5} \times \frac{1}{6}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}-2\over3{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}3\over5{/tex} {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}-{tex}3\over5{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}1\over6{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex} [{tex}—2\over3{/tex}-{tex}1\over6{/tex}] {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex} [{tex}—2 \times2 —1 \times1\over6{/tex}] {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex}[{tex}—4—1\over6{/tex}] {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}3\over5{/tex}(-{tex}5\over6{/tex}) {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}—1\over2{/tex} {tex}+{/tex} {tex}5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}—1+5\over2{/tex}
= {tex}4\over2{/tex}
= 2

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{2}{5} \times \left( { - \frac{3}{7}} \right) - \frac{1}{6} \times \frac{3}{2} + \frac{1}{{14}} \times \frac{2}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}2\over5{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex}(—{tex}3\over7{/tex}) —{tex}1\over6{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}3\over2{/tex} {tex}+{/tex} {tex}1\over14{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}2\over5{/tex}

{tex}\frac{-6}{35}-\frac3{12}+\frac2{70}{/tex}

{tex}\frac{-72-105+12}{420}{/tex}

={tex}—11\over28{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{3}{7} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{14}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{9}{{14}}} \right) + \frac{5}{7}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}3\over7{/tex} + ({tex}-5\over14{/tex})] + [({tex}-9\over14{/tex}) + {tex}5\over7{/tex}]
= [{tex}3\times2+ (—5) \times1\over14{/tex}] + [{tex}—9\times1 + 5\times2\over14{/tex}]
= [{tex}6—5\over14{/tex}] + [ {tex}—9+10\over14{/tex}]
= {tex}1\over14{/tex} + {tex}1\over14{/tex}
= {tex}2\over14{/tex}
= {tex}1\over7{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{{11}}{{12}} + \left( { - \frac{5}{6}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{7}{6}} \right) + \frac{7}{{12}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}11\over12{/tex} + ({tex}-5\over6{/tex})] + [({tex}-7\over6{/tex}) + {tex}7\over12{/tex}]
= [{tex}11 \times1 + (—5) \times2\over12{/tex}] + [{tex}(—7) \times 2 +7\times1\over12{/tex}]
= [{tex}11—10\over12{/tex}] + [ {tex}—14+7\over12{/tex}]
= {tex}1\over12{/tex} + {tex}(—7)\over12{/tex}
= {tex}—6\over12{/tex}
= {tex}—1\over2{/tex}

questions.question: Find {tex}\frac{7}{8} + \left( { - \frac{5}{{16}}} \right) + \left( { - \frac{9}{{16}}} \right) + \frac{5}{8}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
[{tex}7\over8{/tex} + (-{tex}5\over16{/tex})] + [(-{tex}9\over16{/tex}) + {tex}5\over8{/tex}]
= [{tex}7\times2 + (—5) \times1\over16{/tex}] + [{tex}(—9) \times1 + 5\times2\over16{/tex}]
= [{tex}14—5\over16{/tex}] + [{tex}—9+10\over16{/tex}]
= {tex}9\over16{/tex} + {tex}1\over16{/tex}
= {tex}10\over16{/tex}
= {tex}5\over8{/tex}

questions.question: How many numbers in the following list are irrational? {tex}\sqrt 3 ,\pi ,\frac{1}{5},\sqrt[5]{3},\sqrt {49} ,\frac{{2}}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation: 3 numbers in the following list are irrational, they are {tex}\sqrt3, \pi,\sqrt[5]{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Between two rational numbers

options.html: there are only rational numbers and no irrational numbers
there are infinitely many rational numbers
there is exactly one rational number

questions.explanation: Between any two rational numbers, there are infinitely many rational numbers. For example between 2 and 3, we can write many rational numbers like 2.1, 2.2,2.3.... and we can further extend the decimal point like 2.11,2.12,2.13 if we keep on extending the decimal point we can get infinite rational numbers

questions.question: The product of any two irrational numbers is
questions.explanation: The product of any two irrational numbers is sometimes rational, sometimes irrational, for example, the product of two irrational numbers {tex}\sqrt3{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt3{/tex} = 3 which is a rational number, whereas the product of  two irrational numbers {tex} \sqrt3{/tex}  {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt6{/tex} which is an irrational number.

questions.question: Who was the first Mathematician to compute digits in the decimal expansion of {tex}\pi {/tex}?
questions.explanation: The first mathematician to compute digits in the decimal expansion of {tex}\pi{/tex} was Archimedes. He was a greek mathematician who approximated the decimal expansion of {tex}\pi{/tex}.

questions.question: If a = 2 and b = 3, then value of {tex}{\left( {\frac{1}{a} + \frac{1}{b}} \right)^a}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Given , a = 2 , b = 3 so,
{tex}{\left( {\frac{1}{a} + \frac{1}{b}} \right)^a}{/tex} = {tex}{\left( {\frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{3}} \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}{\left( {3+ 2}\over6 \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}{\left( {5}\over6 \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}25\over36{/tex}

questions.question: The two irrational numbers between {tex}\sqrt 2 {/tex} and {tex}\sqrt 3 {/tex} are
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt2{/tex} = 1.414....
{tex}\sqrt3{/tex} = 1.732...
So between 1.414... and 1.732... two irrational numbers are 1.5010010001.... and 1.601001000100001...
As all other options contains irrational numbers which do not lie between 1.414 and 1.732

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\frac{{{a^{ - 1}}}}{{{a^{ - 1}} + {b^{ - 1}}}} + \frac{{{a^{ - 1}}}}{{{a^{ - 1}} - {b^{ - 1}}}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{{a^{ - 1}}}}{{{a^{ - 1}} + {b^{ - 1}}}} + \frac{{{a^{ - 1}}}}{{{a^{ - 1}} - {b^{ - 1}}}}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{\frac{1}{a}}{\frac{1}{a} + \frac{1}{b}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{\frac{1}{a}}{\frac{1}{a} - \frac{1}{b}}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{\frac{1}{a}}{\frac{a + b}{ab}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{\frac{1}{a}}{\frac{b - a}{ab}}{/tex}
={tex}b\over b+a{/tex}+{tex}b\over b—a{/tex}
= b[{tex}1\over b+ a{/tex} + {tex}1\over b—a{/tex}]
= b[{tex}b — a + b + a\over b² —a²{/tex}]
= b[{tex}2b\over b²—a²{/tex}]
= {tex}2b²\over b²—a²{/tex}

questions.question: If r is a rational number and s is an irrational number, then r + s and r - s are ________.
questions.explanation: r is a rational number and s is an irrational number so, r + s and r - s both will be irrationals, for example let r = 2 and s = {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex}, so r + s = 2 + {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex} and r - s = 2 - {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex}  which are both irrationals.

questions.question: If {tex}\frac{1}{x} = \frac{{{x^2}}}{{27}}{/tex}, then x is ________ number.
questions.explanation:
{tex}1\over x{/tex} = {tex}x²\over 27{/tex}
x3 = 27 
x = {tex}\sqrt[3]{27}{/tex}
x = 3 and x is a rational number

questions.question: {tex}\sqrt {45} {/tex} is ________ number.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt 45{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{(3 \times 3 \times 5)}{/tex}
= 3 {tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} which is an irrational number

questions.question: {tex}\sqrt {80} {/tex} is ________ number.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\sqrt 80{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt {( 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 5)}{/tex}
= 2 {tex} \times {/tex} 2 {tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} 
= 4{tex}\sqrt 5{/tex} which is an irrational number

questions.question: Find the decimal representation of {tex}\frac{8}{3}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}8\over 3{/tex} = 2.666666....
= {tex}2.\overline 6{/tex}

questions.question: Express {tex}\frac{7}{8}{/tex} in the decimal form.
questions.explanation: {tex}7\over 8{/tex} = 0.875

questions.question: Find: {tex}{\left( {\frac{{64}}{{25}}} \right)^{ - \frac{3}{2}}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}{\left( {\frac{{64}}{{25}}} \right)^{ - \frac{3}{2}}}{/tex}
​= {tex}{\left( {\frac{{25}}{{64}}} \right)^{ \frac{3}{2}}}{/tex}
= {tex}{\left( {\frac{{5}}{{8}}} \right)^{ 2 \times \frac{3}{2}}}{/tex}
= ({tex}5\over 8{/tex})3
= {tex}125\over 512{/tex}

questions.question: What can the maximum number of digits be in the repeating block of digits in the decimal expression of {tex}\frac{22}{{7}}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
{tex}22\over 7{/tex} = 3.142857142857....
= {tex}3.\overline{142857}{/tex}
So, here the maximum number of digits in the repeating block of digits in {tex}22\over 7{/tex} is 6 digits.

questions.question: Find the solution of  2x - 3 = 7
questions.explanation:
by transposing,the signs will be change
2x-3=7
2x=7+3
2x=10
x=10/2
x=5.
The correct option is 5

questions.question: Solve for x: 3x = 12
questions.explanation:
By transposing 3 to another side, it will be in the denominator
3x = 12
x = {tex}\frac{12}{3}{/tex} (Now divide 12 by 3)
x = 4

questions.question: Solve: 2x - 3 = x + 2
questions.explanation:
By transposing numbers and variable both, their sign will change
2x - 3 = x + 2
2x - x = 2 + 3
x = 5

questions.question: Solve for x: 5x + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{2}x{/tex} - 14
questions.explanation:
5x + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{2}x{/tex} - 14
By transposing the number and variable both
i.e. taking variables(x) on left hand side and numbers({tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex} and 14) on right hand side and changing the signs of transposing values
5x - {tex}\frac{3x}{2}{/tex} = -14 - {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
L.c.m of both sides
{tex}\frac{10x - 3x}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-28-7}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{7x}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-35}{2}{/tex}
By cancelling 2 both sides
7x = -35 (transpose 7 to other side in next step)
x = {tex}\frac{-35}{7}{/tex} (divide by 7)
x = -5.

questions.question: Solve: 5x - 2(2x - 7) = (3x - 1) + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
5x - 2(2x - 7) = (3x - 1) + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
5x - 4x + 14 = 3x - 1 + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
x + 14 = 3x - 1 + {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
3x - x = 14 + 1 - {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
2x = {tex}\frac{{28 + 2 - 7}}{2}{/tex}
2x = {tex}\frac{{30 - 7}}{2}{/tex}
2x = {tex}\frac{{23}}{2}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{{23}}{{2 \times 2}}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{{23}}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: An algebraic equation is an ________ involving variables.
questions.explanation:
For an equation to be valid it is necessary that both values of R.H.S and L.H.S must be equal. Hence an equation is equality.
L.H.S = R.H.S

questions.question: What should be added to twice the rational number {tex}\frac{{ - 7}}{3}{/tex} to get {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
Let x be the rational number to be added,
Now, According to question,
2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{7}{3}{/tex} + x = {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex} 
{tex}-\frac{14}{3}{/tex} + x = {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{14}{3}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{9 + 98}{21}{/tex} ... (taking L.C.M)
x = {tex}\frac{107}{21}{/tex}

questions.question: The difference between two whole numbers is 66. The ratio of the two numbers is 2 : 5. What are the two numbers?
questions.explanation:
Since ratio = 2:5 is given
So let the numbers be 2x and 5x. (Point to remember: if the ratio is given let the numbers to be the same numeral value with the variable x)
5x - 2x = 66 (the difference between numbers is 66)
or, 3x = 66
by transposing to another side
or, x = {tex}\frac{66}{3}{/tex}
or, x = 22
Numbers are 2x = 44
5x = 110
Two numbers are 44 and 110.

questions.question: The digits of a two-digit number differ by 3. If the digits are interchanged, and the resulting number is added to the original number, we get 143. What can be the original number?
questions.explanation:
Let us suppose, unit digit's number is x
Tens digit's number is x + 3
Then the number is 10(x + 3) + x. If the digits are interchanged then the number became 10x + (x + 3)
By condition,
10(x + 3) + x + 10x + (x + 3) = 143
or, 10x + 30 + x + 10x + x + 3 = 143
or, 22x + 33 = 143
or, 22x = 143 - 33
or, 22x = 110
or, x = {tex}\frac{{110}}{22}{/tex}
or, x = 5
Then the number be 10(5 + 3) + 5 = 85

questions.question: Solve 2y + 9 = 4.
questions.explanation:
2y + 9 = 4.
By transposing
2y = 4 - 9
2y = -5 (transposing 2 to other side)
y = -5 / 2

questions.question: Solve: 4y = 20
questions.explanation:
4y = 20
By transposing 
y = {tex}\frac{20}{4}{/tex}
y = 5

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 2x + 18
questions.explanation:
3x = 2x + 18
3x - 2x = 18 (Transposing 2x to L.H.S)
x = 18

questions.question: Solve: x = {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}(x + 10)
questions.explanation:
x = {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}(x + 10)
by transposing 5 to other side, we get
5x = 4(x + 10)
5x = 4x + 40 (opening the bracket and multiplying it by 4)
5x - 4x = 40 (transposing 4x to L.H.S.)
x = 40

questions.question: Which of the following is the product of 7/8 and -4/21?
In an equation, the values of the expressions on the LHS and RHS are ________.
questions.explanation: To balance the equation the value of L.H.S and R.H.S must be equal.

questions.question: The perimeter of a rectangle is 13cm and its width is {tex}2\frac 3 4{/tex}cm. Find its length.
questions.explanation:
perimeter of rectangle = 13cm
2(l + b) = 13 (where l = length of rectangle & b = breadth of rectangle)
or, l + b = {tex}\frac{13}{2}{/tex}
or, l + {tex}\frac{11}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{13}{2}{/tex} (b = {tex}\frac{11}{4}{/tex} given)
or, l = {tex}\frac{13}{2}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{11}{4}{/tex} (transposing {tex}\frac{11}{4}{/tex} to other side)
or, l = {tex}\frac{26 - 11}{4}{/tex} (taking L.C.M)
or, l = {tex}\frac{15}{4}{/tex}{tex} l= \frac {15}{4}{/tex}
or, l = {tex}3\frac{3}{4}{/tex} (converting to mixed fraction again)

questions.question: Aarushi has a total of Rs 590 as currency notes in the denominations of Rs 50, Rs 20 and Rs 10. The ratio of the number of Rs 50 notes and Rs 20 notes is 3:5. If she has a total of 25 notes, how many notes of each denomination she has?
questions.explanation:
Let the number of 50 rupees note = 3x and 20 rupee note = 5x ( since ratio is 3:5)
total no. of notes = 25
Rs.50 notes + Rs. 20 notes + Rs.10 notes= 25
3x + 5x + no. of 10 Rs. notes = 25
Number of 10 rupee note = 25 - (3x + 5x) = 25 - 8x
Amount from Rs.50 = 50 {tex}\times{/tex} 3x = 150x
Amount from Rs. 20 = 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 5x = 100x
Amount from Rs.10 = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} (25 - 8x) = 250 - 80x
According to question,total amount = 590
amount from Rs.50 + amount from RS 20 + amount from Rs. 10 = 590
or, 150x + 100x + 250 - 80x = 590
or, 170x + 250 = 590
or, 170x = 590 - 250
or, 170x = 340
or, x = {tex}\frac{340}{170}{/tex}
or, x = 2
\now the number of 50 rupees note = 3x = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 6 and 20 rupee note = 5x = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 10
Number of 10 rupee note = 25 - (3x + 5x) = 25 - 8x = 25 - 16 = 9

questions.question: Arjun is twice as old as Shriya. Five years ago his age was three times Shriya’s age. Find their present ages.
questions.explanation:
Let Shriya' s age = x
Arjun's age = 2x
Five years ago,
Shriya' s age = x - 5
Arjun's age = 2x - 5
According to question,
3(x - 5) = 2x - 5
or, 3x - 15 = 2x - 5
By trasposing both sides
or, 3x - 2x = -5 + 15 (terms with x on L.H.S. & terms without x on R.H.S.)
or, x = 10
So, Shriya's present age = x = 10 years
Arjun's present age = 2x = 20 years

questions.question: Solve: {tex}x - 2 = 7 {/tex}
Solve: 5a = 30
questions.explanation:
5a = 30
or,a = {tex}\frac{30}{5}{/tex}
or, a = 6

questions.question: Solve: 5t - 3 = 3t - 5
questions.explanation:
5t - 3 = 3t - 5
By transposing both sides
5t - 3t  = - 5 +3
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 2t = -2
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} t = {tex}\frac{-2}{2}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} t = -1

questions.question: If 3x - 4(64 - x) = 10, then the value of x is
questions.explanation:
3x - 256 + 4x = 10
7x = 10 + 256 = 266
x = {tex}\frac{266}{7}{/tex} = 38

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{3t - 2}}{4} - \frac{{2t + 3}}{3} = \frac{2}{3} - t{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{3t - 2}}{4} - \frac{{2t + 3}}{3} = \frac{2}{3} - t{/tex}
By L.C.M on both sides
or, {tex}\frac{9t - 6 - 8t - 12}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 - 3t}{3}{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{t - 18}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 - 3t}{3}{/tex}
By cross multiplication,
or, 3t - 54 = 24 - 36t
by transposing
or, -54 - 24 = -36t - 3t
or, -78 = -39t
by canceling (-)both sides, 
or, t = {tex}\frac{78}{39}{/tex}
or, t = 2

questions.question: The value of the expression on one side of the equality sign is ________ to the value of the expression on the other side.
questions.explanation: Both the values of  L.H.S and R.H.S must be equal.

questions.question: The present age of Sahil’s mother is three times the present age of Sahil. After 5 years their ages will add to 66 years. Find their present ages.
questions.explanation:
Let sahil's age = x
sahil's mother's age = {tex}3x{/tex}
after 5 years their age will be
sahil's age = {tex}x+5{/tex}
sahil's mother's age = {tex}3x + 5{/tex}
According to question,
{tex}x + 5 + 3x+ 5 = 66{/tex}
or, {tex} 4x+ 10 = 66{/tex}
or, {tex}4x = 66-10{/tex}
or, {tex}4x= 56{/tex}
by transpposing
or,{tex}x= 56/4{/tex}
or, {tex}x = 14{/tex}.

Now sahil's age = {tex}14{/tex}years
sahil's mothers age = {tex}42{/tex}years

questions.question: Sum of two numbers is 95. If one exceeds the other by 15. Find the numbers.
questions.explanation:
Let the number be = x
another nymber exceeded by 15 = x + 15
According to question,
x + x + 15 = 95
or, 2x + 15 = 95
By transposing 
or, 2x = 95 - 15
or, 2x = 80
or, x = 40
Now the numbers are x = 40, x + 15 = 40 + 15 = 55
Numbers are 40 and 55.

questions.question: Amina thinks of a number and subtracts {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex} from it. She multiplies the result by 8. The result now obtained is 3 times the same number she thought of. What is the number?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be = x
According to question,
8(x - {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}) = 3x
or, 8x - 20 = 3
or, 8x - 3x = 20
or, 5x = 20
or, x = 4

questions.question: Solve: y + 3 = 10
questions.explanation:
y + 3 = 10
by transposing
y = 10 - 3
y = 7

questions.question: Solve: 7x = 21
questions.explanation:
7x = 21
x = {tex}\frac{21}{7}{/tex}
x = 3

questions.question: The solution of the equation ax + b = 0 is
questions.explanation: {tex}x = \frac { - b } { a }{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{8x - 3}}{{3x}}{/tex} = 2
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{8x - 3}}{{3x}} = 2{/tex}
By crossmultiplication
8x - 3 = 2 {tex} \times {/tex} 3x
8x - 3 = 6x
By transposing
8x - 6x = 3
2x = 3
x = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: A linear equation may have for its ________ any rational number.
questions.explanation: The soution of a linear equation can be a rational number

questions.question: Bansi has 3 times as many two-rupee coins as he has five-rupee coins. If he has in all a sum of Rs 77, how many coins of each denomination does he have?
questions.explanation:
Let the no. of 5rupees coin is x
The no. of 2 rupees coin is 3x
According to question
5x +  2(3x) = 77
5x + 6x = 77
11x = 77
x ={tex}\frac{77}{11}{/tex}
x = 7
Now 5 rupee coin = 7 and 2 rupee coin = 21

questions.question: Two numbers are in the ratio 5:3. If they differ by 18, what are the numbers?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be = x
Ratio = 5 : 3
Numbers = 5x and 3x
According to question
5x - 3x = 18
2x = 18
by transposing
x = {tex}\frac{18}{2}{/tex}
x = 9
Now the numbers are = 5x = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 45 and 3x = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 27
45 and 27

questions.question: If 8x - 3 = 25 + 17x, then x is
questions.explanation:
8x - 3 = 25 + 17x
8x - 17x = 25 + 3
-9x = 28
x = {tex}\frac{28}{9}{/tex} ; a rational number

questions.question: Solve: 6 = z + 2
questions.explanation:
6 = z + 2
By transposing
6 - 2 = z
4 = z

questions.question: Solve: 8y = 32
questions.explanation:
8y = 32
by transposing
y = {tex}\frac{32}{8}{/tex}
y = 4

questions.question: Solve: 4z + 3 = 6 + 2z
questions.explanation:
4z + 3 = 6 + 2z
4z - 2z = 6 - 3
2z = 3
by transposing
z = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: The shifting of a number from one side of an equation to other is called
questions.explanation: In a linear equation, the shifting of a number from one side of an equation to other side is transposition.

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{z}{{z + 15}} = \frac{4}{9}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{z}{{z + 15}} = \frac{4}{9}{/tex}
BY crossmultiplication
9z = 4(z+15)
9z = 4z+60
9z - 4z = 60
5z = 60
by transposing
z = {tex}\frac{60}{5}{/tex}
z = 12

questions.question: The equations are linear, i.e., the highest power of the variable appearing in the equation is ________.
questions.explanation: Only linear equation has the greatest power one.

questions.question: The sum of three consecutive multiples of 11 is 363. Find these multiples.
questions.explanation:
Let the first multiple of 11 be = x
second multiple of 11 = x + 11
third multiple of 11 = x + 22
According to question,
x + x + 11 + x + 22 = 363
or, 3x + 33 = 363
or, 3x = 363 - 33
or, 3x = 330
By transposing
or, x = {tex}\frac{330}{3}{/tex}
or, x = 110
put the value of x = 110
The first multiple of 11 = x = 110
second multiple of 11 = 110 + 11 = 121
third multiple of 11 = 110 + 22 = 132

questions.question: If {tex}\frac { 5 x } { 3 } - 4 = \frac { 2 x } { 5 }{/tex}, then the numerical value of 2x - 7 is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac { 5 x } { 3 } - 4 = \frac { 2 x } { 5 }{/tex}
{tex}\frac { 5 x } { 3 } - \frac { 2 x } { 5 }{/tex} = 4
{tex}\frac{25x-6x}{15}{/tex} = 4
19x = 15 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
x = {tex}\frac{60}{19}{/tex}
hence,
2x - 7
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{60}{19}{/tex} - 7
= {tex}\frac{120}{19}{/tex} - 7
= {tex}\frac{120-133}{19}{/tex}
={tex}- \frac { 13 } { 19 }{/tex}

questions.question: The value of x for which the expressions 3x - 4 and 2x + 1 become equal is
questions.explanation:
When the value of x is 5 then both the expressions will be equal.
Proof
3x - 4 = 2x + 1
x = 5

questions.question: Solve: 2x -3 = 7
questions.explanation:
2x = 10

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{3} + \frac{5}{2} = - \frac{3}{2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{3} + \frac{5}{2} = - \frac{3}{2}{/tex}
By transposing the value
{tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-3}{2}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-8}{2}{/tex}
x = -4 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
x = -12

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{3} + \frac{5}{2} = - \frac{3}{2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{3} + \frac{5}{2} = - \frac{3}{2}{/tex}
By transposing the value
{tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-3}{2}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-8}{2}{/tex}
x = -4 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
x = -12

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{15}}{4}{/tex} - 7x = 9
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{15}}{4}{/tex} - 7x = 9
By transposing
-7x = 9 - {tex}\frac{15}{4}{/tex}
by L.C.M
-7x = 36 - {tex}\frac{15}{4}{/tex}
-7x = {tex}\frac{21}{4}{/tex}
-x = {tex}\frac{21}{28}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{-3}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: y - 5 = 3
Solve: z + 5 = 15
Solve: a - 6 = 12
Solve: 7 = x - 3
questions.explanation:
7 = x - 3
by transposing
7 + 3 = x
10 = x

questions.question: Solve: 2x - 5 = 3
questions.explanation:
2x - 5 = 3
2x = 3 + 5
2x = 8 
x = {tex}\frac{8}{2}{/tex}
x = 4

questions.question: Solve: 7x - 3 = 4
questions.explanation:
7x - 3 = 4
by transposing
7x = 4 + 3
7x = 7
by transposing
x = {tex}\frac{7}{7}{/tex}
x = 1

questions.question: Solve: 3x + 5 = 20
questions.explanation:
3x + 5 = 20
By transposing
3x = 20 - 5
3x = 15
By transposing
x = {tex}\frac{15}{3}{/tex}
x = 5

questions.question: Solve: 7 = 3x - 2
questions.explanation:
7 = 3x - 2
7 + 2 = 3x
9 = 3x
{tex}\frac{9}{3}{/tex} = x
3 = x

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{4} - \frac{3}{4} = \frac{7}{4}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{4} - \frac{3}{4} = \frac{7}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7}{4}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{10}{4}{/tex}
by cancelling 4 both sides
x = 10

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{y}{2} + \frac{3}{2} = \frac{7}{2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{y}{2} + \frac{3}{2} = \frac{7}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{y}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{y}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{2}{/tex}
by canceling 2 both sides
y = 4

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{3x}}{4} - \frac{x}{4} = \frac{2}{4}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{3x}}{4} - \frac{x}{4} = \frac{2}{4}{/tex}
by subtraction 
{tex}\frac{2x}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{4}{/tex}
by cancelling 4 both sides
2x = 2
by transposing
x = {tex}\frac{2}{2}{/tex}
x = 1

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 15
Solve: 4x = 24

divide both sieds by 4

divide both sieds by 7
{tex}\frac{7x}{7}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{35}{7}{/tex}
x = 5

questions.question: Solve: 8x = 4

2x = 1

questions.question: Solve: 9x = 108
questions.explanation:
9x = 108

12x = 132
by transposing

questions.question: Solve: 15x = 165
questions.explanation:
15x = 165
by transposing

questions.question: Solve: 16x = 96
questions.explanation:
16x = 96
by transposing

x = 6

questions.question: Solve: 17x = 153
questions.explanation:
17x = 153
by transposing

questions.question: Solve: 15x = 240
questions.explanation:
15x = 240
divide both sides by 15
{tex}\frac{15x}{15}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{240}{15}{/tex}
x = 16

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex} = 12
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex} = 12
x = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
x = 36

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{y}{5}{/tex} = 1
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{y}{5}{/tex} = 1
y = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
y = 5

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{2x}{3}{/tex} = 12
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{2x}{3}{/tex} = 12
2x = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
2x = 36
x = {tex}\frac{36}{2}{/tex}
x = 18

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{a}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{a}{4} = \frac{7}{2}{/tex}
by cross multiplication
a {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
by transposing
a = {tex}\frac{28}{2}{/tex}
a = 14

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{3x}}{5} = \frac{{12}}{{15}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{3x}}{5} = \frac{{12}}{{15}}{/tex}
by crossmultiplication
3x {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
45x = 60
x = {tex}\frac{60}{45}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{5b}}{6}{/tex} = 5
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{5b}}{6}{/tex} = 5
by transposing
5b = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
5b = 30
b = {tex}\frac{30}{5}{/tex}
b = 6

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{6x}}{7}{/tex} = 42
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{6x}}{7}{/tex} = 42
by transposing
6x =42 {tex}\times{/tex} 7
6x = 294
x = {tex}\frac{294}{6}{/tex}
x = 49

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{5y}}{2}{/tex} = 15
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{5y}}{2}{/tex} = 15
by transposing
5y = 15 {tex} \times {/tex} 2
5y = 30
y = {tex}\frac{30}{5}{/tex}
y = 6

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{4a}}{5}{/tex} = 16
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{4a}}{5}{/tex} = 16
by transposing
4a = 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
4a = 80
4 = {tex}\frac{80}{4}{/tex}
a = 20

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{m}{7} = \frac{2}{7}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{m}{7} = \frac{2}{7}{/tex}
Cancelling 7 from the both sides 
m = 2

questions.question: What should be added to twice the rational number {tex} - \frac{7}{3}{/tex} to get {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be = x
according to question
2  (-7/3) + x = 3/7
-14 /3 + x = 3/7
by transposing
x = 3/7 + 14/3
by L.C.M 
x = ( 9 + 98 ) / 21
x = 107 / 21

questions.question: The difference between two whole numbers is 66. The ratio of the two numbers is 2:5. What are the two numbers?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be = x
ratio 2:5
according to question
5x - 2x = 66
or, 3x = 66
by transposing
or, x = 22
Now put the value of x
first number = 5x = 110
second number = 2x = 44

questions.question: The perimeter of a rectangular swimming pool is 154m. Its length is 2m more than twice its breadth. What are the length and breadth of the pool?
questions.explanation:
Let the breadth be = x
now according to question length = 2x + 2
perimeter of rectangle = 154m
perimeter of rectangle = 2(l + b)
or, 154 = 2(x + 2x + 2)
or, {tex}\frac{154}{2}{/tex} = 3x + 2
by transposing
or, 77 - 2 = 3x
or, {tex}\frac{75}{3}{/tex} = x
or, x = 25
Now,  breadth = 25m
length = 2x + 2 = 50 + 2 = 52m

questions.question: The base of an isosceles triangle is {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}cm. The perimeter of the triangle is {tex}4\frac{2}{{15}}{/tex}cm. What is the length of either of the remaining equal sides?
questions.explanation:
The base of an isosceles triangle  =  {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}cm
let two equal sides are = x
perimeter of the triangle = {tex}4\frac{2}{{15}}{/tex}cm
the perimeter of the triangle = sum of all sides
{tex}\frac{62}{15}{/tex} = x + x + {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{62}{15}{/tex} = 2x + {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{62}{15}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(6x + 4)}{3}{/tex}
By crossmutliply, 
or, 186 = 90x + 60
or, 186 - 60 = 90x
or, 126 = 90x
or, {tex}\frac{126}{90}{/tex} = x
or, {tex}\frac{7}{5}{/tex} = x
{tex}1\frac{2}{5}cm{/tex} = x

questions.question: Sum of two numbers is 95. If one exceeds the other by 15, find the numbers.
questions.explanation:
Let two numbers be x and x + 15
Therefore, x + x + 15 = 95 
or, 2x = 80
or, x = 40
One number is 40.
Another number is 55.

questions.question: Two numbers in the ratio 5:3. If they differ by 18, what are the numbers?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be 5x and 3x.
Therefore, 5x - 3x = 18
or, 2x = 18
or, x = 9
So, the numbers are 45 and 27.

questions.question: Three consecutive integers add upto 51. What are these integers?
questions.explanation:
Let the interest are x, x + 1, x - 1.
Therefore, x + x + 1 + x - 1 = 51
or, 3x = 51
or, x = 17
One number is 17.
Other are 16 and 18.

questions.question: The sum of three consecutive multiples of 8 is 888. Find multiples.
questions.explanation:
let first number be = x
second multiple of 8 = x + 8
third multiple of 8 = x + 16
According to question
x + x + 8 + x + 16 = 888
or, 3x + 24 = 888
or, 3x = 888 - 24
or, 3x = 864
or, x = {tex}\frac{864}{3}{/tex}
or, {tex} x = 288{/tex}
now the first multiple of 8 = 288
second multiple of 8 = 296
third multiple of 8 = 304

questions.question: Three consecutive integers are such that when they are taken in increasing order and multiplied by 2, 3 and 4 respectively, they add up to 74. Find these numbers.
questions.explanation:
Let first integer = a
Therefore, second consecutive integer = a + 1
And, third consecutive integer = a + 2
Since, according to question, by taking in increasing number, first integer is multiplied by 2, second consecutive integer is multiplied by 3 and third consecutive integer is multiplied by 4. And sum of all the three integers = 74
Therefore,
(First integer x 2) + (second consecutive integer x 3) + (third consecutive integer x 4) = 74
or, 2a + 3a + 3 + 4a + 8 = 74
or, 9a = 74 - 11
or, 9a = 63
or, a = 7
By substituting the value of ‘a’ the rest two consecutive integers can be obtained.
Second consecutive integer = a + 1 = 7 + 1 = 8
Third consecutive integer = a + 2 = 7 + 2 = 9
Thus, three required consecutive integers are 7, 8 and 9

questions.question: The number of boys and girls in a class is in the ratio 7:5. The number of boys is 8 more than the number of girls. What is the total class strength?
questions.explanation:
let the number of boys and girls = x
ratio = 7 : 5
boys =7x
girls = 5x
According to question,
7x = 5x + 8
By transposing,
7x - 5x = 8
2x = 8
x = {tex}\frac{8}{2}{/tex}
x = 4
now the number of boys = 7x = 28
the number of girls = 5x = 20
total students = 28 + 20 = 48

questions.question: Fifteen years from now Ravi’s age will be four times his present age. What is Ravi’s present age?
questions.explanation:
Let Ravi's age = x
15 years from now his age will be = x + 15
According to question,
x + 15 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} x
or, x + 15 = 4x
or, 15 = 4x - x
or, 15 = 3x
or, {tex}\frac{15}{3}{/tex} = x
or, x = 5
Ravi's present age =  5 years

questions.question: Solve the equation: 2x - 3 = x + 2
questions.explanation:
2x - 3 = x + 2
By transposig both sides
2x - x = 2 + 3
x = 5

questions.question: Solve the equation: 3x = 2x + 18
Solve: {tex}{\text{5}}t-{\text{ 3 }} = {\text{ 3}}t-{\text{ 5}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}{\text{5}}t-{\text{ 3 }} = {\text{ 3}}t-{\text{ 5}}{/tex}
by transposing both sides
5t - 3t = - 5 + 3
2t = -2
t = -2/2
t = -1

questions.question: Solve: 5x + 9 = 5 + 3x
Solve the following equation: 4z + 3 = 6 + 2z
questions.explanation:
4z + 3 = 6 + 2z
by transposing both sides
or, 4z - 2z = 6 - 3
or, 2z = 3
or, z = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: 2x - 1 = 14 - x
questions.explanation:
2x - 1 = 14 - x
by transposing both sides
2x + x = 14 + 1
3x = 15
by transposing 3 to R.H.S
x = {tex}\frac{15}{3}{/tex}
x = 5

questions.question: Solve: 8x + 4 = 3(x - 1) + 7
questions.explanation:
8x + 4 = 3(x - 1) + 7
or, 8x + 4 = 3x - 3 + 7 (solve bracket first)
or, 8x + 4 = 3x + 4
By transposing both sides
or, 8x - 3x = 4 - 4
or, 5x = 0
or, x = 0

questions.question: The digits of a two-digit number differ by 3. If the digits are interchanged and the resulting number is added to the original number, we get 143. What can be the original number?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be x and x + 3.
The number is 10(x + 3) + x
= 11x + 30
If interchange the number, we get, 
10x + x + 3 = 11x + 3
Now, 11x + 30 + 11x + 3 = 143
or, 22x + 33 = 143
or, 22x = 110
or, x = 5
One digit is 5.
Another digit is 8.
So the number is 85.

questions.question: Arvind is twice as old as Shafali. Five years ago his age was three times Shafali’s age. Find their present ages.
questions.explanation:
Let Arvind's age be  = x
Shefali's age = 2x
Five years ago,
Arvind's age be  = x - 5
Shefali's age = 2x - 5
According to question,
2x - 5 = 3(x - 5)
or, 2x - 5 = 3x - 15
or, 2x - 3x = -15 + 5
or, -x = -10
by cancelling (-) from both sides,
or, x = 10
Now,
Arvind's age be = x = 10 years
Shefali's age = 2x = 20 years

questions.question: A positive number is 5 times another number. If 21 is added to both the numbers, then one of the new numbers becomes twice the other new number. What are the numbers?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be x. 
Then, five times the number is 5x.
Therefore, 2(x + 21) = (5x + 21)
or, 2x + 42 = 5x + 21
or, 3x = 21
or, x = 7
One number is 7.
Another is 35.

questions.question: Sum of the digits of a two-digit number is 9. When we interchange the digits, it is found that the resulting new number is greater than the original number by 27. What is the two-digit number?
questions.explanation:
Let the number be 10x + y.
Now, x + y = 9
or, x = 9 - y 
Also, if we interchanged the number then it is 10y + x. 
So, (10y + x) - (10x + y) = 27
or, 9y - 9x = 27
or, y - x = 3
or, y - (9 - y) = 3
or, 2y = 12
or, y = 6
Then x = 9 - 6 = 3
So, the number is = 36

questions.question: One of the two digits of a two-digit number is three times the other digit. If you interchange the digits of this two-digit number and add the resulting number to the original number, you get 88. What is the original number?
questions.explanation:
let the unit place be = 2
tens place = 3x
number = 30x + x = 31x
when interchange the digit 
number = 10x + 3x = 13x
Now according to question
31x + 13x = 88
or, 44x = 88
or, x = {tex}\frac{88}{44}{/tex}
or, x = 2
The number will be = 13x = 26

questions.question: Half of a herd of deer are grazing in the field and three-fourths of the remaining are playing nearby. The rest 9 are drinking water from the pond. Find the number of deer in the herd.
questions.explanation:
Let the number of deer in herd be = x
number of deer grazing in field = {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex}
number of deer left = x - {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex}
number of deer playing nearby = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} of {tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3x}{8}{/tex}
Number of deer drinking water from pond = 9
Now according to question, 
{tex}\frac{x}{2}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3x}{8}{/tex} + 9 = x
by L.C.M 
or, (4x + 3x + 72) = x
or, 7x + 72 = 8x
or, 72 = 8x - 7x
or, 72 = x
Number of deer = 72

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{3} + 1 = \frac{7}{{15}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{3} + 1 = \frac{7}{{15}}{/tex}
by transposing the value
or, {tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex} = {tex}(\frac{7}{15}){/tex} - 1
or, {tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7 - 15}{15}{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{x}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-8}{15}{/tex}
by transposing again, 
or, x = {tex}\frac{-24}{15}{/tex}
in lowest term 
or, x = {tex}\frac{-8}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{2x}}{3} + 1 = \frac{{7x}}{{15}} + 3{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{2x}}{3} + 1 = \frac{{7x}}{{15}} + 3{/tex}
by transposing 
or, {tex}\frac{2x}{3}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{7x}{15}{/tex} = 3 - 1
or, {tex}\frac{10x - 7x}{15}{/tex} = 2
or, 3x = 30
or, x = 10

questions.question: Solve: {tex}2y + \frac{5}{3} = \frac{{26}}{3} - y{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}2y + \frac{5}{3} = \frac{{26}}{3} - y{/tex}
or, 2y + y = {tex}\frac{26}{3}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{5}{3}{/tex}
or, 3y = {tex}\frac{21}{3}{/tex}
or, 3y = 7
or, y = {tex}\frac{7}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: 3m = 5m - {tex}\frac{8}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}3m = 5m - \frac{8}{5}{/tex}
or, 3m - 5m = {tex}\frac{-8}{5}{/tex}
or, -2m = {tex}\frac{-8}{5}{/tex}
Divide both sides by (-2), we get,
or, m = {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{x}{2} - \frac{1}{5} = \frac{x}{3} + \frac{1}{4}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{x}{2} - \frac{1}{5} = \frac{x}{3} + \frac{1}{4}{/tex}
By L.C.M
or, {tex}\frac{(5x - 2)}{10}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(4x + 3)}{12}{/tex}
by cross multiplication
or, 60x - 24 = 40x + 30
by transposing
or, 60x - 40x = 30 + 24
or, 20x = 54
or, x = {tex}\frac{54}{20}{/tex}
in lowest term
or, x = {tex}\frac{27}{10}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{n}{2} - \frac{{3n}}{4} + \frac{{5n}}{6}{/tex} = 21
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{n}{2} - \frac{{3n}}{4} + \frac{{5n}}{6}{/tex} = 21
by L.C.M of 2, 4 and 6 = 12
or, {tex}\frac{(6n - 9n + 10n)}{12}{/tex} = 21
or, {tex}\frac{7n}{12}{/tex} = 21
or, 7n = 252
or, n = {tex}\frac{252}{7}{/tex}
or, n = 36

questions.question: Solve: {tex}x + 7 - \frac{{8x}}{3} = \frac{{17}}{6} - \frac{{5x}}{2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}x + 7 - \frac{{8x}}{3} = \frac{{17}}{6} - \frac{{5x}}{2}{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{x - 8x}{3}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{5x}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{17}{6}{/tex} - 7
or, {tex}\frac{(6x - 16x + 15x)}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{17 - 42}{6}{/tex}
Eliminating denominator both sides and calculating, we get,
or, 5x = -25
or, x = -5

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{x - 5}}{3} = \frac{{x - 3}}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{x - 5}}{3} = \frac{{x - 3}}{5}{/tex}
by cross multiplication
or, 5(x - 5) = 3(x - 3)
or, 5x - 25 = 3x - 9
by transposing
or, 5x - 3x = -9 + 25
or, 2x = 16
or, x = {tex}\frac{16}{2}{/tex}
or, x = 8

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{3x - 2}}{4} - \frac{{2x + 3}}{3} = \frac{2}{3} - x{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{3x - 2}}{4} - \frac{{2x + 3}}{3} = \frac{2}{3} - x{/tex}
L.C.M on both sides
or, {tex}\frac{(9x - 6 - 8x - 12)}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(2 - 3x)}{3}{/tex}
or, {tex}\frac{(x - 8)}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(2 - 3x)}{3}{/tex}
by cross-multiply
or, 3x - 54 = 24 - 36x
or, -54 - 24 = -36x - 3x
or, -78 = -39x
or, {tex}\frac{-79}{-39}{/tex} = x
or, 2 = x

questions.question: Solve: {tex}a - \frac{{a - 1}}{2} = 1 - \frac{{a - 2}}{3}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}a - \frac{{a - 1}}{2} = 1 - \frac{{a - 2}}{3}{/tex}
By L.C.M on both sides
{tex}or,\frac{{2a- a+1}}{2} = \frac{{3 - a + 2}}{3}{/tex}
{tex}or,\frac{{a + 1}}{2}=\frac{{5 - a}}{3}{/tex}
By cross-multiply,
or, 3a + 3 = 10 - 2a
by transposing
or, 3a + 2a = 10 - 3
or, 5a = 7
or, a = {tex}\frac{7}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: 3(t - 3) = 5(2t + 1)
questions.explanation:
3(t - 3) = 5(2t + 1
3t - 9 = 10t + 5
10t - 3t = -9 - 5
7t = -14
t = 14 {tex}\div{/tex} 7
t = -2

questions.question: Solve: 15(y - 4) - 2(y - 9) + 5(y + 6) = 0
questions.explanation:
15(y - 4) -2(y - 9) + 5(y + 6) = 0
15y - 60 - 2y + 18 + 5y + 30 = 0
18y - 12 = 0
18y = 12
y = {tex}\frac{12}{18}{/tex}
y = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve: {tex}\frac{{3x + 4}}{{2 - 6x}} = \frac{{ - 2}}{5}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{3x + 4}}{{2 - 6x}} = \frac{{ - 2}}{5}{/tex}
by cross multiply
5(3x + 4) = -2(2 - 6x)
15x + 20 = -4 + 12x
15x - 12x = -4 - 20
3x = -24
x = -24 {tex}\div{/tex} 3
x = -8

questions.question: The denominator of a rational number is greater than its numerator by 8. If the numerator is increased by 17 and the denominator is decreased by 1, the number obtained is {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}. Find the rational number.
questions.explanation:
Let the number of numerator be = x
denominator = x + 8
According to the given condition
{tex}\frac{x + 17}{x + 8}{/tex} - 1 = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x + 17}{x + 7}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex}
by crossmultiply,we get,
2(x + 17) = 3(x + 7)
2x + 34 = 3x + 21
34 - 21 = 3x - 2x
x= 13
Numerator = x = 13
Denominator = x + 8 = 21
Fraction = {tex}\frac{13}{21}{/tex}

questions.question: How many diagonals does a convex quadrilateral have?
questions.explanation: The two diagonals of a convex quadrilateral are the line segments that connect opposite vertices.

questions.question: State the name of a regular polygon of 5 sides.
questions.explanation: A pentagon is a regular polygon with 5 sides and 5 vertices.

questions.question: Which of the following quadrilaterals has two pairs of adjacent sides equal and diagonals intersecting at right angles?
questions.explanation: kite

questions.question: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 45o.
questions.explanation:
The measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon of n-sides = {tex}\frac{360}{n}{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}\frac{360}{n}{/tex} = 45
so, 45n = 360
n = 8
the regular polygon has 8 sides

questions.question: Given a parallelogram ABCD. AD = ......
questions.explanation: Opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal

questions.question: The paper is a model for a ________.

options.html: Plane surface

questions.explanation: A paper is a model of a plane surface with no 3-d (three - dimensional) shape.

questions.question: The sum of the measures of the three angles of a triangle is ________.
questions.explanation:
Construction: Draw AC || line l
Since {tex}\angle{/tex}a, {tex}\angle{/tex}b, and {tex}\angle{/tex}c make a straight line l,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}a + {tex}\angle{/tex}b + {tex}\angle{/tex}c = 180o (Angles on one side of a straight line = 180o)
{tex}\because{/tex} AC || line l
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Alternate interior angles are equal, {tex}\angle{/tex}a = {tex}\angle{/tex}x and {tex}\angle{/tex}b = {tex}\angle{/tex}y
Therefore, {tex}\angle{/tex}x + {tex}\angle{/tex}y + {tex}\angle{/tex}c = 180o

questions.question: Which of the following quadrilaterals has a pair of opposite sides parallel?
questions.explanation: parallelogram

questions.question: The ________ of a rhombus are perpendicular bisectors of one another.
questions.explanation: Diagonals bisect each other at a right angle in a rhombus.

questions.question: In Given parallelogram, find the value of the unknown x.
questions.explanation:
∵ {tex}AB{\rm{ }}||{\rm{ }}CD{/tex}

∴ {tex}x{\rm{ }} + {\rm{ }}{50^o} = {\rm{ }}{180^o}{/tex} ( the sum of adjacent angle of a parallelogram is 180o).

{tex} \Rightarrow x = 180 - 50 = {130^o}{/tex}

questions.question: If ABCD is a parallelogram, find the value of the unknown y.
questions.explanation:
∵ AC || BD and AB is the transversal intersecting them,
∴ y + 50°= 180°( Interior angles on the same side of transversal are supplementary )
y = 130°

questions.question: If Given fiqure is a parallelogram, find the value of the unknown z.
questions.explanation:
∵ {tex}AB{\rm{ }}||{\rm{ }}CD{/tex}
∴ {tex}x{\rm{ }} + {\rm{ }}{50^o} = {\rm{ }}{180^o}{/tex} ( the sum of adjacent angle of a parallelogram is 180o ).
{tex} \Rightarrow x = 180 - 50 = {130^o}{/tex}
Now ∵ {tex}AC{\rm{ }}||{\rm{ }}BD{/tex} and transversal CE intersects them
∴ {tex}X=Z{/tex} (corresponding angles)
{tex} \Rightarrow Z = {130^o}{/tex}

questions.question: RICE is a rhombus. Find x.
questions.explanation:
Diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other.
∴ EO = OI
⇒ EO (x) = 5

questions.question: RICE is a rhombus. Find y.
questions.explanation:
Diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other.
∴ CO = OR = !2
⇒ OR (y) = 12

questions.question: RICE is a rhombus. Find z.
questions.explanation:
All sides of a rhombus are equal. 
∴ z = ER = 13

questions.question: How many diagonals does a regular hexagon have?
questions.explanation:
A diagonal is a line segment joining two adjacent vertices of a polygon. A regular hexagon has six sides and six vertices. One vertex has three diagonals, so a hexagon would have three diagonals times’ six vertices or 18 diagonals. Divide this number by 2 to account for duplicate diagonals between vertices. The formula for the number of vertices in a polygon is: 
{tex}x = {n . (n - 3) \over 2} ={/tex}{tex}{6. ( 6 - 3) \over 2}{/tex}= 9

questions.question: State the name of a regular polygon of 6 sides.
questions.explanation: A hexagon is a six-sided polygon or 6-gon. The total of the internal angles of any hexagon is 720o.

questions.question: Find the angle measure x in figure.
questions.explanation:
Interior Angle = 180o - 90o = 90o (linear pair)

x + 70o + 60o + 90o = 360o (Angle sum property of a quadrilateral)

x + 220o = 360o

x = 360o - 220o

x = 140o

questions.question: Find the measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon of 9 sides.
questions.explanation:
Each exterior angle of a regular polygon of n sides n = {tex}{360^o \over n}{/tex};  where n is the number of sides
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} each exterior angle = {tex}x = {360^o \over 9}{/tex} = 40o

questions.question: Given a parallelogram ABCD. {tex}\angle {/tex}DCB = ....
questions.explanation:
Opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal.

∴ ​​​​​​{tex}\angle {/tex}DAB = {tex}\angle {/tex}DCB

questions.question: A simple closed curve made up of only line segments is called a ________.
questions.explanation: A polygon is any 2-dimensional shape formed with straight lines. Triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, and hexagons are all examples of polygons.

questions.question: A ________ is both equiangular and equilateral.
questions.explanation:
A regular polygon is a plane shape (two-dimensional) whose all the sides are equal and all the angles are equal.
So we can say a regular polygon is both equilateral as well as equiangular
Equiangular - all angles are equal.
Equilateral - all sides are the same length.
A square is equilateral as well as equiangular.

questions.question: A ________ is a quadrilateral whose opposite sides are parallel.
questions.explanation:
A parallelogram has two parallel pairs of opposite sides. 
A rectangle has two pairs of opposite sides parallel, and four right angles. It is also a parallelogram since it has two pairs of parallel sides. 
A square has two pairs of parallel sides, four right angles, and all four sides are equal. It is also a rectangle and a parallelogram. 
A rhombus is defined as a parallelogram with four equal sides.

questions.question: A ________ has all the properties of a parallelogram and also that of a kite.
questions.explanation:
A parallelogram has opposite sides parallel and equal in length. Also, opposite angles are equal
Note: Squares, Rectangles, and Rhombuses are all Parallelograms
In a rhombus, all sides are equal and diagonals bisect each other
Note: A kite can become a rhombus.
In the special case where all 4 sides are the same length, the kite satisfies the definition of a rhombus. A rhombus, in turn, can become a square, if its interior angles are 90o.

questions.question: Which of the following quadrilaterals is a regular quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: square

questions.question: Find the value of the unknown y in given parallelogram.
questions.explanation: y = 100° (opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal)

questions.question: Find the value of the unknown z in give parallelogram.
questions.explanation:
∵ AD || BC
∴ x = 180° - 100° = 80° (sum of adjacent angles of a parallelogram = 180°)
x = 80°
y = 100° (opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal )
z = x = 80° (opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal)

questions.question: RENT is a rectangle. Its diagonals meet at O. Find x, if OR = 2x + 4 and OT = 3x + 1.
questions.explanation:
OR = OT (diagonals of a rectangle are equal in length and bisect each other)
∴ 2x + 4 = 3x + 1
4 - 1 = 3x - 2x
3 = x

questions.question: How many diagonals does a triangle have?
questions.explanation: A triangle is made up of three lines and three vertex points. Adjacent vertices are those which are not formed from the same line. All of the vertices in a triangle are adjacent to one another, so, therefore, a triangle is not formed from any diagonal line segments. The triangle is the only polygon without diagonals. A quadrilateral, for example, is made up of four lines and four vertex points. It is possible to draw two diagonals, from one non-adjacent vertex to the other, across the centre of the quadrilateral.

questions.question: Find the angle measure x in the figure :
questions.explanation:
It is given that all the sides of the given pentagon are equal
so, x + x + x + x + x = 540o (angle sum property of pentagon)
5x = 5400
{tex}x = { 540^o\over 5}{/tex}
x = 108o

questions.question: Find the measure of each exterior angle of a regular polygon of 15 sides.
questions.explanation:
The measure of each exterior angle = {tex}{360^o \over number of sides}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{360^ \circ}{15}{/tex}
x = 24o

questions.question: Given a parallelogram ABCD. OC = ......
questions.explanation:
Diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.

Therefore, OC = OA

questions.question: A ________ is a line segment connecting two non-consecutive vertices of a polygon.
questions.explanation: A diagonal is a straight line joining two opposite corners of a square, rectangle, or another straight-sided shape.

questions.question:
The sum of the measures of the four angles of a quadrilateral is___________.
Quadrilateral has 4 sides so, n = number of sides = 4
Sum of the measures of 4 angles
= (n - 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 180o = (4  -2){tex}\times{/tex} 180 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 180=360o
questions.explanation:
Consider a quadrilateral.{tex}PQRS{/tex}
In a triangle,{tex}PQS{/tex} we have,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle P{\rm{ }} + \angle PQS{\rm{ }} + \angle PSQ{\rm{ }} = {\rm{ }}180 ^ {\circ}{/tex} ... (1) [Using Angle sum property of Triangle]
Similarly, in triangle QRS, we can have a sum of angles like 180o. [Using Angle sum property of Triangle]
Therefore, we can say that a quadrilateral contains two triangles and hence the sum of all the angles of a quadrilateral will be 360o (180+180).

questions.question: The opposite angles of a parallelogram are of _________ measure.
questions.explanation:
AB  {tex}\parallel{/tex} CD and AC is the transversal
Hence {tex}∠1 = ∠4{/tex}  ....(i)
ΔABC ≅ ΔADC (SSS Congruence rule)
Hence  {tex}∠3 = ∠4 {/tex} ....(ii) and {tex}∠1 = ∠2 {/tex} .....(iii)
Consider, {tex}∠A = ∠3 + ∠4{/tex}
{tex}= ∠4 + ∠4{/tex}  [From (ii)]
{tex}= 2∠4{/tex}
{tex}= 2∠1 = ∠1 + ∠2 = ∠C{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}∠A = ∠C{/tex}
Similarly, we can prove {tex}∠B = ∠D{/tex}

questions.question: A square is a ________ with right angles.
questions.explanation: A parallelogram has its opposite sides parallel to each other. Square is a type of parallelogram because opposite sides in a square are parallel and every angle of a square are right angles.

questions.question: Find the value of the unknown x.
questions.explanation: x = 90° (vertically opposite angles are equal).

questions.question: Find the value of the unknown y.
questions.explanation:
{tex}∠AOB = 90{/tex}o

{tex}x = 90{/tex}o (vertically opposite angles are equal)

{tex}x + y + 30{/tex}o {tex}= 180{/tex}o (angle sum property of triangle)

{tex}y + 90{/tex}o {tex}+ 30{/tex}o {tex}= 180{/tex}o

{tex}y = 60{/tex}o

questions.question: Find the value of the {tex}\measuredangle z{/tex} where x = 90°
questions.explanation:
{tex}x = 90{/tex}{tex}^\circ {/tex} (vertical opposite angles)
{tex}x + y + 30 = 180 ^\circ {/tex} (angle sum property of a triangle)
{tex}⇒90 + y + 30 = 180 ^\circ {/tex}
{tex}⇒ y = 180 – 120 = 60 ^\circ {/tex}
also, y = z = 60°

questions.question: Which of the quadrilaterals has all angles as right angles, opposite sides equal and diagonals bisect-each other?
questions.explanation: rectangle

questions.question: Which of the following is true for the adjacent angles of a parallelogram?

options.html: they are equal to each other
they are not equal to each other

questions.explanation: they are supplementary angles

questions.question: Find the angle measure x in the figure.
questions.explanation:
Interior angle A = 180o - exterior angle A = 180 - 70 = 110o 
Interior angle B = 180o - exterior angle B = 180 - 60 = 120o
x + x + 120 + 110 + 30 = 540 (sum of all angles of a pentagon is 540o)
2x + 260 = 540
2x = 540 - 260
{tex}x = {280^o \over 2}{/tex}
x = 140o

questions.question: How many sides does a regular polygon have if the measure of an exterior angle is 24o?
questions.explanation: number of sides = {tex}\frac{360^ \circ}{24^ \circ}{/tex} = 15

questions.question: Given a parallelogram ABCD. {tex}\angle {/tex}DAB + {tex}\angle {/tex}CDA = ......
questions.explanation:
The sum of the adjacent angle of a parallelogram is {tex}180{/tex}o. 
Let ABCD be a parallelogram
Then, {tex}AD ∥ BC{/tex} and AB is a transversal.
Therefore, {tex}A + B = 180{/tex}° [Interior angles on the same side of a transversal are supplementary.]
Similarly, {tex}∠B + ∠C = 180{/tex}°, {tex}∠C + ∠D = 180{/tex}° and {tex}∠D + ∠A = 180{/tex}°.
Therefore, the sum of any two adjacent angles of a parallelogram is 180°.
Hence, any two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are supplementary.

questions.question: What is the sum of all interior angles of a regular pentagon?
questions.explanation:
(2n - 4) {tex}\times{/tex} 90 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 - 4) {tex}\times{/tex} 90
= 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 90 = 540o
where n is  the number of sides.
