questions.question: Simplify: (4m + 5n)2 + (5m + 4n)2
questions.explanation:
(4m + 5n)2 + (5m + 4n)2
{(4m)2 + (5n2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4m {tex}\times{/tex} 5n)} + {(5m2) + (4n2) + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5m {tex}\times{/tex} 4n} By using identity (a + b)2 = a2 + b2 + 2ab. 
16m2 + 25n2 + 40mn + 25m2 + 16n2 +40mn
41m2 + 80mn + 41n2

questions.question: (a - b) (a + b) + (b - c) (b + c) + (c - a) (c + a) = ?
questions.explanation:
(a-b)(a+b) + (b-c)(b+c) +(c-a)(c+a)
= a2 - b2  + b2 - c2  +  c2  - a2 (using identity (a - b) (a + b) = a2 - b2)
= 0

questions.question: Using identity (x - a)(x + a) = x2 - a2 find 62 - 52
questions.explanation:
62 - 52
= (6 + 5) (6 - 5) (Using identity (x  + a) (x - a) = x2 - a2)
= 11 {tex}\times{/tex} 1
= 11

questions.question: Find the product of (7x - 4y) and (3x - 7y)
questions.explanation:
(7x - 4y) (3x - 7y)
= 7x (3x - 7y) - 4y (3x - 7y)
= 7x {tex}\times{/tex} 3x - 7x {tex}\times{/tex} 7y - 4y {tex}\times{/tex} 3x + 4y {tex}\times{/tex} 7y
= 21x2 - 49xy  -12xy + 28y2
= 21x2 - 61xy + 28y2

questions.question: Using suitable identity find (a + 3)(a + 2).
questions.explanation:
(a + 3) (a + 2)
= a2 + (3 + 2) x a  + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 (using identity (x + a) (x + b) = x2 + (a + b)x + ab
= a2 + 5a + 6

questions.question: Using identity (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2 find the value of 1032.
questions.explanation:
1032 
= (100 + 3)2
= (100)2  + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 {tex}\times{/tex} 3  + (3)2 (Using identity (a + b) = a2 + 2ab + b2)
= 10000 + 600 + 9
= 10609

questions.question: Using identity (a - b)2 = a2 - 2ab + b2 find the value of 982.
questions.explanation:
982
= (100 - 2)2
= 1002 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 22 (using identity a2 - b2 = a2 - 2ab + b2 )
= 10000 - 400 + 4
= 9600 + 4
= 9604

questions.question: Using a suitable identity find (2x + 3)2.
questions.explanation:
(2x + 3 )2
= (2x )2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2x {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + (3)2 (using identity (a + b)2 = a2 + b2 + 2ab)
= 4x2 + 12x + 9

questions.question: Subtract 7x - 3x2 from 4x + 8x2
questions.explanation:
(4x + 8x2) - (7x - 3x2)
open brackets we get,
4x + 8x2 - 7x + 3x2
8x2 + 3x2 + 4x - 7x
11x2 - 3x

questions.question: Using a suitable identity find (7x - 3y)2
questions.explanation:
Using identity
(a - b)2 = a2 + b2 - 2ab
(7x - 3y)2 = (7x)2 + (3y)2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7x {tex}\times{/tex} 3y
(7x - 3y)2 = 49x2 + 9xy2 - 42xy
(7x - 3y)2 = 49x2 - 42xy + 9y2

questions.question: Add: 4x2 + 2xy - 4 and  7x2 - 3xy + 4
questions.explanation:
(4x2 + 2xy - 4) + (7x2 - 3xy + 4)
opening brackets we get,
4x2 + 2xy - 4 + 7x2 - 3xy + 4
4x2 + 7x2 + 2xy - 3xy - 4 + 4
11x2  - xy + 0
11x2 - xy

questions.question: Find the product of 4x, 7x2, -2x
questions.explanation:
product of 4x, 7x2, -2x = 4x {tex}\times{/tex} 7x2 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2x)
Multiply the constants and  variables we get,
4 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2) {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} x2 {tex}\times{/tex} x
(-56) {tex}\times{/tex} x4
-56x4

questions.question: Find the product of (x2 - y2)(2x+ y)
questions.explanation:
(x2 - y2)(2x + y)
opening brackets we get,
x2(2x + y) - y2(2x + y)
(x2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2x + x2 {tex}\times{/tex} y) - (y2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2x + y2 {tex}\times{/tex} y)
(2x3 + x2y) - (2xy2 + y3)
2x3 + x2y - 2xy2 - y3
2x3 - y3 + x2y - 2xy2

questions.question: Simplify: (xy + yz)2 - (xy - yz)2
questions.explanation:
(xy + yz)2 - (xy - yz)2
Let a = xy + yz) and b = (xy - yz)  
Use identity a2 - b2 =  (a + b) (a - b)  
(xy + yz)2 - (xy - yz)2 = [(xy + yz) + (xy - yz)][(xy + yz) - (xy - yz)]
opening brackets we get,
= (xy + yz + xy - yz)(xy + yz - xy + yz)
= (xy + xy + yz - yz)(xy - xy + yz + yz)
=(2xy + 0)(0 + 2yz)
=(2xy)(2yz)
= 2xy {tex}\times{/tex} 2yz
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex}y {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} z
= 4xy2z

questions.question: Using identity find the product of {tex}\left( {\frac{a}{2} + \frac{{3b}}{4}} \right)\left( {\frac{a}{2} + \frac{{3b}}{4}} \right){/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left( {\frac{a}{2} + \frac{{3b}}{4}} \right)\left( {\frac{a}{2} + \frac{{3b}}{4}} \right){/tex}={tex}{\left( {{a \over 2} + {{3b} \over 4}} \right)^{\rm{2}}}{/tex}
Use identity (a + b)2 = a2 + b2 + 2ab
{tex}{\left( {\frac{a}{2} + \frac{{3b}}{2}} \right)^2} = {\left( {\frac{a}{2}} \right)^2} + {\left( {\frac{{3b}}{7}} \right)^2} + 2\left( {\frac{a}{2}} \right)\left( {\frac{{3b}}{4}} \right){/tex}
{tex}{\left( {\frac{a}{2} + \frac{{3b}}{4}} \right)^2} = \frac{{{a^2}}}{4} + \frac{{9{b^2}}}{{16}} + \frac{{3ab}}{4}{/tex}
{tex}{\left( {\frac{a}{2} + \frac{{3b}}{4}} \right)^2} = \frac{{{a^2}}}{4} + \frac{{3ab}}{4} + \frac{{9{b^2}}}{{16}}{/tex}

tests.name: Algebraic Expressions Test 05
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 8 Algebraic Expressions and Identities CBSE Online Test 05 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Multiply: (a2 + 2c2)(3a - 3c)
questions.explanation:
(a2 + 2c2)(3a - 3c)
solving brackets we get,
a2(3a - 3c) + 2c2(3a - 3c)
3a3 - 3a2c + 6ac2 - 6c3

questions.question: Simplify: (x + y) (2x - 3y + z) - (2x - 3y)z
questions.explanation:
(x + y) (2x - 3y + z) - (2x - 3y)z
Solving brackets we get,
x(2x - 3y + z) + y(2x - 3y + z) - (2xz - 3yz)
Opening brackets we get,
x(2x - 3y + z) + y(2x - 3y + z) - (2xz - 3yz)
Opening brackets we get,
2x2 - 3xy + xz + 2xy - 3y2 + yz - 2xz + 3yz
Solving like terms and unlike terms we get,
2x2 - 3y2 - 3xy + 2xy + yz + 3yz + xz - 2xz
2x2 - 3y2 - xy + 4yz - xz

questions.question: Subtract: 3x (x - 4y + 5z) from 4x (2x - 3y + 10z)
questions.explanation:
[4x (2x – 3y + 10z)] - [3x (x – 4y + 5z)]
opening big brackets we get,
(8x2 - 12xy + 40xz) - (3x2 - 12xy + 15xz)
open small brackets we get, 
(8x2 - 12xy + 40xz) - 3x2 + 12xy - 15xz
8x2 - 3x2 - 12xy + 12xy + 40xz - 15xz
5x2 - 0 + 25xz
= 5x2 + 25xz

questions.question: Simplify: (x2 - y2)2
questions.explanation:
Use identity (a - b)2 = a2 - 2ab+ b2
(x2 - y2)2 =(x2)2 - 2(x2)(y2) + (y2)2
(x2 - y2)2 = x4 - 2x2y2 + y4

questions.question: Using a suitable identity find the product of {tex}\left( {\frac{2}{3}x - 5} \right)\left( {\frac{2}{3}x + 5} \right){/tex}
questions.explanation:
Use identity (a - b) (a + b) = a2 - b2
{tex}\left( {\frac{2}{3}x - 5} \right)\left( {\frac{2}{3}x + 5} \right) = {\left( {\frac{2}{3}x} \right)^2} - {(5)^2}{/tex}
{tex}\left( {\frac{2}{3}x - 5} \right)\left( {\frac{2}{3}x + 5} \right) = \frac{4}{9}{x^2} - 25{/tex}

questions.question: Simplify 3a (4a - 5) + 3
questions.explanation:
3a(4a – 5) + 3
open brackets we get,
(3a {tex}\times{/tex} 4a) - (3a {tex}\times{/tex} 5) + 3
(3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} a {tex}\times{/tex} a) - (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a) + 3
12a2 - 15a + 3

questions.question: Using identity a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b), find (102)2 - (0.98)2
questions.explanation:
using identity a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)
Here a = 1.02 and b = 0.98
(1.02)2 - (0.98)2 = (1.02 + 0.98)(1.02 - 0.98)
(1.02)2 - (0.98)2 = (2.00) (0.04)
(1.02)2 - (0.98)2 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.04 = 0.08

questions.question: Using (x + a)(x + b) = x2 + (a + b)x + ab find 105 {tex}\times{/tex} 107
questions.explanation:
using identity (x + a)(x + b) = x2 + (a + b)x + ab
105 {tex}\times{/tex} 107
Here x = 100, a = 5 and b = 7
(100 + 5)(100 + 7)
(100)2 + (5 + 7) (100) + (5) (7)
10000 + 1200 + 35
11235

questions.question: Using a suitable identity find the value of (7.2)3
questions.explanation:
Use identity (a - b)2 = a2 + b2 - 2ab
here a = 10.0 and b = 2.8
(10 - 2.8)2 = (10)2 + (2.8)2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.8
(10 - 2.8)2 = 100 + 7.84 - 56
(10 - 2.8)2 = 107.84 - 56
(10 - 2.8)2 = 51.84

questions.question: Using a suitable identity evaluate 297 {tex}\times{/tex} 303
questions.explanation:
Use identity (a - b) (a + b) = a2 - b2  Here a = 300 and b = 3
(300 - 3) (300 + 3) = (300)2 - (3)2
(297) (303) = 90000 - 9
297 {tex}\times{/tex} 303 = 89991

questions.question: Using a suitable identity find the value of (4.7)2
questions.explanation:
Use identity (a - b)2 = a2 + b2 - 2ab
Here a= 10.0 and b = 5.3
(10 - 5.3)2 = (10)2 + (5.3)3 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 5.3
(10 - 5.3)2 = 100 + 28.09 - 106
(10 - 5.3)2 = 128.09 - 106
(10 - 5.3)2 = 22.09

questions.question: Simplify: (xy + yz)2 - 2x2y2z.
questions.explanation:
Use identity (a + b)2 = a2 + b2 + 2ab
(xy + yz)2 - 2x2y2z
|(xy)2 + (yz)2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex}xy {tex}\times{/tex} yz| - 2x2y2z
x2y2 + y2z2 + 2xy2z - 2x2y2z
x2y2 + 2xy2z + y2z2 - 2x2y2z

questions.question: Find the value of x2y2 + 2xy2z + y2z2 - 2x2y2z, when x = -1, y = 1 and z = 2
questions.explanation:
x2y2 + 2xy2z + y2z2 - 2x2y2z
x = -1 , y = 1 , z = 2
put value of x , y and z  we get,
[(-1)2 {tex}\times{/tex} (12)] + [2 {tex}\times{/tex} (-1) {tex}\times{/tex} (1)2 {tex}\times{/tex} (2)] + [(1)2 {tex}\times{/tex} (2)2] - [2 {tex}\times{/tex} (-1)2 {tex}\times{/tex} (1)2 {tex}\times{/tex} (2)]
(1 {tex}\times{/tex} 1) + (2 {tex}\times{/tex} -1 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) + (1 {tex}\times{/tex} 4) - (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 2)
1 - 4 + 4 - 4
1 + 4 - 4 - 4 = 5 - 8 = -3

questions.question: Which of the following is like term as 3a2b2?
questions.explanation:
like terms are the terms which contain same variables and same power.
here terms are 3a2b2, 13a2b2
variables are same i.e a2b2
Hence 13a2b2 is like term as 3a2b2

questions.question: Which of the following is like term as 15abc?
questions.explanation:
like terms are the terms whoch contain same variables and same power.
here the terms are 15abc, 8abc
variables are same i.e abc
hence 8abc is like term as 15abc.

questions.question: Which of the following is like term as 8x2yz?

variables are same i.e x2yz

questions.question: Which of the following is like term as 6xy?
questions.explanation:
like terms are the terms which contain same variables and same power.
here terms are 6xy, 7xy
variables rae same i.e xy 
7xy is like term as 6xy.

questions.question: Which of the following is like term as 15mn?

like terms are the terms that contain same variables and same power.
Here terms are 15mn , 3mn
variables are same i.e  mn 
so, 3mn is like term as 15mn.

questions.question: Which of the following is like term as 14m2n2?
questions.explanation:
like terms are the terms that contain the same variables and the same power.
Here terms are 14m2n2, 15m2n2
variables and power are same i.e m2n2
so {tex}{\text{15}}{{\text{m}}^{\text{2}}}{{\text{n}}^{\text{2}}}{/tex} is like term as 14m2n2

questions.question: Which of the following is like term as 21xyz2?
questions.explanation:
Like terms are terms that contain the same variables and the same power

variables are same i.e xyz2 so 7xyz2 is like term as 21xyz2

questions.question:
Which of the following is a monomial?

  1. 4a
  2. 5x + 7
  3. 3x + y
  4. 7x + 5y + 5
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression which consists of one non-zero term only is called a monomial.
4a is a monomial as it contain one term only i.e 4a.

questions.question:
Which of the following is a monomial?

  1. 7x
  2. 5x + 9
  3. 4x + 3y
  4. 2x + 5y + 7
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression which consists of one non-zero term only is called a monomial.
7x is a monomial as it contain one term only i.e 7x.

questions.question:
Which of the following is a monomial?

  1. 7y
  2. 7x + 3
  3. 4x + 5y
  4. ​​​​​​​5x + 3y + 7
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression that consists of one non-zero term only is called a monomial.
7y is a monomial as it contains one term only i.e 7y.

questions.question:
Which of the following is a monomial?

  1. 7z
  2. 2x + 7y
  3. 4x + y2
  4. 4x + 3y + 5
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression that consists of one non-zero term only is called a monomial.
7z is a binomial as it contains one term only i.e 7z.

questions.question: Which of the given is a monomial?
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression that consists of one non-zero term only is called a monomial.
8m is a monomial as it contains one term only i.e 8m.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a binomial?
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression which consists of two non-zero terms is called a binomial.
3a – 2b is a binomial as it contains two terms i.e 3a, -2b.

questions.question: Which of the following is a binomial?
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression which consists of two non-zero terms is called a binomial.
4x – 5y is a binomial as it contains two terms i.e 4x , -5y

questions.question: Which of the given is a binomial?
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression of two non-zero terms only is called a binomial.
3y - a is a binomial as it contains two terms i.e 3y, -a.

questions.question: Which of the following is a trinomial?
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression of three non-zero terms only is called a trinomial.
3a – 2b + 5 is a trinomial as it contains three terms i.e 3a , -2b , 5

questions.question: Which of the given is a trinomial?
questions.explanation:
An algebraic expression of three non-zero terms only is called a trinomial.
6x - 5y - 3 is a trinomial as it contains three terms 6x, -5y, -3.

questions.question: Add: 7xy + 5yz - 3zx, 4yz + 9zx - 4y , -3xz + 5x - 2xy.
questions.explanation:
(7xy + 5yz - 3zx) + (4yz + 9zx - 4y) + (-3xz + 5x - 2xy)
opening brackets we get, 7xy + 5yz - 3zx + 4yz + 9zx - 4y - 3xz + 5x - 2xy
adding like terms and unlike terms
7xy - 2xy + 5yz + 4yz - 3zx - 3xz + 9zx + 5x - 4y
5xy + 9yz + 3xz + 5x - 4y

questions.question: Subtract 5x2 - 4y2 + 6y - 3 from 7x2 - 4xy + 8y2 + 5x - 3y.
questions.explanation:
(7x2 - 4xy + 8y2 + 5x - 3y) - (5x2 - 4y2 + 6y – 3)
opening brackets we get,
7x2 - 4xy + 8y2 + 5x - 3y - 5x2 + 4y2 - 6y + 3
solving like terms we get,
7x2 - 4xy + 8y2 + 5x - 3y - 5x2 + 4y2 - 6y + 3
7x2 - 5x2 + 8y + 4y2 + 5x - 3y - 6y - 4xy + 3
2x2 + 12y2 + 5x - 9y - 4xy + 3
2x2 - 4xy + 12y2 + 5x - 9y + 3

questions.question: Add: ab - bc, bc - ca, ca - ab
questions.explanation:
(ab - bc) + (bc - ca) + (ca - ab)
opening brackets we get,
ab - bc + bc - ca + ca - ab
solving like terms we get,
ab - ab - bc + bc - ca + ca
0 + 0 + 0 = 0

questions.question: Add: a - b + ab, b - c + bc, c - a + ac
questions.explanation:
(a - b + ab) + (b - c + bc) + (c - a + ac)
opening brackets we get,
a - b + ab + b - c + bc + c - a + ac
solving like terms and unlike terms we get,
a - a - b + b - c + c + ab + bc + ac
0 + 0 + 0 + ab + bc + ac
ab + bc + ac

questions.question: 5x {tex}\times{/tex} 4x2 =?
questions.explanation:
5x {tex}\times{/tex} 4x2
Multiply constants and variables we get,
5 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} x2
20 {tex}\times{/tex} x2
20x3

questions.question: 5x {tex}\times{/tex} (-4xyz) = ?
questions.explanation:
5x {tex}\times{/tex} (-4xyz)
Multiply the constants and variables
5 {tex}\times{/tex} (-4) {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} z
-20 {tex}\times{/tex} x2 {tex}\times{/tex} y {tex}\times{/tex} z
-20x2yz

questions.question: 2x {tex}\times{/tex} 5y {tex}\times{/tex} 7z =?
questions.explanation:

questions.question: How many circular bases does a cylinder have?
questions.explanation: A cylinder is similar to a prism, but its two bases are circles, not polygons. Also the sides of a cylinder are curved, not flat. There are 2 circular bases 1 on top and 1 on bottom.

questions.question: The base of a prism is an octagon. Find the total number of edges on the prism.
questions.explanation:
An Octagon is a polynomial with 8 sides. In an octagonal prism, we have two octagonal faces so to 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 16 sides or edges.
And that eight corners of one octagon is additionally connected to 8 corners of other Octagon, form more 8 edges.
So total number of edges = 16 + 8 = 24

questions.question: How many vertices does a cone have?
questions.explanation: A cone has only one vertice.

questions.question: The lateral faces of a pyramid are ________.
questions.explanation: Each lateral faces of a pyramid are in triangular shape.

questions.question: How many congruent edges does a cube have?
questions.explanation: A cube has 12 edges. Because all faces are squares and congruent to each other, all 12 edges are the same length. A point formed where edges meet. A cube has 8 vertices.

questions.question: How many bases does a pyramid have?
questions.explanation: It has one base. A pyramid has one base only

questions.question: The base of a prism is a hexagon. How many edges will the prism have
questions.explanation:
The hexagonal prism has 18 edges
As a hexagonal prism have two hexagonal faces. So they have 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 12 edges. And it has 6 rectangular faces who are connected to each other so 6 edges.
So total 12 + 6 = 8 edges.

questions.question:
Which of the statements is true?

  1. The triangular prism has 2 triangular faces and 3 square bases.
  2. The square pyramid has 4 triangular faces and 1 square base.
  3. The square pyramid has 4 triangular faces and 2 square bases.
  4. The triangular prism has 4 triangular faces and 1 square base.
questions.explanation:
Triangular Prism - A triangular prism is a three-sided prism. It is a polyhedron made os a triangular base, a translated copy and three faces joining corresponding sides
Square pyramid - In a square pyramid, the four side faces are triangles. The base is a square. It has 5 vertices (corner points). It has 8 edges.
So both (a) and (b) is true only

questions.question: How many triangles would you find in a net that folds into a square pyramid?
questions.explanation: A square pyramid has four faces so there will be 4 triangles and one base which is square.

questions.question: How many circles would you find in a net that can be folded to form a cylinder?
questions.explanation: There are two circles to form a cylinder. A cylinder has two circular faces.

questions.question: Gaurav wants to make a pentagonal pyramid. How many triangles does he need to make the object?
questions.explanation:
Gaurav needs 5 triangles to make a pentagonal pyramid. A pentagonal pyramid is a pyramid with a pentagonal base upon which are erected five triangular faces that meet at a point.
Hence 5 triangles are required to make a pentagonal pyramid.

questions.question: How many rectangles do we need to make a pentagonal prism?
questions.explanation:
We need 5 rectangles to make a pentagonal prism.
The pentagonal prism is a prism with a pentagonal base. It is a type of heptahedron with 7 faces, 15 edges and 10 vertices.

questions.question:
Which of the statements is false for a pyramid?

  1. A pyramid can have only rectangular base.
  2. A pyramid has triangular faces.
  3. A pyramid is named by the shape of its base.
  4. A pyramid has a polygonal base.
questions.explanation:
A pyramid is a structure whose outer surfaces are triangular and converge to a single point at the top, making the shape like a pyramid in the geometric sense. The base of a pyramid can be trilateral, quadrilateral or any polygonal shape.
So, a pyramid can have an only a rectangular base in wrong.

questions.question: In the art class, Vijay made a design as shown in the figure. If he folds along the dotted lines, which figure will he get?
questions.explanation:
The base of the figures has 6 sides. So, it represents a hexagon. When folded along the dotted lines, the figure represents a pyramid with one hexagonal base. So, Vijay will get a hexagonal pyramid when folded along the dotted lines.
A hexagonal pyramid is a pyramid with a hexagonal base upon which a erected six triangular faces that meet at a point.

questions.question: Which of the following can be calculated only for a cone but not for a cylinder?
questions.explanation:
The slant height of an object (such as a frustum, or pyramid) is the distance measured along a lateral face from the base to th e apex along the center of the face. In other words, it is the altitude of the triangle comprising a lateral face.
So, a cylinder can not have a slant height as it has no slanting face.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is true? 

  1. The lateral faces of a triangular prism can be squares or rectangles
  2. The lateral faces of a triangular pyramid can be squares or rectangles.
  3. The lateral faces of a square pyramid can be squares.
  4. The lateral faces of a square prism are triangles.
questions.explanation:
A triangular prism is a three-sided prism. It has a triangular base. And the lateral faces can be squares or rectangle.
The lateral faces of a triangular pyramid are triangle. The lateral faces of a square pyramid are also the triangle
The lateral faces of a square prism are square.
So (a) is true

questions.question:
Identify the statement that is false for a prism. 

  1. A right prism has triangular lateral faces.
  2. A right prism has two bases.
  3. A right prism has rectangular lateral faces.
  4. A right prism has identical parallel faces.
questions.explanation: A prism is a polyhedron whose bottom and top faces ( or base) are congruent polygons and faces known as lateral faces are parallelogram. When the sid faces of a prism are rectangles, the shape is known as a right prism.

questions.question:
Identify the statement that is true. 

  1. A cone has one circular base.
  2. A cylinder has three circular bases.
  3. A cone has one rectangular base.
  4. A cylinder has two rectangular bases.
questions.explanation:
A cone has one circular base and a lateral surface. the lateral surface of a cone comes to a point called its vertex.
A cylinder has two parallel, congruent circular bases connected by a lateral surface.

questions.question: The faces of a triangular pyramid consist of
questions.explanation:
There are 4 triangles in a triangular pyramid
A triangular pyramid is a pyramid with a triangle as a base and three triangular faces. It has four vertices or points and six edges.

questions.question: The faces of a square prism consist of
questions.explanation:
A square prism has 6 faces, 8 vertices and 12 edges.
A square prism is a three dimensional shape with six rectangular shaped sides, where two of them are squares. All angles are right angles. It is special type of cuboid which has two of its dimensions are same.

Not for Square Prism

6 faces{tex}\left[ \begin{gathered} 2 \ Square \ and \hfill \\ 4 \ rec\tan gles \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right.{/tex}

questions.question: The faces of a cube consist of
questions.explanation: The faces of a cube consist of six square.

questions.question: Find the number of rectangular faces in a decagonal prism.
questions.explanation: The decagonal prism is formed by 10 square sides and two regular decagon base or cap.

questions.question: The base of a pyramid is a polygon with n sides. How many triangular faces does the pyramid have?
questions.explanation: It will have the same number of faces as the number of sides. A pyramid with n sided base has n+1 vertices, n+1 faces and 2n edges.

questions.question: A square pyramid always has ________.

options.html: Two bases which are congruent and parallel.
Four lateral faces, which are congruent equilateral triangles and a rectangular base.
Four lateral faces, which are parallel to each other.
Four lateral faces, which are congruent isosceles triangles and a square base.

questions.explanation: A square pyramid always has a square base and 4 triangular faces which are congruent isosceles triangle. The faces meet as a single point called the apex.

questions.question: A cylinder has ________.

options.html: two bases which may or may not be congruent.
one base which is a circle.
two bases which are congruent triangles.
two bases which are congruent circles.

questions.explanation: A cylinder is a three-dimensional figure with a pair of parallel and congruent ends or base. It has a single curved side that forms a rectangle when laid out flat.

questions.question: Which of the following has no rectangular faces?
questions.explanation: Triangular Pyramid has 4 faces, the 3 side faces are triangles, the base is also a triangle. So no rectangular face.

questions.question: A hexagonal pyramid has ________.

options.html: Six triangular lateral faces and a hexagonal base.
Two parallel bases, which are triangles.
Eight triangular lateral faces and a hexagonal base.
Six triangular lateral faces and two hexagonal bases.

questions.explanation: A hexagonal pyramid has a hexagonal base and six triangular faces that meet a point.

questions.question: The faces of a pentagonal pyramid consist of
questions.explanation: The faces of a pentagonal pyramid consist of five triangles that meets a point.

questions.question: An octagonal prism has ________.
questions.explanation: An octagonal prism has two octagonal faces and 8 square faces. It has 24 edges and 16 vertices.

questions.question: What is the name of this solid?
questions.explanation: It is a squar a pyramid. It has 4 side faces which are triangles. The base is a sqaure. it has 5 vertices and 8 edges.

questions.explanation: Solid geometry is the traditional mane of the geometry of three-dimensional

questions.question: The most important part of a map is ________.
questions.explanation: The scale of a map is the ratio of a distance on the map to the corresponding distance on the ground.

questions.question: What is the base of a hexagonal prism?
questions.explanation: A hexagonal prism is a prism with hexagonal base. It has 8 faces, 18 edges and 12 vertices.

questions.question: What is the base of a heptagonal prism?
questions.explanation: The heptagonal prism is formed by 7 square faces and two heptagons as base or cap. It has 14 vertices and 21 edges.

questions.question: What is the base of an octagonal prism?
questions.explanation: The octagonal prism is formed by sqaure sides and two regular octagon base or caps. So it's base is an octagon.

questions.question: What is the base of a pentagonal prism?
questions.explanation: The pentagonal prism is a prism with a pentagonal base. It has 7 faces,15 edges and 10 vertices.

questions.question: A pyramid with a square base has 5 faces and 8 edges. By Euler’s formula, find the vertices of the pyramid.
questions.explanation:
Euler’s formula: V + F - E = 2
where V, E, and F are respectively the numbers of vertices (corners), edges and faces in the given polyhedron
Here F = 5 and E = 8
So,  V = 2 + E - F = 2 + 8 - 5 = 5

questions.question: Find the number of edges in a rectangular pyramid.
questions.explanation:
A rectangular pyramid has 4 side faces and one base. It has 5 vertices and 8 edges
So, the rectangular pyramid has 5 faces and 5 vertices.
Euler’s formula
F - E + V = 2
E = F + V - 2 = 5 + 5 - 2 = 8

questions.question: Find the number of vertices in a rectangular pyramid.
questions.explanation:
A rectangular pyramid has 4 side faces which are triangles. The base is a square. It has 5 vertices and 8 edges.
Euler’s formula
F - E + V = 2
V = 2 + E - F
here E = 8 and F =5
So, V = 2 + 8 - 5 = 2 + 3 = 5

questions.question: Find the number of faces in a rectangular pyramid.
questions.explanation: A rectangular pyramid has a rectangular base and 4 triangular faces. So total 4 + 1 = 5 faces

questions.question: A two dimensional shape is ________ shape.
questions.explanation: Plane has two dimension, length and breadth.

questions.question: ________ are used to depict different objects/places in a map.
questions.explanation: Symbols are used to depict different objects/places in a map.

questions.question: What are the three views in a solid?
questions.explanation: The three views of solid structures are front view, top view and side view.

questions.question: How many views are there in a solid?
questions.explanation: There are three views of a solid structure. They are a front view, top view and side view.

questions.question: Find the number of edges in the given solid.
questions.explanation:
An edge is a line segment that joins two vertices.
So, there are 15 edges in the above figure.

questions.question: Find the number of faces in a given solid.

questions.explanation: A pentagonal prism is a prism with a pentagonal base. it is a type of heptahedron with 7 faces, 15 edges and 10 vertices

questions.question: By using Euler’s formula find the unknown. Vertices = 12, Faces = 4, Edges =?
questions.explanation:
Formula: F + V - E = 2
Here Vertices = 12, Faces = 4 . i.e. V = 12 and F = 4
4 + 12 - E = 2
i.e. E = 4 + 12 - 2 = 14
So Edges = 14

questions.question: By using Euler’s formula find the unknown. Faces = 5, Edges = 8, Vertices =?
questions.explanation:
Formula: F + V - E = 2
Here  Faces = 5, Edges = 8, So F = 5 and E = 8
So, 5 + V - 8 = 2
V = 2 - 5 + 8 = 5
So vertices = 5

questions.question: By using Euler’s formula find the unknown. Edges = 2, Vertices = 3, Faces = ?
questions.explanation:
Formula is F - V + E = 2
So F = 2 + V - E
Here Edges = 2, Vertices = 3
So E = 2 and V = 3
F = 2 + V - E = 2 + 3 - 2 = 3
So 3 faces

questions.question: There is no reference or perspective in a ________.
questions.explanation: There is no reference or perspective in a map .i.e. objects that are closer to the observer are shown to be of the same size as those that are farther away.

questions.question: F + V - E = ________
questions.explanation:
Formula is F + V - E = 2. It is called Euler Formula
where V = number of vertices, E = number of edges, F= number of faces.

questions.question: F + V - ________ = 2
questions.explanation:
The formula is F + V - E = 2. It is called the Euler Formula
where V = number of vertices, E= number of edges, F= number of faces.

questions.question: F + V ________ E = 2
questions.explanation:
Formula is F + V - E = 2. It is called the Euler Formula
Where V = number of vertices, E= number of edges, F= number of faces.

questions.question: F ________ V – E = 2
questions.explanation:
Formula is F + V - E = 2. It is called Euler Formula
Where V = number of vertices, E = number of edges, F= number of faces.

questions.question: ___ + V - E = 2
questions.explanation:
Formula is F + V - E = 2. It is called Euler Formula
where V = number of vertices, E = number of edges, F= number of faces.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Find the volume of a cuboid whose length is 8 cm, breadth 6 cm and height 3.5 cm.
questions.explanation:
Length of the cuboid = 8 cm
Breadth of the cuboid = 6 cm
Height of the cuboid = 3.5 cm
Volume of the cuboid = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth {tex}\times{/tex} height
= 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 3.5 = 168cm3
Therefore,volume of  the cuboid = 168cm3

questions.question: Find the area of a triangle whose base is 4 cm and altitude is 6 cm.
questions.explanation:
Given that, Base of the triangle = 4 cm
The altitude of the triangle = 6cm
Area of a triangle = {tex} {1 \ \over 2}{/tex}× base {tex}\times{/tex} altitude
So,Area of the given triangle = ({tex} {1 \ \over 2}{/tex}) {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 =12cm2

questions.question: Find the volume of a cuboid whose length is 8 cm, width is 3 cm and height is 5 cm.
questions.explanation:
Length of  the cuboid = 8 cm
Width of the cuboid = 3 cm
Height of the cuboid = 5 cm
Volume of  a cuboid  = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth {tex}\times{/tex} height
Therefore, Volume of the given  cuboid = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 120 cm3

questions.question: Find the cube root of 140 {tex}\times{/tex} 2450
questions.explanation:
140 {tex}\times{/tex} 2450 = 343000
Cube root of 343000  = (343000)1/3 = (703)1/3 = 70

questions.question: Find the perimeter of a rectangle whose length is 4 cm and breadth is 3 cm.
questions.explanation:
Length of the rectangle,l = 4cm
Breadth of the rectangle,b = 3 cm
Perimeter of a rectangle = 2(l + b) = 2(4 + 3) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 14 cm

questions.question: Area of a trapezium = Half of the sum of the lengths of parallel sides {tex}\times{/tex} ________.
questions.explanation: Area of a trapezium = (Half of the sum of the length of parallel sides) {tex}\times{/tex} (perpendicular distance between them).

questions.question: ________ of a solid is the sum of the areas of its faces.
questions.explanation: The surface area of a three-dimensional figure is the sum of the areas of all its faces.

questions.question: The diagonal of a quadrilateral shaped field is 24 m and the perpendiculars dropped on it from the remaining opposite vertices are 6 m and 12 m. Find the area of the field.
questions.explanation:
Diagonal of the quadrilateral shaped field = 24m
Perpendiculars dropped on the diagonal from the remaining opposite vertices = 6 m and 12 m
Area of a quadrilateral = {tex} {1} \over 2{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} diagonal {tex}\times{/tex} sum of the perpendiculars dropped on it from remaining opposite vertices
= {tex} {1} \over 2{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 24 {tex}\times{/tex} (6+12)
= 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 18
= 216m2

questions.question: A rectangular paper of width 7 cm is rolled along its width and a cylinder of radius 20 cm is formed. Find the volume of the cylinder.
questions.explanation:
Here, r = 20cm, h = 7cm
Volume of a cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
={tex}22 \over 7{/tex} x 20 x 20 x 7
= 8800 cm3

questions.question: The top surface of a stage is in the shape of a regular octagon with side 5 m. Find the area of the octagonal surface.
questions.explanation:
Octagon having eight equal sides, each 5 m.
Construction: Divided the octagon in 3 figures, two trapeziums whose parallel and perpendicular sides are 11 m and 4 m respectively and third figure is rectangle having length and breadth 11 m and 5 m respectively.


Now Area of two trapeziums {tex} = 2 \times \frac{1}{2}(a + b) \times h{/tex}
{tex} = 2 \times \frac{1}{2}(11 + 5) \times 4{/tex}{tex} = 4 \times 16 = 64{m^2}{/tex}
And Area of rectangle = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth
{tex} = 11 \times 5 = 55{m^2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Total area of octagon = 64 + 55
= 119 m2

questions.question: The parallel sides of a trapezium are 25 cm and 13 cm. Its non-parallel sides are equal, each being 10 cm. Find the area of the trapezium.
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be the trapezium such that AB = 13 cm and CD = 25 cm and AD = BC = 10 cm
Then ABPE is a parallelogram.
So AB = EP = 13 cm
CD = DE + EP + PC
25 = DE + 13 + PC
25 - 13 = DE + PC
12cm = DE + PC            
DE = PC = 6 cm
In ΔBPC, we have CB = 10 cm, PC = 6 cm
By Pythagoras theorem,
CB2 = BP2 + CP2
(10)2 = BP2 + (6)2
100 = BP2 + 36
100 - 36 = CF2
64 = BP2
{tex}\sqrt {64} = BP{/tex}
8CM = BP
{tex}Area = \frac{1}{2}(sum\;of\;parallel\;sides) \times height{/tex}
{tex}Area = \frac{1}{2}(AB + CD) \times BP{/tex}
{tex}Area = \frac{1}{2}(13 + 25) \times 8{/tex}
{tex}Area = \frac{1}{2}(38) \times 8 = 38 \times 4 = 152c{m^2}{/tex}

questions.question: Find the area of a quadrilateral whose one of the diagonals is 12cm and the length of the perpendiculars drawn from its opposite vertices are 3.5 cm and 4 cm respectively.
questions.explanation:
Area of a quadrilateral = {tex}{1 \over 2} {/tex} (Length of the diagonal) {tex}\times{/tex} (Sum of the perpendiculars drawn from the remaining vertices)
= {tex}{1 \over 2} {/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} (3.5 + 4) = {tex}{1 \over 2} {/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 7.5
= 45 cm2

questions.question: Find the altitude of a trapezium, the sum of the lengths of whose bases is 6.5 cm and whose area is 26cm2.
questions.explanation:
Area of trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (sum of parallel sides) {tex}\times{/tex} (perpendicular distance between them) 
26 = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 6.5 {tex}\times{/tex} Perpendicular distance
Perpendicular Distance or altitude = {tex} {26\times 2 \over 6.5}{/tex} = 8 cm

questions.question: Find the total surface area of a cube whose volume is 343cm2.
questions.explanation:
Volume of cube = l3, where l = length
343 = l3
l = (343)1/3 = 7 cm
Now, total surface area of a cube = 6 l2 = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 72 = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 49 = 294 cm2

questions.question: A cylindrical tank has a capacity of 5632m3. If the diameter of its base is 16 m, find its depth.
questions.explanation:
Given that, Volume of the cylindrical tank = 5632m3
Diameter of its base, d = 16m
So, Radius of its base, r = {tex}\frac{d}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{16}{2}{/tex} = 8 m
Let 'h' be its depth.
Now, Volume of  a cylinder =  {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 5632 = {tex}22 \over7{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 82 {tex}\times{/tex} h 
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} 5632 = {tex}22 \over7{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 64 {tex}\times{/tex} h 
{tex} \Rightarrow {/tex} h = 5632 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}7 \over22{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}1 \over64{/tex} = 28 cm
Therefore, the depth of the tank = 28 cm

questions.question: Find the area of a rhombus whose diagonals are of lengths 20 cm and 16 cm.
questions.explanation:
Area of a rhombus = {tex}1 \over 2{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (The product of its diagonals)
{tex}1 \over 2{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 16
= 160 cm2

questions.question: Find the area of the given figure.
questions.explanation:
Area of  a parallellogram =  Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5
= 10 cm2

questions.question: Area of a rhombus = ________

options.html: Double the product of its diagonals
One-fourth of the product of its diagonals
Half the product of its diagonals
One-third of the product of its diagonals

questions.explanation:
Area of a rhombus = Half the product of its diagonals
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} d1 {tex}\times{/tex} d2

questions.question: Surface area of a cuboid = ________.
questions.explanation:
Surface area of a cuboid = 2(lb + bh + hl)
where, l = length
b = breadth
and h = height

questions.question: A godown is in the form of a cuboid of measures 60m {tex}\times{/tex} 40m {tex}\times{/tex} 20m. How many cuboidal boxes can be stored in it if the volume of one box is 0.8 m3?
questions.explanation:
length = 60m , breadth = 40m , height = 20m
volume of godown = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth {tex}\times{/tex} height
v = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 40 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 48,000m3
volume of one box = 0.8 m3
Number of cuboidal boxes = {tex}{Volume \ of \ godown \over Volume \ of \ one\ box}{/tex}
={tex}{{48000} \over {0.8}} = {{480000} \over 8}{/tex} = 60,000
number of cuboidal boxes = 60,000

questions.question: The perimeter of a trapezium is 52 cm. Its non-parallel sides are 10 cm each and the distance between two parallel sides is 8 cm. Find the area of the trapezium.
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of trapezium = Sum of all sides = Sum of non parallel sides + sum of parallel sides
52 = 20 + sum of parallel sides
Sum of parallel sides = (52 - 20) = 32 cm
Area of trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (sum of parallel sides) {tex}\times{/tex} (perpendicular distance between them) 
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}  {tex}\times{/tex} 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 8
= 128 cm2

questions.question: The cost of papering the wall of a room, 12 m long, at the rate of Rs. 1.35 per square meter is Rs. 340.20. The cost of matting the floor at Re. 0.85 per square metre is Rs. 91.80. Find the height of the room.
questions.explanation:
Let the length of room = l = 12 m
Breadth of room = b m
Height of room = h m
Cost of matting the floor at the rate of Re. 0.85 per m2 = Rs. 91.80
Area of the floor = 91.80 / 0.85 = 108 m2
⇒ l {tex}\times{/tex} b = 108
⇒ 12 {tex}\times{/tex} b = 108
⇒ b = {tex}\frac{108}{12}{/tex}1
⇒ b = 9 m
Cost of papering the wall at the rate of Rs. 1.35 per m2 = Rs. 340.20
Area of the wall is the lateral surface area.
Area of the wall = Lateral surface area = {tex}\frac{340.20}{1.35}{/tex} = 252 m2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2h(l + b) = 252
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2h(12 + 9) = 252
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2h {tex}\times{/tex} 21 = 252
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = {tex}\frac{252}{2 \times 21}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = 6 m
Height of the room is 6m.

questions.question: The area of a trapezium is 384cm2. Its parallel sides are in the ratio 3:5 and the distance between them is 12 cm. Find the length of each parallel side.
questions.explanation:
Let the parallel sides be 3p and 5p
Area of trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (sum of parallel sides) {tex}\times{/tex} (perpendicular distance between them)
384 = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (3p + 5p) {tex}\times{/tex} 12
384 = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 8p {tex}\times{/tex} 12
{tex}\frac{384 \times 2}{12}{/tex} = 8p
64 = 8p
p = 8 cm
3p = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 24 cm
5p =  5 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 40 cm
Therefore, the parallel sides are 24 cm and 40 cm.

questions.question: In the given figure, find the area of the path.
questions.explanation:
Here we have to find the difference between the area of big rectangle and small rectangle.
Area of the big rectangle = Length x Breadth
{tex}150 \times 110{/tex}
= 16500cm2
Width of the path = 5 cm
Length of the small rectangle = Length of the big rectangle - (2 {tex}\times{/tex} width of the path) = 150 - 10 = 140 cm
Breadth of the small rectangle = Breadth of the big rectangle - (2 {tex}\times{/tex} width of the path) = 110 - 10 = 100cm
Area of the small rectangle = Length x Breadth 
{tex} = 140 \times 100{/tex}
= 14000cm2
Area of the path = 16500cm2 - 14000cm2
= 2500cm2

questions.question: Find the height of a cuboid whose volume is 275cm3 and base area is 25cm3.
questions.explanation:
Area of the base = Product of length and breadth
Volume of cuboid = Length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth {tex}\times{/tex} Height
= Area of its base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
275 = 25 {tex}\times{/tex} Height
Height = {tex}\frac{275}{25}{/tex}
= 11 cm

questions.question: If the edge of a cube is 1 cm then which of the following is its volume?
questions.explanation: 1 m3

questions.question: Find the volume of 64 cubes where the side of each cube is 4 cm.
questions.explanation:
Volume of one cube = l3
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
= 64 cm3
Volume of 64 cubes = 64 {tex}\times{/tex} 64
= 4096cm3

questions.question: Find the height of a cuboid whose volume is 490cm3 and base area is 35cm3.
questions.explanation:
Area of its base = Product of its length and breadth
Volume of a cuboid = Product of its length, breadth and height
or, Volume of the cuboid = Product of its height and the area of its base
490 = Height {tex}\times{/tex} 35
Height = {tex}\frac{490}{35}{/tex}
Height = 14cm

questions.question: Find the perimeter of the given figure.
questions.explanation:
First, we have to find the perimeter of its top by using the formula of the perimeter of a semi-circle = {tex}\pi r{/tex}
{tex}(22/7) \times (3.5/2){/tex}
= 5.5 cmPerimeter of the given figure = Perimeter of the semi- circular top + Sum of the remaining sides = 5.5 + 2+ 2
=  9.5 cm

questions.question: Surface area of a cube = ________
questions.explanation:
Surface area of a cube = 6l2 , where l is the  side of the cube.
Since, there are six faces of a cube each of area l2 , the total surface area of the cube is 6l2

questions.question: Amount of region occupied by a solid is called its ________.
questions.explanation: Volume is the amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object or region of space, expressed in cubic units.

questions.question: The internal measures of a cuboidal room are 10m {tex}\times{/tex} 8m {tex}\times{/tex} 4m. Find the total cost of whitewashing four walls of a room, if the cost of whitewashing is Rs 5 per m2.
questions.explanation:
Area of four walls = 2h(l + b)  = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 (10+8)
= 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 144m2
Cost of washing per metre square = Rs.5
Cost of washing 144m2
= 144 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = Rs.720

questions.question: Square and a rectangle have the same perimeter, if the side of the square is 16m and the length of the rectangle is 18 m, find the breadth of the rectangle.
questions.explanation:
side of square = 16 m
perimeter of square = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} side
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 16 = 64m
length of rectangle = 18 m , let breadth of rectangle = b 
perimeter of rectangle = 2(l + b) = 2(18 + b) 
perimeter of square = perimeter of rectangle
64 = 2(18 + b)
64 = 36 + 2b
64 = 36 = 2b
28 = 2b
{tex}\frac{{28}}{2} = b{/tex}
14m = b
breadth = 14m

questions.question: A cylindrical container of radius 28 cm contains sufficient water to submerge a rectangular solid of dimensions 32cm {tex}\times{/tex} 22cm {tex}\times{/tex} 14 cm. Find the rise in the level of water, when the solid is completely submerged.
questions.explanation:
Radius of the cylindrical container = 28 cm
Dimensions of the rectangular solid or cuboid i.e. length = 32 cm, breadth = 22 cm and height = 14 cm
Let the height of the water level be  'h' cm
So, the volume of the water displaced by the rectangular solid = volume of the cylindrical container
{tex}l \times b \times h = \pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}32 \times 22 \times 14 = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(28)^2} \times h{/tex}
{tex}9856 = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 784 \times h{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{9856 \times 7}}{{22 \times 784}} = h{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{68992}}{{17248}} = h{/tex}
4cm = height

questions.question: A cylindrical tube, open at both ends is made of metal. The internal diameter of the tube is 10.4 cm and its length is 25 cm. The thickness of the metal is 8 mm everywhere. Calculate the volume of the metal in the cylinder.
questions.explanation:
Diameter of inner cylinder = 10.4 cm
{tex}radius = r = {d \over 2} = {{10.4} \over 2} = 5.2cm{/tex} , height = 25 cm
volume of inner cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(5.2)^2} \times 25{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 27.04 \times 25{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{14872}}{7} = 2124.57c{m^3}{/tex}
thickness of the metal = 8mm = 0.8 cm
Radius of 0uter cylinder = 5.2 cm +0.8 cm = 6cm
volume of outer cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(6)^2} \times 25{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 36 \times 25{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{19800}}{7} = 2828.57c{m^3}{/tex}
Volume of the metal in the cylinder = volume of outer cylinder - volume of inner cylinder
=  (2828.57 - 2124.57) cm3
= 704cm3
volume of the metal = 704 cm3

questions.question: Find the area of the roads, if two roads are running in cross-section, through the middle of a ground.
questions.explanation:
Length of horizontal road = 225m and breadth = 10m
Length of vertical road = 10m and breadth = 175m
Side of square = 10m
In rectangle (HEFG), length = 225 m and breadth = 10 m
Area of horizontal road (HEFG)  = {tex}l \times b = 225 \times 10 = 2250{m^2}{/tex}
In rectangle(LKJI), length = 10m and height = 175 m
Area of vertical road (LKJI) = {tex}l \times b = 10 \times 175 = 1750{m^2}{/tex}
Area of square (MNOP) = (side)2 = (10)2 = 100 m2
Area of cross roads = area of horizontal road (HEFG) + area of vertical road (LkJI) - arae of square (MNOP)
= 2250 + 1750 - 100 = 4000 - 100 = 3900 m2

questions.question: Find the area of a rhombus whose diagonals are of measurements 6 cm and 8 cm.
questions.explanation:
Diagonals of rhombus are 6cm and 8cm 
so let first diagonal be p and 2nd diagonal be q
p = 6 cm and q = 8cm 
area of rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {/tex} product of diagonals
area = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times p \times q = {1 \over 2} \times 6 \times 8 = {{48} \over 2} = 24c{m^2}{/tex}
Area of rhombus = half the product of its diagonals
Area of rhombus = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 8
Area of rhombus = 24cm2

questions.question: How many bricks will be required for a wall which is 8 m long, 6 m high and 22.5 cm thick, if each brick measures 25cm {tex}\times{/tex} 11.25cm {tex}\times{/tex} 6cm?
questions.explanation:
length of wall = 8m = 800cm, breadth = 22.5cm, height = 6m = 600cm
volume of wall = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth {tex}\times{/tex} height
v = 800 {tex}\times{/tex} 22.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 600 = 10800000cm3
length of brick = 25 cm, breadth = 11.25 cm, height = 6 cm
volume of each brick = l {tex}\times{/tex} b {tex}\times{/tex} h
v = 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 11.25 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 1687.5 cm3
Number of bricks = {tex}\frac{volume \ of \ walls}{volume \ of \ each \ brick}{/tex}
={tex}{{10800000} \over {1687.5}} = {{108000000} \over {16875}}{/tex} = 6400
number of bricks is 6400.

questions.question: Find the volume of a cylinder whose base radius is 14 cm and height is 35 cm.
questions.explanation:
raidus = 14 cm , height = 35 cm
Volume of cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(14)^2} \times 35{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 196 \times 35{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{150920}}{7}{/tex} = 21560cm3

questions.question: Find the volume of the cylinder whose height is 7 cm and radius is 20 cm.
questions.explanation:
raidus = 20 cm , height = 7cm
Volume of cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(20)^2} \times 7{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 400 \times 7{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{61600}}{7}{/tex} = 8800cm3

questions.question: Find the area of the following trapezium.
questions.explanation:
Given : parallel sides of trapezium are 17 cm and 34 cm and height is 10cm
Area of trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (sum of parallel sides) {tex}\times{/tex} (height)
Area of trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (17+ 34) {tex}\times{/tex} 10
Area of trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 51 {tex}\times{/tex} 10
Area of trapezium =  255 cm2

questions.question: Total surface area of a cylinder = ________
questions.explanation:
Total surface area of a cylinder = {tex}{\text{2}}\pi r\left( {r + h} \right){/tex}
or we can write S = {tex}2\pi {r^2} + 2\pi rh{/tex}
where r = radius , h = height

questions.question: 1cm3 = ________ mL
questions.explanation:
1cm3 = 1 mL
or 1 mL = 1cm3

questions.question: Find the area of a rhombus whose side is 5 cm and its altitude is 4 cm.
questions.explanation:
here rhombus is a parallelogram,
side = base = 5cm and altitude = height = 4cm
Area of rhombus = Area of parallelogram
= base {tex}\times{/tex} height = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
= 20cm2

questions.question: Radha bought a rectangular plot of dimensions 120m {tex}\times{/tex} 80m and Radhika bought a square field of dimension 95 m. Who bought a plot of greater area and by how much?

options.html: Radha bought a plot of the greater area by 575m<sup>2</sup> more
Radha bought a plot of the greater area by 675m<sup>2</sup> more
Radhika bought a plot of the greater area by 375m<sup>2</sup> more
Radhika bought a plot of the greater area by 475m<sup>2</sup> more

questions.explanation:
Area of a rectangular plot of Radha = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth
Area of a rectangular plot of Radha = 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 80 
Area of a rectangular plot of Radha = 9600m2
Area of a square field of Radhika = (side)2
Area of sqaure plot of Radhika = 95 {tex}\times{/tex} 95
Area of a square plot of Radhika = 9025m2
area of a rectangular plot is greater than the area of the square plot (9600 - 9025 = 575m2)
Radha bought a plot of the greater area by 575m2 more

tests.name: Mensuration Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 8 Mensuration CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A pool is 20 m long, 15 m broad and 4 m deep. Find the cost of cementing its floor and its walls at the rate of Rs.12 per square metre.
questions.explanation:
length = 20m, breadth = 15 m, height = 4m 
area of walls = 2h(l+b) 
area = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4(20 + 15) = 8(35) = 280m2
area of floor = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth = 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 300m2
total area = area of walls + area of floor = 280 + 300 = 580m2
the cost of cementing its floor and its walls at the rate of per square metre = Rs 12
the cost of cementing its floor and its walls at the rate of 580m2 = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 580 = 6960
so, the cost is Rs.6960

questions.question: A tin is in a cylindrical shape whose base has a diameter of 14 cm and height 20 cm. A label is placed around the surface of the container. If the label is placed 2 cm from top and bottom, what is the area of the label?
questions.explanation:
diameter = d = 14 cm
radius = r = {tex}{d \over 2} = {{14} \over 2}{/tex} = 7 cm, height = 20 cm
curved surface area of Cylinder = {tex}2\pi rh{/tex}
CSA = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
CSA = {tex}2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 7 \times 20{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{6160}}{7}{/tex} = 880cm2
New height if label is placed 2 cm from top and bottom = 20 - 2 - 2 = 16 cm
curved surface area of labelled Cylinder = {tex}2\pi rh{/tex}
CSA = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
CSA = {tex}2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 7 \times 16 = \frac{{4928}}{7}{/tex} = 704cm2
curved surface area of the label = CSA of cylinder - CSA of labelled cylinder
= (880 - 704) cm2
= 176 cm2

questions.question: Find the perimeter of the dollhouse.
questions.explanation:
dollhouse is made up of traingle and rectangle.
sum of all sides are 2+2+3+3+2=12m

questions.question: Find the volume of the cylinder whose base diameter is 14 cm and height is 10 cm.
questions.explanation:
diameter = 14 cm
Radius = 7 cm, height = 10 cm
Volume of cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(7)^2} \times 10{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 49 \times 10 = \frac{{10780}}{7}{/tex}
= 1540cm2

questions.question: The diameter of the garden roller is 1.4 m and it is 2 m long. How much area will it cover in 5 revolutions?
questions.explanation:
Diameter = d = 1.4 m
radius = r = {tex}{d \over 2} = {{1.4} \over 2}{/tex} = 0.7m
height = 2 m
Curved surface area of cylinder = {tex}2\pi rh{/tex}
CSA = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
CSA = {tex}2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 0.7 \times 2 = \frac{{61.6}}{7}{/tex} = 8.8m2
no. of revolutions = 5 
Area it will cover in 5 revolutions = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 8.8
= 44m2

questions.question: Find the area of a parallelogram whose base and height are 8cm and 3 cm.
questions.explanation:
base = 8 cm, height = 3 cm
Area of parallelogram = {tex}base \times height{/tex}
Area of parallelogram = {tex}8 \times 3 = 24c{m^2}{/tex}
Area of parallelogram = 2 × (1/2 x 8x 3)
Area of parallelogram = (2 × 12) cm2
Area of parallelogram = 24cm2

questions.question: Find the side of a cube whose surface area is 2400cm2.
questions.explanation:
let the side of cube be 'a' cm
surface area of cube = 2400 cm2
Surface area of cube = 6(side)2 = 6a2
2400 = 6a2
{tex}\frac{{2400}}{6} = {a^2}{/tex}
400 = a2
{tex}\sqrt {400}{/tex} = a
200 = a = side
side of cube = 20 cm

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus are 16 cm and 12 cm, find its area.
questions.explanation:
let the 1st diagonal be p and 2nd diagonal be q 
p = 16 cm and q = 12 cm
Area of rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {/tex} product of its diagonals
area = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times p \times q = {1 \over 2} \times 16 \times 12 = {{192} \over 2} = 96c{m^2}{/tex}

questions.question: Volume of a cuboid = ________.
questions.explanation:
Volume of a cuboid = (l {tex}\times{/tex} b {tex}\times{/tex} h) cubic units
where l = length, b = breadth, h = height

questions.question: 1L = ________ cm3
questions.explanation:
1L = 1000cm3
or we can write, 1 litre = 1000 cubic centimetre

questions.question: In a building, there are 4 cylindrical pillars. The radius of each pillar is 21 cm and the height is 5 m. Find the curved surface area of four pillars.
questions.explanation:
radius =  21 cm = 0.21m , height = 5m
Curved surface area of cylinder = {tex}2\pi rh{/tex}
CSA = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
{tex}CSA = 2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 0.21 \times 5{/tex}
{tex}CSA = \frac{{46.2}}{7} = 6.6{m^2}{/tex}
the curved surface area of one pillar = 6.6 m2
the curved surface area of four pillars = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 6.6 =26.4 m2

questions.question: The parallel sides of a trapezium are in the ratio 2: 3 and the area of the trapezium is 125 cm2. The distance between the parallel lines is 10 cm. Find the length of the parallel sides of the trapezium.
questions.explanation:
The ratio of two parallel sides of a trapezium = 2:3
let the 1st parallel side be 2x and 2nd parallel side be 3x
height = 10 cm
area of trapezium = 125 cm2
Area of trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (sum of two parallel sides) × height
125 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times (2x + 3x) \times 10{/tex}
125 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times (2x + 3x) \times 10{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{125 \times 2}}{{5 \times 10}}{/tex} = x
5 = x
1st parallel side = 2x = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 10 cm
2nd parallel side = 3x = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 15 cm

questions.question: A rectangular piece of iron sheet is 44 m long and 20 m broad. It is rolled along its length to form a cylinder. Find the volume of the cylinder so formed.
questions.explanation:
Length of the iron sheet = 44 m
breadth = 20m
now it is rolled along its length
so the circumference of cylinder = 44m
height of the cylinder will be 20 m
circumference of cylinder = {tex}2\pi r{/tex}
{tex}44 = 2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times r{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{44 \times 7}}{{2 \times 22}} = r{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{308}}{{44}} = r{/tex}
7m = r
radius = 7m                          
volume of cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(7)^2} \times 20{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 49 \times 20{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{21560}}{7} = 3080{m^3}{/tex}
so, volume of cylinder = 3080 m3

questions.question: A rectangle piece of metal sheet 11m {tex}\times{/tex} 4m is folded without overlapping to make a cylinder of height 4 m. Find the volume of the cylinder.
questions.explanation:
A rectangle piece of metal sheet is folded without overlapping to make a cylinder.
length of cylinder = 11m and height of cylinder = 4m
circumference of cylinder = 11m 
circumference of cylinder= {tex}2\pi r{/tex}
{tex}C = 2\pi r{/tex}
{tex}11 = 2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times r{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{11 \times 7}}{{2 \times 22}} = r{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{77}}{{44}} = r{/tex}
1.75cm = radius
volume of cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}V = \pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(1.75)^2} \times 4{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{22}}{7} \times (1.75 \times 1.75 \times 4){/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{269.5}}{7} = 38.5{m^3}{/tex}
volume of cylinder = 38.5 m3

questions.question: In the given figure of a cube and a cuboid which one has a greater surface area and by how much?

options.html: Cuboid has the greater area by 168 m<sup>2</sup> more
Cube has the greater area by 148 m<sup>2</sup> more
Cube has the greater area by 268 m<sup>2</sup> more
Cuboid has the greater area by 148 m<sup>2</sup> more

questions.explanation:
Cuboid, length = 15m, breadth = 6m, height = 2m 
Surface area of cuboid =  {tex}2(length \times breadth + breadth \times height + height \times length){/tex}
{tex}S = 2(15 \times 6 + 6 \times 2 + 2 \times 15){/tex}
S = 2(90 + 12 + 30)
{tex}S = 2(132) = 2 \times 132 = 264{m^2}{/tex}
Surface area of cuboid = 264m2
cube, side = 4m
Surface area of cube = 6(side)2
Surface area of cube = {tex}6{(4)^2} = 6 \times 4 \times 4 = 96{m^2}{/tex}
Surface area of cube = 96m2
surface area of cuboid = 264 m2 and surface area of cube = 96 m2
Cuboid has the greater area by (264 - 96)=168 m2 more

tests.name: Mensuration Test 05
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 8 Mensuration CBSE Online Test 05 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Mrs. Kaushik has a square plot with the measurement as shown in the figure. She wants to construct a house in the middle of the plot. A garden is developed around the house. Find the total cost of developing a garden around the house at the rate of Rs 55 per {tex}{{\text{m}}^{\text{2}}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Side of square ABCD = 25 m
Area of square ABCD = (side)2
A=(25)2 = 625 m2
Area of square plot = 625 m2
length of the house (QR) = 20 m and breadth ( PQ) = 15 m
Area of house PQRS = {tex}length \times breadth{/tex}
A = {tex}20 \times 15 = 300{m^2}{/tex}
Area of house = 300 m2
Area of the garden = Area of square plot - area of house 
Area of the garden = {tex}(625 - 300){m^2}{/tex}
Area of the garden = 325 m2
Cost of developing a garden per m2 = Rs 55
Cost of developing a garden 325 m2 = {tex}325 \times 55 = Rs17875{/tex}
cost of developing a garden is Rs 17,875.

questions.question: A flooring tile has the shape of a parallelogram whose base is 24 cm and the corresponding height is 10 cm. How many such tiles are required to cover a floor of area 1080 m2?
questions.explanation:
Tile is in the shape of parallelogram.
base of parallelogram = 24 cm = 0.24 m , height = 10 cm = 0.1 m
Area of parallelogram = base {tex}\times{/tex} height
A = 0.24 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.1 = 0.024 m2
Area of 1 tile = 0.024 m2
Area of floor = 1080 m2
Number of tiles required to cover the floor
= {tex}\frac{{Area\;of\;floor}}{{Area\;of\;one\;tile}}{/tex}
={tex}{{1080} \over {0.024}}{/tex} = 45000 tiles
the number of tiles are 45000.

questions.question: The area of a trapezium-shaped field is 480 m2, the distance between two parallel sides is 15 m and one of the parallel sides is 20 m. Find the other parallel side.
questions.explanation:
Height = 15 m, let 1st parallel side = 20m, let  2nd parallel side be x
Area of a trapezium = 480 m2
Area of a trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {/tex} (sum of two parallel sides) {tex} \times {/tex} height
{tex}480 = \frac{1}{2} \times (20 + x) \times 15{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{480 \times 2}}{{15}} = 20 + x{/tex}
64 = 20 + x
64 - 20 = x
44m = x
2nd parallel side = 44 m

questions.question: The area of a rhombus is 240 cm2 and one of the diagonals is 16 cm. Find the other diagonal.
questions.explanation:
Area of rhombus = 240 cm2
1st diagonal = 16 cm and let 2nd diagonal be x
Area of rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {/tex} product of diagonals
{tex}240 = {1 \over 2} \times {/tex} 1st diagonal {tex} \times {/tex} 2nd diagonal
{tex}240 = \frac{1}{2} \times 16 \times x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{240 \times 2}}{{16}}{/tex} = x
{tex}\frac{{480}}{{16}}{/tex} = x
30cm = x
2nd diagonal = 30 cm

questions.question: The shape of the top surface of a table is a trapezium. Find its area if its parallel sides are 1 m and 1.2 m and the perpendicular distance between them is 0.8 m.
questions.explanation:
Height of trapezium = 0.8 m, 1st parallel side = 1 m, 2nd parallel side = 1.2 m
Area of trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (sum of two parallel sides) {tex}\times{/tex} height
{tex}A = \frac{1}{2} \times (1 + 1.2) \times 0.8{/tex}
{tex}A = \frac{1}{2} \times 2.2 \times 0.8{/tex}
{tex}A = \frac{{1.76}}{2} = 0.88{m^2}{/tex}
area of trapeizum = 0.88 m2

questions.question: The area of a trapezium is 34 cm2 and the length of one of the parallel sides is 10 cm and its height is 4 cm. Find the length of the other parallel side.
questions.explanation:
Height of trapezium = 4 cm, 1st parallel side = 10 cm, let 2nd parallel side be x
Area of trapezium = 34 cm2
Area of trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (sum of parallel sides) {tex}\times{/tex} height
{tex}34 = \frac{1}{2} \times (10 + x) \times 4{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{34 \times 2}}{4} = 10 + x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{68}}{4} = 10 + x{/tex}
17 = 10 + x
17 - 10 = x
7cm = x
2nd parallel side = 7cm

questions.question: Length of the fence of a trapezium-shaped field ABCD is 120 m. If BC = 48 m, CD = 17 m and AD = 40 m, find the area of this field. Side AB is perpendicular to the parallel sides AD and BC.
questions.explanation:
Length of the fence of a trapezium shaped field ABCD = 120 m
perimeter of trapezium = sum of all sides of trapezium
120 = AB + BC + CD + AD 
120 = AB + 48 + 17 + 40 
120 = AB + 105 
120 - 105 = AB 
15 m = AB
height = 15 m and 1st parallel side = 40 m and 2nd parallel side = 48 m
Side AB is perpendicular to the parallel sides AD and BC.
Area of the field =  {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {/tex} ( Sum of parallel sides) {tex} \times {/tex} height
{tex}A = \frac{1}{2} \times (40 + 48) \times 15{/tex}
{tex}A = \frac{1}{2} \times (88) \times 15{/tex}
{tex}A = \frac{{1320}}{2} = 660{m^2}{/tex}
Area of the field = 660m2

questions.question: The diagonal of a quadrilateral shaped field is 24 m and the perpendiculars dropped on it from the remaining opposite vertices are 8 m and 13 m. Find the area of the field.
questions.explanation:
ABCD quadilateral is divided into two traingles ABC and ADC.
in traingle ABC, base = 24 m and height = 13 m
Area of triangle ABC = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times base \times height{/tex}
Area of traingle ABC = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times AC \times BQ{/tex}
{tex}A = {1 \over 2} \times 24 \times 13={1 \over 2} \times 312=156{m^2}{/tex}
Area of  traingle ABC = 156 m2
Area of triangle ADC = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times base \times height{/tex}
Area of traingle ADC = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times AC \times DP{/tex}
{tex}A = {1 \over 2} \times 24 \times 8 = {1 \over 2} \times 192 = 96{m^2}{/tex}
Area of  traingle ADC = 96 m2
Area of quadilateral = area of  traingle ABC + area of traingle ADC
= 156 + 96 = 252 m2

questions.question: Find the volume of a box, which is in the form of a rectangular prism, with a length of 6 in., a height of 3 in. and width of 5 in.
questions.explanation:
length = 6 in. , breadth = 5 in., height = 3 in.
Volume of a box = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth {tex}\times{/tex} height
v = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 90 in3
volume of a box is 90 in.3

questions.question: What is the surface area of a book 13 cm long, 10 cm wide, and 3 cm high?
questions.explanation:
length of book = 13 cm, breadth = 10 cm, height = 3 cm
Surface area of book = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex} b + b {tex}\times{/tex} h + h {tex}\times{/tex} l)
S = 2(13 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 + 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 13)
S = 2(1300 + 30 + 39)
S = 2(199) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 199 = 398cm2
surface area of book = 398 cm2

questions.question: Each side of a square piece of tissue paper is 3 centimetres long. What is the tissue paper's area?
questions.explanation:
side of square = 3 cm
Area of square piece of a tissue paper= (side)2
= (3)2 = 9 cm2

questions.question: What is the surface area of the drawing box, if its length is 16 cm, width 6 cm, and height 3 cm?
questions.explanation:
length of drawing box= 16 cm , breadth = 6 cm , height = 3 cm
Surface area of drawing box = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex}b + b {tex}\times{/tex} h + h {tex}\times{/tex} l)
S = 2(16 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 + 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 16)
S = 2(96 + 18 + 48)
S = 2(162) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 162 = 324cm2

questions.question: The dimensions of an iron box are 9ft {tex}\times{/tex} 4.4ft {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5ft. What is the cost of the iron sheet used to make the box, if the cost of the sheet is Rs 6 per square foot?
questions.explanation:
length of iron box = 9 ft , breadth = 4.4 ft , height = 2.5 ft
Surface area of iron box = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex} b + b {tex}\times{/tex} h + h {tex}\times{/tex} l)
S = 2(9 {tex}\times{/tex} 4.4 + 4.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 + 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 9)
S = 2(39.6 + 11 + 22.5)
S = 2(73.1) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 73.1 = 146.2ft2
Surface area of iron box = 146.2 ft2
The cost of the sheet per square foot = Rs 6
The cost of the sheet 146.2 ft2 = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 146.2 = Rs 877.2
The cost is Rs 877.2

questions.question: A rectangular ink pad has a perimeter of 36 centimetres and an area of 80 square centimetres. What are the dimensions of the ink pad?

options.html: 8 cm and 10 cm or 10cm and 8 cm

questions.explanation:
perimeter of rectangular ink pad = 36 cm
periemeter of rectangle = 2(length+breadth)
36 =2(l+b)
{tex}\frac{{36}}{2} = l + b{/tex}
18 = l + b
or we we can write l = 18 - b
area of rectangular ink pad = 80 cm2
area of rectangle = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
80 = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
put l = 18 - b we get,
80 = (18 - b) {tex}\times{/tex} b
80 = 18b - b2
80 - 18b + b2 = 0
b2 - 18b + 80 = 0
b2 - 10b - 8b + 80 = 0
b(b - 10) - 8(b - 10) = 0
(b - 10)(b - 8) = 0
b - 10 = 0, b - 8 = 0
b = 0 + 10, b = 0 + 8
b = 10cm, b = 8cm 
breadth = 10 cm or 8 cm
put the value of breadth in l = 18 - 6
l = 18 - 10 = 8cm
l = 18 - 8 = 10
length = 8 cm or 10 cm
if breadth is 10 cm, length is 8 cm 
or if breadth is 8 cm, length is 10 cm.

questions.question: Two cubes each with an edge of 30 cm are joined to form a rectangular prism. What is the surface area of the rectangular prism formed?
questions.explanation:
length of rctangular prism = 30 + 30 = 60 cm and breadth = 30 cm and height = 30 cm
The surface area of rectangular prism = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex} b + b {tex}\times{/tex} h + h {tex}\times{/tex} l)
S = 2(60 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 + 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 + 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 60)
S = (1800 + 900 + 1800)
S = 2(4500) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4500 = 9000cm2
Surface arae of rectangular prism = 9000 cm2
