questions.question: The total surface area of a rectangular block is 1097 cm2. What is the area of four sides of the block, if length is 19 cm and the width is 16 cm?
questions.explanation:
The total surface area of a rectangular block is 1097 cm2
length = 19 cm , breadth = 16 cm and let the height be x cm.
The total surface area of a rectangular block = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex} b + b {tex}\times{/tex} h + h {tex}\times{/tex} l)
1097 = 2(19 {tex}\times{/tex} 16 + 16 {tex}\times{/tex} x + x {tex}\times{/tex} 19)
1097 = 2(304 + 16x + 19x)
{tex}\frac{{1097}}{2}{/tex} = 304 + 35x
548.5 = 304 + 35x
548.5 - 304 = 35x
22.45 = 35x
6.98cm = x
height = 6.98 cm
Area of four sides of the block = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} height(length + breadth)
A = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 6.98(19 + 16)
A = 13.96(35) = 13.96 {tex}\times{/tex} 35 = 488.6 cm2
area of four sides of the block = 488.6 cm2

questions.question: A rectangular cement patio is 10 meters long and 8 meters wide. What is its area?
questions.explanation:
length of rectangular cement patio = 10 m and breadth = 8 m
Area of cement patio = length {tex}\times{/tex} breadth
Area of cement patio = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 80m2

questions.question: The total surface area of a cube is 1014 ft2. What is the length of its edge?
questions.explanation:
Total surface area of cube = 1014 ft2 and let the side of cube be x ft
Total surface area of cube = 6(side)2
1014 = 6(x)2
{tex}\frac{{1014}}{6} = {x^2}{/tex}
169 = x2
{tex}\sqrt {169}{/tex} = x
13ft = x = side
the length is 13 ft.

questions.question: A rectangular ink pad is 12 centimetres long and 5 centimetres wide. What is its perimeter?
questions.explanation:
length = 12 cm and breadth = 5 cm
Perimeter of rectangular ink pad = 2(length + breadth)
P = 2(12 + 5)
P = 2(17) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 = 34cm
perimeter of rectangular ink pad = 34 cm.

questions.question: What is the surface area of the four walls of the water tank, if its length is 8 ft, width 5 ft and height 5 ft?
questions.explanation:
length = 8 ft. , breadth = 5 ft. and height = 5 ft.
The surface area of the four walls of the water tank = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} height(length + breadth)
S = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5(8 + 5)
S = 10(13) = 130 ft2
The surface area of four walls of the water tank is 130 ft2.

questions.question: What is the volume of this cylinder? Use {tex}\pi {\text{ }} \approx {\text{ 3}}.{\text{14}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
radius of cylinder = 1 cm and height of cylinder = 3cm
Volume of a cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}V = 3.14 \times {(1)^2} \times 3{/tex}
{tex}V = 3.14 \times 1 \times 3 = 9.42c{m^3}{/tex}
volume of cylinder is 9.42 cm3.

questions.question: The outer dimensions of a water tank are 7 meters, 6 meters and 5 meters. A quart of paint covers about 15 m2. How many quarts do you need to paint the outer walls of the tank?
questions.explanation:
length = 7m , breadth = 6m and height = 5m
Surface area of four walls = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} height(length + breadth)
S = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5(7 + 6)
S = 10(13) = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 = 130m2
A quart of paint covers 15m2
15m2 = 1 Qaurt of paint
1m2 = {tex}{1 \over {15}} {/tex} quart of paint
130 m2 = {tex}{1 \over {15}} \times 130 = 8.6{/tex} quart of paint.
8.6 quarts of paint is needed.

questions.question: The surface area of the four sides of a cubical dice is 4cm2. What must be the length of its edge?
questions.explanation:
the surface area of four sides = lateral surface area.
So, lateral surface area of cube = 4 cm2 and side be x cm
lateral surface area of cube = 4(side)2
4 = (4x)2
{tex}\frac{4}{4} = {x^2}{/tex}
1 = x2
{tex}\sqrt 1{/tex} = x
1cm = x = side

questions.question: The dimensions of an iron box are 9 ft {tex}\times{/tex} 4.4 ft {tex}\times{/tex} 7.2 ft. What is the cost of the iron sheet used to make the box, if the cost of the sheet is Rs 0.90 per square foot?
questions.explanation:
length of an iron box = 9 ft., breadth = 4.4 ft., height = 7.2 ft.
Surface area of the iron box = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex} b + b {tex}\times{/tex} h + h {tex}\times{/tex} l)
S = 2(9 {tex}\times{/tex} 4.4 + 4.4 {tex}\times{/tex} 7.2 + 7.2 {tex}\times{/tex} 9)
S = 2(39.6 + 31.68 + 64.8)
S = 2(136.08) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 136.08 = 272.16 ft2
surface area of an iron box is 272.16 ft2
the cost of the sheet per ft2= Rs. 0.90
272.16 ft2 cost = 0.90 {tex}\times{/tex} 272.16 = Rs 244.94
the cost of the iron sheet used to make the box is Rs 244.94.

questions.question: The shape of a garden is rectangular in the middle and semi circular at the ends as shown in the diagram. Find the area of this garden.
questions.explanation:
Diameter of semi circle = 7m

radius of semi circle = {tex}r = {1 \over 2} \times diameter = {1 \over 2} \times 7 = {7 \over 2} = 3.5m{/tex}

Length of rectangular field = 20 - (3.5 +3.5) = 20 - 7 = 13 m  and breadth = 7m 

 Area of rectangular field = {tex}length \times breadth{/tex}

Area of rectangular field = {tex}13 \times 7 = 91{m^2}{/tex}

Area of two semi circles = {tex}2 \times {1 \over 2} \times \pi {r^2}{/tex}

Area of two semi circles= {tex}2 \times {1 \over 2} \times {{22} \over 7} \times {(3.5)^2}{/tex}{tex}= 2 \times {1 \over 2} \times {{22} \over 7} \times 12.25 = {{539} \over {14}} = 38.5{m^2}{/tex}

Area of garden = Area of rectangular field + Area of two semi circles

Area of garden =91 + 38.5 = 129.5m2

questions.question: The shape of a garden is rectangular in the middle and semi circular at the ends as shown in the diagram. Find the perimeter of this garden.
questions.explanation:
given : PQ = 20 m

Diameter of semi circle (CD) = 7m

radius of semi circle = {tex}r = {1 \over 2} \times diameter = {1 \over 2} \times 7 = {7 \over 2} = 3.5m{/tex}

perimeter of a semi circle = {tex}\pi r{/tex}

perimeter of two semi circle = {tex}2 \times \pi r = 2 \times {{22} \over 7} \times 3.5 = {{154} \over 7} = 22m{/tex}

In rectangle ABCD,

 length of rectangular field (BC) =PQ - (r + r) = 20 - (3.5 + 3.5) = 20 - 7.0 = 13m and BC = AD = 13m

perimeter of garden = perimeter of two semi circles + BC + AD

perimeter of garden = ​​​​​​​22 + 13 + 13 = 48m​​​​​​​

questions.question: The dimensions of a rectangular iron box are l {tex}\times{/tex} w {tex}\times{/tex} h. How many times will the surface area of the iron box increase, if all its dimensions are doubled?
questions.explanation:
length = l  , breadth = w and height = h
Surface area of rectangular iron box = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex} b + b {tex}\times{/tex} h + h {tex}\times{/tex}l)
S = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex} w + w {tex}\times{/tex} h + h + l)
S = 2(lw + wh + hl)
when dimensions are doubled,
New Length = 2l, breadth = 2w, height = 2h
The new surface area of the iron box = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex} b + b {tex}\times{/tex} h + h {tex}\times{/tex} l)
S = 2(2l {tex}\times{/tex} 2w + 2w {tex}\times{/tex} 2h + 2h {tex}\times{/tex} 2l)
S = 2(4lw + 4wh + hl)
S = 8(lw + wh + hl)
No. of times surface area will increase = new surface area/surface area of iron box
= {tex}{{8(lw + wh + hl)} \over {2(lw + wh + hl)}} = {8 \over 2}{/tex} = 4
surface area will increase 4 times.

questions.question: The length and width of a park are 20 m and 15 m respectively. There is a path of 1 m width inside the park; along the periphery. Find the area of the path.
questions.explanation:
length of the park = 20 m and breadth = 15 m
Area of park = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 300m2
area of park = 300 m2
length of park excluding path = 20 -1 -1 = 18 m, breadth = 15 - 1- 1 = 13 m
area of park without path = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 18 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 = 234m2
area of park without path is 234 m2
area of path = area of park - area of park without path 
= ( 300 - 234 ) m2
= 66 m2

questions.question: The length and width of a park are 20 m and 15 m respectively. There is a path of 1 m width inside the park; along the periphery. Find the area of the park excluding the path.
questions.explanation:
length of the park = 20 m and breadth = 15 m
Area of park = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 300m2
area of park = 300 m2
length of park excluding path = 20 -1 -1 = 18 m , breadth = 15 - 1- 1 = 13 m
area of park without path = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 18 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 = 234m2
area of park without path is 234 m2.

questions.question: A park has a 2-meter walkway inside along the periphery. Park is 40 meters long and 22 m wide. Find the area of walkway running inside the park.
questions.explanation:
length of park = 40 m and breadth = 22 m
Area of park = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 40 {tex}\times{/tex} 22 = 880 m2
area of park = 880 m2
Length of park without pathway = 40 - 2 - 2 = 36 m, Breadth = 22 - 2 - 2 = 18 m
Area of the park without pathway = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 36 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 648 m2
The area of the park without the walkway is  648 m2
area of walkway running inside the park = area of the park - an area of park without a pathway
= (880 - 648) m2
= 232 m2

questions.question: A park has a 2 meter walkway inside along the periphery. Park is 40 meter long and 22 m wide. Find the area of the park without the walkway.
questions.explanation:
length of park = 40 m and breadth = 22 m
Area of park = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 40 {tex}\times{/tex} 22 = 880 m2
area of park = 880 m2
Length of park without pathway = 40 - 2 - 2 = 36 m, Breadth = 22 - 2 - 2 = 18 m
Area of park without pathway = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 36 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 648m2
The area of the park without the walkway is  648 m2.

questions.question: The side of a square field is equal to 60 meter. There is a path of 10 meter width which is running inside the field along its periphery. Find the area of the path.
questions.explanation:
side of outer square = 60 m
Area of outer square field = (side)2
A = (60)2
A = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 = 3600 m2
area of outer square field = 3600 m2
side of  inner square field =  60 - 10 - 10 = 40 m
Area of inner square field = (side)2
A = (40)2
A = 40 {tex}\times{/tex} 40 = 1600 m2
area of inner square field = 1600 m2
Area of path = Area of the outer square field - Area of an inner square field
= (3600 - 1600) m2
= 2000 m2

questions.question: There is a 1 m wide walkway running inside a square park along its circumference. If the side of the park is 32 meter, find the area of the path.
questions.explanation:
side of the park = 32 m
Area of park= (side)2
A = (32)2
A = 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 32 = 1024 m2
Area of park = 1024 m2
side of park without park = 32 - 1 - 1 = 30 m
Area of  park without path= (side)2
A = (30)2
A = 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 = 900 m2
Area of path = Area of Park - Area of the park without path
= (1024 - 900) m2
= 124 m2

questions.question: There is a 1 m wide walkway running inside a square park along its circumference. If the side of the park is 32 meter, find the area of park without the path.
questions.explanation:
side of the park = 32 m
Area of park = (side)2
A = (32)2
A = 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 32 = 1024 m2
Area of park = 1024 m2
side of park without path = 32 - 1 - 1 = 30m
Area of park without path= (side)2
A = (30)2
A = 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 = 900m2
the area of park without the path = 900 m2

questions.question: The perimeter of one face of a cube is 40 cm. What is its surface area?
questions.explanation:
The perimeter of one face of a cube = 40 cm
The perimeter of one face of a cube = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} side
40 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} side
{tex}\frac{{40}}{4}{/tex} = side
10cm = side
The surface area of cube = 6(side)2
S = 6(10)2
S = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 600 cm2
surface area of cube = 600 cm2

questions.question: A square park has 3-meter wide road around its periphery. If the side of the park is 100 meter, find the area of the path.
questions.explanation:
side of park = 100 m
Area of park = (side)2
Area = (100)2
Area = 100 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = 10000 m2
Area of park = 10000 m2
side of periphery = 100 + 3 + 3 = 106 m
Area of park around its periphery = (side)2
Area = (106)2
Area = 106 {tex}\times{/tex} 106 = 11236 m2
Area of park around its periphery = 11236 m2
Area of path = Area of the park with a path around its periphery - Area of the park
= (11236 - 10000)m2 = 1236 m2

questions.question: A square park has 3-meter wide road around its periphery. If the side of the park is 100 meter, find the area of the park with the path.
questions.explanation:
side of park = 100 m
Area of park = (side)2
A = (100)2
A = 100 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = 10000 m2
Area of park = 10000 m2
side of park with path = 100 + 3 + 3 = 106 m
Area of park with path = (side)2
A = (106)2
A = 106 {tex}\times{/tex} 106 = 11236 m2
Area of the park with a path around its periphery is 11236 m2

questions.question: If the volume of a cube is divided by its surface area, the result is 2.5 cm. What is the length of the edge of the cube?
questions.explanation:
let the side of cube be x cm
Volume of cube = (side)3
V = (x)3 = x3
Surface area of cube = 6(side)2
S = 6(x)2 = 6x2
according to question,
{tex}{{volume} \over {surfacearea}} = 2.5cm{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{{x^3}}}{{6{x^2}}}{/tex} = 2.5
{tex}\frac{x}{6}{/tex} = 2.5
x = 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 15 cm
length of cube is 15 cm.

questions.question: A 3-meter wide road is running horizontally across the middle of a square park. If the side of the park is 60 meter, find the area of the road.
questions.explanation:
length of road = side of park = 60 m
breadth of road = 3 m 
Area of road = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 180 m2
Area of road = 180 m2

questions.question: A 3-meter wide road is running horizontally across the middle of a square park. If the side of the park is 60 meter, find the area of the park without the road.
questions.explanation:
length of the park without the road = 60 m and breadth = 60 - 3 = 57 m
Area of park without the road = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
A = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 57 = 3420 m2
Area of park without road = 3420 m2

questions.question: The large cube is made up of small cubes each with an edge of 4 units. What is the surface area of the resultant cube, if one cube in the middle of the top row is removed?
questions.explanation:
Side of a cube = 4 units 
Side of a large cube = 4 + 4 + 4 = 12 units
Surface area of large cube = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} (side)2
Surface area of large cube = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} (12)2 = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 144 = 864 unit2
Now one cube in the middle of the top row is removed,
Side of a cube = 4 units
Surface area of a cube = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} (side)2
surface area of cube = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} (4)2 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 16 = 64 unit2
Surface area after cube is removed = surface area of large cube + surface area of cube
Surface area = 864 + 64 = 928 unit2

questions.question: Find the surface area of a rectangular packing box measuring 12 in. in length, 5 in. in width, and 2 in. in height.
questions.explanation:
length of box = 12 in. , breadth = 5 in, and height = 2 in.
Surface area of box = 2(l {tex}\times{/tex} b + b {tex}\times{/tex}h + h {tex}\times{/tex} l)
S = 2(12 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 + 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 12)
S = (2(60 + 10 + 24)
S = 2(94) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 94 = 188 in.2
the surface area of the box is 188 in.2

questions.question: What is the surface area of a cylindrical iron bar, if the base radius is 5 in. and its height is 72 in.?
questions.explanation:
radius of cylindrical bar = 5 in. and height = 72 in.
The surface area of the cylindrical bar = {tex}2\pi r(r + h){/tex}
{tex}S = 2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 5(5 + 72){/tex}
{tex}S = \frac{{22}}{7}(77){/tex}
{tex}S = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 77{/tex}
{tex}S = \frac{{16940}}{7}{/tex} = 2420 in.2
the surface area of cylindrical bar = 2420 in.2

questions.question: The base area of a cylindrical wooden block is 113.04 cm2 and its height is 10 cm. What is its surface area?
questions.explanation:
The base area of a cylindrical wooden block = 113.04 cm2 and height = 10 cm
area of base = {tex}\pi {r^2}{/tex}
{tex}113.04 = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {r^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{113.04 \times 7}}{{22}} = {r^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{791.28}}{{22}} = {r^2}{/tex}
35.96 = r2
{tex}\sqrt {35.96}{/tex} = r
5.99mc = radius
radius of cylindrical bar = 5.99 cm and height = 10 cm
Surface area of cylindrical bar = {tex}2\pi r(r + h){/tex}
{tex}S = 2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 5.99(5.99 + 10){/tex}
{tex}S = \frac{{263.56}}{7}(15.99){/tex}
{tex}S = \frac{{263.56}}{7} \times 15.99{/tex}
{tex}S = \frac{{4214.32}}{7} = 602.04c{m^2}{/tex}
the surface area of cylindrical bar = 602.04 cm2

questions.question: A cylinder has a volume of 965 cm3. If the height of the cylinder is 16 cm, find the radius.
questions.explanation:
height of cylinder = 16 cm and volume of cylinder = 965 cm3
Volume of a cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}965 = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {r^2} \times 16{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{965 \times 7}}{{22 \times 16}} = {r^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{6755}}{{352}} = {r^2}{/tex}
19.19 = r2
{tex}\sqrt {19.19}{/tex} = r
4.38cm = radius
radius of cylinder = 4.38 cm

questions.question: The base of a cone has a radius of 4 cm. The height of the cone is 6 cm. Find the volume of the cone.
questions.explanation:
radius of cone = 4 cm and height of cone = 6 cm
volume of cone = {tex}{1 \over 3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times {(4)^2} \times 6{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times 16 \times 6{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{{96\pi }}{3} = 32\pi {/tex}
the volume of a cone is 32{tex}\pi{/tex} cm3

questions.question: A cone has a volume of 1650 cm3. The cone has a height of 28 cm. Find the radius of the cone.
questions.explanation:
volume of a cone = 1650 cm3 and height = 28 cm
Volume of a cone = {tex}{1 \over 3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}1650 = \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times {(r)^2} \times 28{/tex}
{tex}1650 = \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times {r^2} \times 28{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{1650 \times 3 \times 7}}{{22 \times 28}} = {r^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{34650}}{{616}} = {r^2}{/tex}
56.25 = r2
{tex}\sqrt {56.25}{/tex} = r
7.5cm = radius
radius of cone is 7.5 cm
radius of cone is 7.5 cm

questions.question: A cylinder is made from metal. It has a base but no lid. The height of the cylinder is 8 cm. The radius of the cylinder is 3 cm. Find the amount of metal required to make the cylinder.
questions.explanation:
radius of cylinder = 3 cm and height of cylinder = 8 cm
Amount of metal required to make the cylinder = {tex}2\pi rh + \pi {r^2}{/tex}
Amount = {tex}\pi{/tex}(2rh + r2)
Amount = {tex}\pi{/tex}(2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 + (3)2)
Amount = {tex}\pi{/tex}(48 + 9)
Amount = {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 57 = 57cm2
Amount of metal required to make the cylinder = 57{tex}\pi{/tex}cm2

questions.question: What is the base radius of the cylinder, if a 44 in. long and 20 in. the broad rectangular tin sheet is rolled along its length to form the cylinder by making the opposite edges just touch each other?
questions.explanation:
length of rectangle = circumference of the base of cylinder = 44 inch
circumference of the base of cylinder= {tex}2\pi r{/tex}
{tex}44 = 2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times r{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{44 \times 7}}{{2 \times 22}} = r{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{308}}{{44}} = r{/tex}
7 = radius
radius of cylinder = 7 inch

questions.question: The base area of a cylindrical wooden block is 254.34 cm2 and its height is 13 cm. What is its surface area?
questions.explanation:
base area of a cylindrical wooden block =  254.34 cm2 and height = 13 cm
base area of cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}{/tex}
{tex}254.34 = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(r)^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{254.34 \times 7}}{{22}} = {(r)^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{1780.38}}{{22}} = {r^2}{/tex}
80.92 = r2
{tex}\sqrt {80.92} = r{/tex}
8.99cm = radius
the surface area of cylinder {tex} = 2\pi r(r + h){/tex}
{tex}254.34 = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(r)^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{254.34 \times 7}}{{22}} = {r^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{1780.38}}{{22}} = {r^2}{/tex}
80.92 = r2
{tex}\sqrt {80.92} = r{/tex}
8.99 cm = radius
the surface area of cylinder = {tex}2\pi r(r + h){/tex}
{tex}2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 8.99(8.99 + 13){/tex}
S = {tex}\frac{{395.56}}{7}(21.99){/tex}
S = {tex}\frac{{395.56}}{7}{/tex} = 21.99
S = {tex}\frac{{8608.36}}{7}{/tex}
S = 1242.62 cm2
The surface area of the cylindrical wooden block is 1242.62 cm2

questions.question: If the radius of the base of a right circular cone is 9r mm. and its height is equal to the radius of the base, find its volume in mm3.
questions.explanation:
radius of cone = 9r and height = radius = 9r
Volume of a cone = {tex}{1 \over 3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times {(9r)^2} \times 9r{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times 81{r^2} \times 9r{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{{729\pi {r^3}}}{3} = 243\pi {r^3}{/tex}
volume of cone = 243{tex}\pi {r^3}{/tex} mm3

questions.question: The circumference of the base of a cone of height 9 in. is 44 in. Find the volume of the cone.
questions.explanation:
circumference of the base of a cone = 44 in.
c = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r
{tex}44 = 2 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times r{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{44 \times 7}}{{2 \times 22}} = r{/tex} 
7 in radius
the volume of cone = {tex}{1 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
{tex}v = \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(7)^2} \times 9{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 7 \times 7 \times 9{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{9702}}{{21}} = 462\;in{.^3}{/tex}

questions.question: What is the volume of a cone, if its radius is 9 cm and its curved surface area is 423.9 cm2?
questions.explanation:
radius of cone = 9cm
curved surface area of cone = 423.9 cm2
curved surface area of cone = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
CSA = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
{tex}423.9 = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 9 \times l{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{423.9 \times 7}}{{22 \times 9}} = l{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{2967.3}}{{198}} = l{/tex}
14.98cm = l
slant height = l = 14.98 cm
l2 = r2 + h2
(14.98)2 = (9)2 + h2
224.4004 = 81 + h2
224.4004 - 81 = h2
143.4004 = h2
{tex}\sqrt {143.4004}{/tex} = h = 11.97 cm
height = 11.97 cm
the volume of cone = {tex}{1 \over 3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}Volume = \frac{1}{3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(9)^2} \times 11.97{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 9 \times 9 \times 11.97{/tex}
{tex}v = \frac{{21330.54}}{{24}} = 1015.74c{m^3}{/tex}
volume of cone = 1015.74 cm3

questions.question: The height of a right circular cone is 9 cm. If its volume is 432{tex}\pi{/tex} cm3, what is the slant height of the cone?
questions.explanation:
the volume of right circular cone =  432{tex}\pi{/tex} cm3
{tex}v = \frac{1}{3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}432\pi = \frac{1}{3}\pi {r^2}(9){/tex}
{tex}\frac{{432\pi \times 3}}{{\pi \times 9}} = {r^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{1296}}{9} = {r^2}{/tex}
144 = r2
{tex}\sqrt {144}{/tex} = 12cm = r
slant height = l
l2 = r2 + h2
l2 = (12)2 + (9)2 = 144 + 81 = 225
l = {tex}\sqrt {225}{/tex} = 15 cm

questions.question: A tent is in the shape of a cylinder with a conical top. The radius of the base of the tent is 8 m. The height of the cylindrical part is 15 m. and that of the conical part is 24 m. Find the volume of air that occupies the tent.
questions.explanation:
radius of cone = 8 m and height of cone = 24 m
Volume of cone = {tex}{1 \over 3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(8)^2} \times 24{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 64 \times 24{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{{33792}}{{21}} = 1609.14{m^3}{/tex}
volume of cone = 1609.14 m3
radius of cylinder = 8 m and height of cylinder = 15 m
volume of cylinder = {tex}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(8)^2} \times 15{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 64 \times 15{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{{21120}}{7} = 3017.14{m^3}{/tex}
volume of cylinde r= 3017.14 m3
The volume of air that occupies the tent = Volume of the cylindrical part + Volume of the conical part
Volume of air that occupies the tent = (3017.14 + 1609.14 ) m3 = 4626.28 m3
The volume of air that occupies the tent = 4626.28 m3

questions.question: A tent is in the shape of a cylinder with a conical top. The radius of the base of the tent is 9 m. The height of the cylindrical part is 16 m. and that of the conical part is 25 m. Find the volume of air that occupies the tent.
questions.explanation:
Volume of air that occupies the tent = Volume of the cylindrical part + Volume of the conical part
Volume of air that occupies the tent = {tex}\pi {r^2}h + \frac{1}{3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{22}}{7} \times 9 \times 9 \times 16 + \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 9 \times 9 \times 25{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{22}}{7} \times 9 \times 9(16 + \frac{{25}}{3}){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{{1782}}{7}(\frac{{48 + 25}}{3}) {/tex} = {tex}\frac{{1782}}{7} \times \frac{{73}}{3}{/tex}
= 6194.57 m3
Volume of air that occupies the tent = 6194.57 m3

questions.question: Find the volume of a cone, if the diameter of the cone is 12.4 cm. and the height of the cone is 30 cm.
questions.explanation:
diameter = 12.4 cm
{tex}radius = \frac{d}{2} = \frac{{12.4}}{2} = 6.2cm{/tex}
height = 30 cm
volume of cone {tex} = \frac{1}{3}\pi {r^2}h{/tex}
V = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(6.2)^2} \times 30{/tex}
V = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times 6.2 \times 6.2 \times 30{/tex}
V = {tex}\frac{{25370.4}}{{21}}{/tex} = 1208.11 cm3
volume of cone is 1208.11 cm3

questions.question: The curved surface area of a cone is 3815.10 mm2 and the radius of the base of the cone is 27 mm. What is its height?
questions.explanation:
radius = r = 27 mm
The curved surface area of a cone(CSA) = 3815.10 mm2
CSA = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
{tex}3815.10 = \frac{{22}}{7} \times 27 \times l{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{3815.10 \times 7}}{{22 \times 27}}{/tex} = l
{tex}\frac{{26705.7}}{{594}}{/tex} = l
44.95mm = l
slant height = l = 44.95 mm and let height be ' h' mm
l2 = r2 + h2
(44.95)2 = (27)2 + h2
2020.5025 = 729 + h2
2020.5025 - 729 = h2
1291.5025 = h2
{tex}\sqrt {1291.5025}{/tex} = h = 35.93 mm
height = 35.93 mm

questions.question: Find what length of canvas 12 meters in width is required to make a conical tent 12 meters in diameter and 6.3 meters in slant height.
questions.explanation:
diameter = 12 m
radius = {tex}r = {1 \over 2} \times d = {1 \over 2} \times 12 = 6m{/tex} and slant height = l = 6.3 m
Therefore, Area of canvas required = Surface area of the conical tent = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
Area = {tex}{{22} \over 7} \times 6 \times 6.3 = {{831.6} \over 7}{/tex} = 118.8 m2
width of the canvas = breadth = 12 m and let the length of the canvas be l m
area of canvas = l {tex}\times{/tex} b
118.8 = l {tex}\times{/tex} 12
{tex}\frac{{118.8}}{{12}}{/tex} = l
9.9m = length
length of canvas = 9.9 m

questions.question: The circumference of the base of a conical tent is 31.4 m. and its slant height is 9 m. Find the area of the canvas used in making the tent.
questions.explanation:
The circumference of the base of a conical tent = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r = 31.4 m
2 {tex}\times{/tex}  3.14 {tex}\times{/tex} r = 31.4
r = 5 m
Area of the canvas used in making the tent = {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r {tex}\times{/tex} l
= {tex}\frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 9
Area of the canvas used in making the tent = 141.42 m2

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: (-2)5 {tex}\div{/tex} (-2)8 =?
questions.explanation: (-2)(5 - 8) = (-2)(-3) = {tex}\frac{1}{(-2)^3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{8}{/tex}

questions.question: Write the expression using exponents: 61 {tex}\times{/tex} 61 {tex}\times{/tex} 61 {tex}\times{/tex} 61 {tex}\times{/tex} 61
questions.explanation:
61(1) {tex}\times{/tex} 61(1) {tex}\times{/tex} 61(1) {tex}\times{/tex} 61(1) {tex}\times{/tex} 61(1)

questions.question: Evaluate: 82

questions.question: Simplify and write in exponential form: (-2)-3 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2)-4
The repeated factor in an exponential expression is called ________.
questions.explanation: In an exponent, the base is raised to the power as rp where r is a base and p is exponent or power.

questions.question: When we have to add numbers in standard form, we convert them into numbers with the ________ exponents.
questions.explanation: Since the addition of numbers with different exponents leads to incorrect results.

questions.question: A group of students were given the assignment to collect different types of leaves. The group collected 32 types of leaves. Represent the number of leaves collected in the form of exponential expression with its base being indivisible.
questions.explanation:

= 25

a =10,
a2 = 102
10 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 100

questions.question: Expand 1025.63 using exponents.

questions.question: Expand 1256.249 using exponents.
questions.explanation: Multiply each digit with the exponential form of its place value. Rules for expansion of digits at thousands, hundred, tens, ones, tenth, hundredth & thousandth.

questions.question: Find m so that (-3)m + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} (-3)5 = (-3)7
questions.explanation:
(-3)m + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} (-3)5 = (-3)7
(-3)m + 1 = {tex}\frac{(-3)^7}{(-3)^5}{/tex}
(-3)m + 1 = (-3)2
so, m + 1 = 2
m = 1

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}{\left( { - {\text{3}}} \right)^{\text{2}}} \times {\left( {\frac{5}{3}} \right)^2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
= {tex}{\left( { - {\text{3}}} \right)^{\text{2}}} \times {\left( {\frac{5}{3}} \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}{\left( { {\text{9}}} \right)} \times {\left( {\frac{{5}^2}{{3}^2}} \right)}{/tex}
= {tex}{ { {\text{9}}}} \times \frac{25}{9 } {/tex}
= 25

questions.question: Write the expression using exponents: 89 {tex}\times{/tex} 89 {tex}\times{/tex} 89 {tex}\times{/tex} 89

891 {tex}\times{/tex} 891 {tex}\times{/tex} 891 {tex}\times{/tex} 891
= 89(1+1+1+1)
= 894

questions.question: Very small numbers can be expressed in standard form using ________ exponents.

questions.explanation: Any number raise to power 0 is equal to 1. Exponents rule.

questions.question: The area of a square is given by the formula A = c2. What will be the total area of 5 such similar squares, if the side of a square is 8 ft?

Area of the square = (Side)2
so, area of one square = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 64

= 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 3-5 {tex}\times{/tex} 36

questions.question: A group of students were given the assignment to collect different types of leaves. The group collected 243 types of leaves. Represent the number of leaves collected in the form of exponential expression with its base being indivisible.
questions.explanation:

questions.explanation:
(-5)a + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} (-5)2 = (-5)6
(-5)a + 3 = (-5)6 {tex}\div{/tex} (-5)2
(-5)a + 3 = (-5)6 - 2
(-5)a + 3 = (-5)4
Hence, a + 3 = 4,
So, a = 1

questions.question: Find the value of b so that (4)b - 3 {tex}\times{/tex} (4)8 = (4)7
questions.explanation:
(4)b - 3 {tex}\times{/tex} (4)8 = (4)7
(4)b - 3 = (4)7 {tex}\div{/tex} (4)8
(4)b - 3 = (4)7 - 8
(4)b - 3 = (4)-1
Hence, b - 3 = -1
b = -1 + 3 = 2

questions.question: Find the value of n so that (6)n + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} (6)5 = (6)11
questions.explanation:
(6)n + 3 {tex}\times{/tex} (6)5 = (6)11
(6)n + 3 = (6)11​ {tex}\div{/tex} (6)5
(6)n + 3 = (6)11​ {tex}\times{/tex} (6)-5
(6)n + 3 = (6)11 - 5​
(6)n + 3 = (6)6
Hence, n + 3 = 6
So, n = 3

questions.question: Simplify and write in exponential form: x2 {tex}\times{/tex} x-5
questions.explanation:
By exponents rule, powers of two exponents with the same base are added for multiplication.
So, x2 {tex}\times{/tex} x-5
= x2 - 5
= x-3

questions.question: Simplify and write in exponential form: m-3 {tex}\times{/tex} m-12
questions.explanation:
By exponents rule, powers of two exponents with the same base are added for multiplication.
So, m-3 {tex}\times{/tex} m-12
= m-3 - 12
= m-15

questions.question: Simplify and write in exponential form: n4 {tex}\times{/tex} n5
questions.explanation:
By exponents rule, powers of two exponents with the same base are added for multiplication.
So, n4 {tex}\times{/tex} n5
= n4 + 5
= n9

questions.question: Simplify and write in exponential form: a-7 {tex}\times{/tex} a10
questions.explanation:
By exponents rule, powers of the exponents with the same base are added for multiplication.
So, a-7 {tex}\times{/tex} a10
= a-7 + 10
= a3

questions.question: Simplify and write in exponential form: b3 {tex}\times{/tex} b6
questions.explanation:
By exponents rule,
Powers of two exponents with same base gets added for multiplication.
So, b3 {tex}\times{/tex} b6
= b3 + 6
= b9

questions.question: x0 = ________
questions.explanation: By exponents rule any number raised to power zero is one.

questions.question: m0 = ________.
questions.explanation: By exponents rule any number raise to power zero is one

questions.question: y0 = ________.
questions.explanation: By exponents rule any number raise to power zero is one

questions.question: Find the value of the expression 5 {tex}\times{/tex} (-m)2, for m = 2.
questions.explanation:
For m = 2
5 {tex}\times{/tex} (-m)2
= 5 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2)2
= 5 {tex}\times{/tex} (4)
= 20

questions.question: Find the value of the expression 4 {tex}\times{/tex} (-x)2, for x = 5.
questions.explanation:
For x = 5
4 {tex}\times{/tex} (-x)2
4 {tex}\times{/tex} (-5)2
4 {tex}\times{/tex} (25)
100

questions.question: Write the expression using exponents: 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 33 {tex}\times{/tex} 33 {tex}\times{/tex} 33
questions.explanation:
121 {tex}\times{/tex} 331 {tex}\times{/tex} 331 {tex}\times{/tex} 331
= 121 {tex}\times{/tex} 331 + 1 + 1
= 121 {tex}\times{/tex} 333

questions.question: Write the expression using exponents: 27 {tex}\times{/tex} 27 {tex}\times{/tex} 27 {tex}\times{/tex} 57 {tex}\times{/tex} 57 {tex}\times{/tex} 57
questions.explanation:
271 {tex}\times{/tex} 271 {tex}\times{/tex} 271 {tex}\times{/tex} 571 {tex}\times{/tex} 571 {tex}\times{/tex} 571
= 271 + 1 + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 571 + 1 + 1
= 273 {tex}\times{/tex} 573

questions.question: Find the value of the expression (3m)3, for m = 4.
questions.explanation:
For m = 4,
(3m)3
= (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4)3
= (12)3
=12 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 12
= 1728

questions.question: Find the value of the expression (5x)2, for x = 2.
questions.explanation:
For x = 2
(5x)2
= (5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2)2
= (10)2
= 100

questions.question: Find the value of the expression (7y)3, for y = 1.
questions.explanation:
For y = 1,
(7y)3
= (7 {tex}\times{/tex} 1)3
= (7)3
= 343

  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: If two quantities x and y vary directly, then
questions.explanation: As x and y vary directly that is x = ky where, k is a constant, then k = {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}. So the correct answer is {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}

questions.question: If two quantities x and y vary inversely then
questions.explanation: As x and y vary inversely then, x = {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}, where k is any constant, so k = x {tex}\times{/tex} y. So the answer is x {tex}\times{/tex} y

questions.question: If 8 : 12 :: 6 : a, the value of a is
questions.explanation:
8 : 12 :: 6 : a
{tex}\frac{8}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{a}{/tex}
8a = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 12
a = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{12}{8}{/tex}
a = {tex}\frac{72}{8}{/tex}
a = 9
So the answer is 9

questions.question: If 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} m, the value of m is
questions.explanation:
16 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} m
16 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{9}{12}{/tex} = m
{tex}\frac{144}{12}{/tex} = m
12 = m. So the answer is 12

questions.question: If the cost of 18 pens is Rs. 234, a dozen pen will cost
questions.explanation:
Cost of 18 pens=  Rs 234
So Cost of 1 pen = {tex}\frac{234}{18}{/tex} = 13
So a dozen pens(12 pens) will cost
= 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 = Rs 156

questions.question: Raju earns Rs 1440 if he works for 12 days. If he works for 30 days, he will earn
questions.explanation:
Money earned for 12 days = Rs 1440
So, money earned in 1 day = {tex}\frac{1440}{12}{/tex} = Rs 120
So, money earned in 30 days = 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 = Rs 3,600

questions.question: A tree of 20m height casts a shadow 8 m long, what is the height of a tree that casts a shadow 10 m long.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in height of tree the height of shadow increases
So, in direct proportion, the constant value is taken as {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{20}{8}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{h}{10}{/tex} (where h is the height of the tree)
8h = 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 10
h = {tex}\frac{200}{8}{/tex} = 25m

questions.question: There is enough food to last for 40 people for 10 days. If 10 more people join them, the food will last for
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the increase in people the day's food will last will decrease
So, in inverse proportion, the constant is taken as x {tex}\times{/tex} y
40 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = (40 + 10) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a = the food will last)
400 = 50 a
{tex}\frac{400}{50}{/tex} = a
8 days = a

questions.question: A car is travelling at a speed of 54 km/ hour, covers a distance in 2.5 hours. At a speed of 45 km/hr, it covers the same distance in
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with a decrease in speed the time taken will increase.
In inverse proportion, the constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
So, 54 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 = 45 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where  a is the time taken)
{tex}\frac{135}{45}{/tex} = a
3 hours = a

questions.question: 25 labourers can build a wall in 4 days. If only 20 labourers report for work, the wall be built-in
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of labourers the time taken to complete the work will increase.
So, in inverse proportion, the constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
So, 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 20 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the time taken to build the wall)
100 = 20a
{tex}\frac{100}{20}{/tex} = a
5 days = a

questions.question: When one quantity is increased, the other quantity is also increased. This proportion is called ________.
questions.explanation: The answer is Direct proportion as the quantity directly increases with the increase in other quantity.

questions.question:
If a and b vary inversely, find the value of x.

a 50 25
b 2 x
questions.explanation:
As in inverse proportion, the constant value is given by a {tex}\times{/tex} b
So, 50 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 25 {tex}\times{/tex} x
100 = 25 x
{tex}\frac{100}{25}{/tex} = x
4 = x

questions.question: 72 books are packed in 4 cartons of the same size. How many cartons are required for 360 books?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of books the number of cartons will increase
So, in direct proportion, the constant is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{72}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{360}{a}{/tex} ( where a is the number of cartons required)
72 {tex}\times{/tex} a = 360 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
a = {tex}\frac{1440}{72}{/tex}
a = 20

questions.question: If 36 men can complete a work in 24 days, how many extra men be employed so as to complete the work in 18 days?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the increase in men the number of days to complete the work will decrease.
So, in inverse proportion, the constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
36 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 18
864 = 18a
{tex}\frac{864}{18}{/tex}= a
48 = a
So 48 - 36 = 12 extra men will be needed.

questions.question: A garrison of 300 men had the provision of food for 50 days. A reinforcement of 200 men arrived. Find the number of days for which the food will last.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the increase in men the days the food will last will decrease
So in inverse proportion, the constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
So, 300 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 = (300 + 200) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days the food will last)
15000 = 500 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{15000}{500}{/tex}= a
30 days = a

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 8 Direct and Inverse Proportions CBSE Online Test 02 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: 12 men can dig 8 meters long trench in a day. How many men should be employed for digging 50-meter long trench of the same type in one day?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in depth of digging the number of men will increase
So, in direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{12}{8}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{a}{50}{/tex} (where a is the number of men to be employed)
12 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} a
600 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{600}{8}{/tex}= a
75 men = a

questions.question: In an army camp, there are 320 soldiers. They have sufficient food for 80 days. If after 20 days, 120 soldiers left the camp, for how many more days will the food last?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the decrease in the number of soldiers the day's food lasts will increase
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
320 {tex}\times{/tex} 80 = (320 - 120) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days food will last)
{tex}25600\over200{/tex} = a
128 days = a
The food will last for 128 - 80 = 48 more days

questions.question: A journey by bus takes 45 minutes at 40 km/hour. How fast must a car go to undertake the same journey in 25 minutes?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of  inverse proportion as with the decrease in time interval the speed of car should increase to complete the journey
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
45 {tex}\times{/tex} 40 = 25 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the speed of the car)
{tex}1800\over25{/tex} = a
72 km / hr = a

questions.question: The cost of 5 metres of a particular quality of cloth is Rs 210. Find the cost of 2 metres of cloth of the same type.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in meters of clothes the price increases
In direct proportion, the constant is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{5}{210}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{a}{/tex} (where a is the cost of cloth to be found out)
210 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{420}{5}{/tex} = a
a = Rs 84

questions.question: A mixture of paint is prepared by mixing 1 part of red pigments with 8 parts of the base. How many parts of the base will be used in mixture by mixing 4 part of red pigment?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in the amount of red pigment the amount of base also increases
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
So, {tex}\frac{1}{8}{/tex} = 4a (where a is the parts of the base to be used)
a = 32

questions.question: 6 pipes are required to fill a tank in 1 hour 20 minutes. How long will it take if only 5 pipes of the same type are used?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of pipes the time taken to fill the tank will increase
In inverse proportion, the constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
6 {tex}\times{/tex} 80 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the time taken)[1hour 20min = 80min]
{tex}\frac{480}{5}{/tex} = a
96min = a

questions.question: Two quantities x and y are said to be in ________ if they increase (decrease) together in such a manner that the ratio of their corresponding values remains constant.
questions.explanation: The answer is Direct proportion as in direct proportion two quantities increase or decrease together to keep the ratio of corresponding value constant.

questions.question: Two quantities x and y are said to be in ________ if an increase in x causes a proportional decrease in y (and vice-versa) in such a manner that the product of their corresponding values remains constant.
questions.explanation: The answer is inverse proportion as in inverse proportion the increase in one quantity causes a proportional decrease in another to keep the product of their corresponding value constant.

questions.question: An electric pole, 14 metres high, casts a shadow of 10 metres. Find the height of a tree that casts a shadow of 15 metres under similar conditions.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in height of tree the length of the shadow it casts will increase
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
So, {tex}\frac{14}{10}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{a}{15}{/tex} (where a is the height of the tree)
14 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{210}{10}{/tex} = a
21meters = a

questions.question: A machine in a soft drink factory fills 840 bottles in six hours. How many bottles will it fill in five hours?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with a decrease in time the bottles which will be filled will also decrease
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{840}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{a}{5}{/tex} (where a is the number of bottles filled)
840 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{4200}{6}{/tex} = a
700 bottles = a

questions.question: Suppose 2 kg of sugar contains {tex}9 \times 10^6{/tex} crystals. How many sugar crystals are there in 1.2 kg of sugar?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with increase in the kg of sugar the number of sugar crystals will increase
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by X/Y
{tex}2/9 \times {10^6} = 1.2/a{/tex} ( where a is the number of sugar crystals)
{tex}9 \times {10^6} \times 1.2 = 2 \times a{/tex}
{tex}10.8 \times {10^6}/2 = a{/tex}
{tex}5.4 \times {10^6} = a{/tex}

questions.question: Two persons could fit new windows in a house in 3 days. One of the persons fell ill before the work started. How long would the job take now?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with a decrease in men the time taken to finish the work will increase
In inverse proportion, the constant value is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the time taken to complete the job)
6 days = a

questions.question: The cost of 5 metres of a particular quality of cloth is Rs 210. Find the cost of 4 metres of cloth of the same type.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in meters of cloth the cost will increase
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{5}{210}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{a}{/tex} (where a is the cost of cloth)
210 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{840}{5}{/tex} = a
Rs 168 = a

questions.question: A mixture of paint is prepared by mixing 1 part of red pigments with 8 parts of base. How many parts of base will be used in mixture by mixing 7 part of red pigment?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion, as with the increase in the part of red pigment the part of base will increase.
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{8}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7}{a}{/tex} (where a is the parts of the base to be used in the mixture)
a = 56

questions.question: There are 100 students in a hostel. Food provision for them is for 20 days. How long will these provisions last, if 25 more students join the group?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the increase in the number of students the days foodstuff last will decrease.
In inverse proportion, the constant value is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
100 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = (400 + 25) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days food will last)
2000 = 125 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{2000}{15}{/tex} = a
16days = a

questions.question: If the weight of 12 sheets of thick paper is 40 grams, how many sheets of the same paper would weigh 2.5 kilograms?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with an increase in the number of sheets the weight of the paper will increase.
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{12}{40}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{a}{250}{/tex} (2.5kg = 2500 grams) ( where a is the number of sheets)
12 {tex}\times{/tex} 2500 = 40 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{30000}{40}{/tex} = a
750 sheets = a

questions.question: A photograph of a bacteria enlarged 50,000 times attains a length of 5 cm as shown in the diagram. What is the actual length of the bacteria?
questions.explanation:
Magnification of bacteria attained = 50000
Length attained = 5cm 
Magnification = length attained {tex}\div{/tex} actual length
50000 = {tex}\frac{5 cm}{actual \ length}{/tex}
actual length = {tex}\frac{5 \ cm}{50000}{/tex}5 cm/50000
actual length = {tex}\frac{1}{10000}{/tex}1 cm /10000
actual length = 10-4 cm

questions.question: Rashmi has a road map with a scale of 1 cm representing 18 km. She drives on a road for 72 km. What would be her distance covered in the map?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in map scale the distance covered will increase.
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{18}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{a}{72}{/tex} (where a is the distance in map scale)
{tex}\frac{72}{18}{/tex} = a
4cm = a

questions.question: A contractor estimates that 3 persons could rewire Jasminder’s house in 4 days. If, he uses 4 persons instead of three, how long should they take to complete the job?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of persons the time taken to complete the job will increase
In inverse proportion, the constant value is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days needed to complete the job)
{tex}\frac{12}{4}{/tex} = a
3 days = a

questions.question: Two persons could fit new windows in a house in 3 days. How many persons would be needed to fit the windows in half-day?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the decrease in the number of days the number of persons needed will increase
In inverse proportion, the constant value is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = a {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (where a is the number of persons needed)
12 persons = a

questions.question: The cost of 5 metres of a particular quality of cloth is Rs 210. Find the cost of 10 metres of cloth of the same type.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in meters of cloth, the cost of cloth will also increase.
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{5}{210}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{10}{a}{/tex} (where a is the cost of cloth)
210 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{2100}{5}{/tex} = a
Rs 420 = a

questions.question: A mixture of paint is prepared by mixing 1 part of red pigments with 8 parts of the base. How many parts of the base will be used in mixture by mixing 12 part of red pigment?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in parts of red pigment the parts of base increases
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{8}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{12}{a}{/tex} (where a is the part of base used)
8 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} a
96 parts = a

questions.question: If A can finish a work in n days then part of work finished in 1 day is:
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac { 1 } { n }{/tex}

questions.question: A train is moving at a uniform speed of 75 km/hour. How far will it travel in 20 minutes?
questions.explanation:
Speed of train = 75km/hr
Time taken= 20 mins = {tex}\frac{20}{60}{/tex} hr
Distance travelled = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time
= 75 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{20}{60}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1500}{60}{/tex}
= 25 km

questions.question: A photograph of a bacteria enlarged 50,000 times attains a length of 5 cm as shown in the diagram. If the photograph is enlarged 20,000 times only, what would be its enlarged length?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the decrease in magnification the length enlarged will also decrease.
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{50000}{5}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{20000}{a}{/tex} (where a is the enlarged length)
5 {tex}\times{/tex} 20000 = 50000 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{100000}{50000}{/tex} = a
2cm = a

questions.question: A 5 m 60 cm high vertical pole casts a shadow 3 m 20 cm long. Find at the same time the length of the shadow cast by another pole 10 m 50 cm high.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in height of pole the shadow it casts will also increase.
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{5 m \ 60cm}{3m \ 20cm}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{10m \ 50cm}{a}{/tex} (where a is the length of shadow)
{tex}\frac{560 \ cm}{320 \ cm}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1050 \ cm}{a}{/tex}
320 {tex}\times{/tex} 1050 = 560 {tex}\times{/tex} a
336000 = 560 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}\frac{336000}{560}{/tex} = a
600cm = a
6m = a

questions.question: If amount of work completed by ‘A’ in one day is {tex}\frac { 1 } { n }{/tex}  then the whole work will be finished by 'A' is:
questions.explanation: n days

questions.question: A school has 8 periods a day each of 45 minutes duration. How long would each period be, if the school has 9 periods a day, assuming the number of school hours to be the same?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion because with the increase in the number of periods the duration of each period will decrease to keep the number of school hours the same.
In inverse proportion, the constant value is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
8 {tex}\times{/tex} 45 = 9 {tex}\times{/tex} a ( where a is the length of each period)
360 = 9a
{tex}360\over9{/tex} = a
40min = a

questions.question: The cost of 5 metres of a particular quality of cloth is Rs 210. Find the cost of 13 metres of cloth of the same type.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in meters of cloth the cost of cloth will increase
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}5\over 210{/tex} = {tex}13\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of cloth)
210 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a
2730 = 5a
{tex}2730\over5{/tex} = a
Rs 546 = a

questions.question: A mixture of paint is prepared by mixing 1 part of red pigments with 8 parts of the base. How many parts of the base will be used in mixture by mixing 20 part of red pigment?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in parts of red pigment the parts of the base used in the mixture will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}1\over 8{/tex} = {tex}20\over a{/tex}( where a is the parts of base used in the mixture)
8 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = a
160 = a

tests.name: Direct and Inverse Proportions Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 8 Direct and Inverse Proportions CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: If a box of sweets is divided among 24 children, they will get 5 sweets each. How many would each get, if the number of the children is reduced by 4?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the decrease in the number of children the number of sweets each student gets will increase
In inverse proportion, the constant value is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
24 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = (24 - 4) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of sweets each student gets)
120 = 20a
{tex}120\over 20{/tex} = a
6 sweets = a

questions.question: A train is moving at a uniform speed of 75 km/hour. Find the time required to cover a distance of 250 km.
questions.explanation:
Speed of the train = 75km/hr
Distance = 250km
Time taken = {tex}Distance\over Speed{/tex}
= {tex}250km\over 75km/hr{/tex}
= 3 hrs 20 mins

questions.question: In a model of a ship, the mast is 9 cm high, while the mast of the actual ship is 12 m high. If the length of the ship is 28 m, how long is the model ship is the same ratio is maintained?
questions.explanation:
From the given information with the increase in the length of the ship the mast of ship increases. So it is a case of  direct proportion
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}9\over 12{/tex}={tex}a\over 28{/tex}( where a is the length of the model ship)
{tex}9 \times 28 = a \times 12{/tex}
{tex}252 = a \times 12{/tex}
{tex}252\over12{/tex}= a
21 cm = a

questions.question: A 5 m 60 cm high vertical pole casts a shadow 3 m 20 cm long. Find at the same time the height of a pole which casts a shadow 5m long.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with an increase in the height of pole the shadow it casts will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}5m60cm\over 3m20cm{/tex} = {tex}a\over 5m{/tex}( where a is the height of pole)
{tex}560cm\over 320cm{/tex} = {tex}a\over500cm{/tex}
560 {tex}\times{/tex} 500 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 320
{tex}280000cm\over320{/tex} = a
875cm = a
8m 75cm = a

questions.question: A factory requires 42 machines to produce a given number of articles in 63 days. How many machines would be required to produce the same number of articles in 54 days?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the decrease in the number of days the number of machines required to complete the articles will increase
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
42 {tex}\times{/tex} 63 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 54 (where a is the number of machines required)
2646 = 54a
{tex}2646\over54{/tex} = a
49 machines = a

questions.question: A school has 8 periods a day each of 45 minutes duration. How long would each period be, if the school has 10 periods a day, assuming the number of school hours to be the same?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the increase in the number of periods the length of each period duration will decrease to keep the number of school hours same
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
8 {tex}\times{/tex} 45 = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the duration of each period)
360 = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}360\over 10{/tex} = a
36 mins = a

questions.question: The cost of 5 metres of a particular quality of cloth is Rs 210. Find the cost of 15 metres of cloth of the same type.
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in meters of cloth the cost of cloth will increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}5\over 210{/tex} = {tex}15\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of cloth)
120 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a
3150 = 5a
{tex}3150\over 5{/tex} = a
Rs 630 = a

questions.question: A farmer has enough food to feed 20 animals in his cattle for 6 days. How long would the food last if there were 10 more animals in his cattle?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the increase in the number of animals in the cattle the days food will last will decrease
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
20 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = (20 + 10) {tex}\times{/tex} a ( where a is the number of days food
120 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}120\over30{/tex} = a
4 days = a

questions.question: The scale of a map is given as 1:30000000. Two cities are 4 cm apart on the map. Find the actual distance between them.
questions.explanation:
The scale of the map given is 1:30000000 which means 1cm of map scale equals 30000000 cm in actual distance. So it is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in distance in the map the actual distance will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}1\over 30000000{/tex} = {tex}4\over a{/tex} (where a is the actual distance)
30000000 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = a
120000000 cm = a
1200km = a( 1 km = 1000m = 1000 {tex}\times{/tex} 100cm = 100000 cm)

questions.question: Suppose 2 kg of sugar contains 9 {tex}\times{/tex} 106 crystals. How many sugar crystals are there in 5 kg of sugar?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in kg of sugar the number of sugar crystals will increase
In direct proportion, the constant value is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}2\over 9\times 10^6{/tex} = {tex}5\over a{/tex}( where a is the  number of sugar crystals)
9 {tex}\times{/tex} 106 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}45\times 10^6\over 2{/tex} = a
22.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 106 crystals = a

questions.question: A loaded truck travels 14 km in 25 minutes. If the speed remains the same, how far can it travel in 5 hours?
questions.explanation:
As the speed remains constant so with the increase in time duration the distance travelled will also increase. So it is a case of direct proportion.
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}14km\over25min{/tex} = {tex}a\over 5 hours{/tex} (where a is the distance travelled)
{tex}14km\over 25 min{/tex} = {tex}a\over 5\times 60min{/tex}
25 {tex}\times{/tex} a = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 14
25a = 4200
a = {tex}4200\over 25{/tex}
a = 168 km

questions.question: A car takes 2 hours to reach a destination by travelling at a speed of 60 km/h. How long will it take when the car travels at the speed of 80 km/h?
questions.explanation:
Speed and time are inversely related as with the increase in speed the time taken to cover the distance will decrease.
In inverse proportion, the constant value is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
2 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 80 (where a is the time taken)
{tex}120\over 80{/tex} = a
1.5 hours = a

questions.question: A factory requires 42 machines to produce a given number of articles in 63 days. How many machines would be required to produce the same number of articles in 49 days?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of inverse proportion as with the decrease in the number of days the number of machines required to complete the articles will increase
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
42 {tex}\times{/tex} 63 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 49 (where a is the number of machines required)
2646 = 49a
{tex}2646\over49{/tex} = a
54 machines = a

questions.question: If 9 kg of rice costs Rs 166.50, how much rice can be purchased for Rs 259?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in the quantity of rice the price will increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}9\over 166.50{/tex} = {tex}a\over 259{/tex} ( where a is the amount of rice to be bought)
166.50 {tex}\times{/tex} a = 259 {tex}\times{/tex} 9
a = {tex}2331\over166.50{/tex}
a = 14 kg

questions.question: If a car covers 80 km in 5 litres of petrol, how much distance will it cover in 13 litres of petrol?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with more litres of petrol more distance can be covered.
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}80\over 5{/tex} = {tex}a\over 13{/tex} (where a is the distance to be covered)
5 {tex}\times{/tex} a = 13 {tex}\times{/tex} 80
5a = 1040
a = {tex}1040\over 5{/tex}
a = 208 km

tests.name: Direct and Inverse Proportions Test 05
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 8 Direct and Inverse Proportions CBSE Online Test 05 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: If 32 men can reap a field in 15 days, in how many days can 20 men reap the same field?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of men the number of days needed to reap the field will increase.
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
32 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 20 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days needed to reap the field)
480 = 20 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}480\over20{/tex} = a
24 days = a

questions.question: If 36 men can do a work in 25 days, in how many days will 15 men do the same work?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with decrease in number of men the days needed to complete the work will increase
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
36 {tex}\times{/tex} 25 = 15 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days)
{tex}900\over 15{/tex} = a
60 days = a

questions.question: If 4 kg of potatoes cost Rs 60, what is the cost of 12 kg of potatoes?
questions.explanation:
This is a question of direct proportion as with the increase in the quantity of potatoes the cost will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}4\over 60{/tex} = {tex}12\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of potatoes)
60 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}720\over 4{/tex} = a
Rs 180 = a

questions.question: A tank travels 15 km in 25 minutes. If the speed remains the same, how far can it travel in 1 hour and 5 minutes?
questions.explanation:
As the speed remains same the tank can cover more distance in more time. So it is a case of direct proportion
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}15km\over 25min{/tex} = {tex}a\over 1hour5min{/tex}( where a is the distance travelled)
{tex}15\over25{/tex} = {tex}a\over 65min{/tex}
15 {tex}\times{/tex} 65 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 25
{tex}975\over25{/tex} = a
39 km = a

questions.question: The cost of 5 metres of cloth is Rs 350. Find the cost of 7 metres of cloth.
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in meters of cloth the cost will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}5\over 350{/tex} = {tex}7\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of cloth)
350 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}2450\over5{/tex} = a
Rs 490 = a

questions.question: A 14 metres stick casts a shadow of 10 metres. Find the height of a stick which casts a shadow of 15 metres under similar conditions.
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in height of stick the shadow it casts will be also longer
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}14\over 10{/tex} = {tex}a\over 15{/tex} (where a is the height of stick)
14 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 10
{tex}210\over10{/tex} = a
21 m = a

questions.question: A train is moving with uniform speed of 80 km/hr. How far will it travel in 35 minutes?
questions.explanation:
Uniform speed of train = 80 km/ hr
Time taken to travel = 35 min = {tex}35\over60{/tex} hr
Distance travelled = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time
= 80 km/hr {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{{35}}{{60}}hr{/tex}
= {tex}2800\over60{/tex} km 
= {tex}46\frac{2}{3}{/tex}km

questions.question: 18 workers can build a wall in 26 hours, how many workers will be required to do the same work in 13 hours?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in working hours increase in workers will be needed to build a wall
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
18 {tex}\times{/tex} 26 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 13 (where a is the number of workers)
{tex}468\over13{/tex} = a
36 workers = a

questions.question: There are 100 students in a hostel. The food provision for them is for 15 days. How long will their provision last if 20 more join the group?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the increase in numbers of students the days food will last will decrease
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
100 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = (100 + 20) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days food will last)
1500 = 120 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}1500\over120{/tex} = a
12.5 days = a

questions.question: 12 men can dig a trench in 8 days. How many men can dig the same in 6 days?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with decrease in number of days more men will be needed to complete the work
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
12 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 6 (where a is the number of men needed)
{tex}96\over6{/tex} = a
16 men = a

questions.question: A garrison of 500 men had provision for 24 days. However, a reinforcement of 300 men arrived. How long the food will last?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the increase in the number of men the days food lasts will decrease
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
500 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 = (500 + 300) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days food will last)
12000 = 800 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}12000\over800{/tex} = a
15 days = a

questions.question: At 60 km/hour a car takes 2 hours to reach its destination. How long will the car take if it travels at a speed of 80 km/hr?
questions.explanation:
Speed and time are inversely related as with the increase in speed the time taken to complete the distance will decrease.
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
60 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 80 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the time taken)
{tex}120\over80{/tex} = a
1.5 hours = a

questions.question: The number of bottles made by a factory in 63 days is 315. How many bottles can it make in 82 days?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of days more bottles can be made
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}63\over315{/tex} = {tex}82\over a{/tex} (where a is the number of bottles that can be made)
315 {tex}\times{/tex} 82 = 63 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}25830\over63{/tex} = a
410 bottles = a

questions.question: A road map with a scale of 1 cm represents 36 km. When Ravi drives on a road for 144 km, what would be the distance covered by him on the map?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in actual distance on road the distance  covered on the map will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}1\over36{/tex} = {tex}a\over144{/tex} (where a is the distance covered on map)
36 {tex}\times{/tex} a = 144
a = {tex}144\over36{/tex}
a = 4 cm

questions.question: If a train is moving at a uniform speed of 75 km/hour, find the time required to cover the distance of 300 km.
questions.explanation:
Speed of the train = 75 km/ hr
Distance covered = 300 km
Time taken to cover the distance = {tex}Distance\over Speed{/tex}
= {tex}300km\over 75km/hr{/tex}
= 4 hours
