  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A car can finish a journey in 10 hours at the speed of 48 km/hr. By how much should its speed be increased so that it may take only 8 hours to cover the same distance?
questions.explanation:
Speed and time are inversely related as with a decrease in time interval the speed should increase
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
10 {tex}\times{/tex} 48 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the speed of the car)
{tex}480\over8{/tex} = a
60 km / hr = a
The speed should be increased by (60 - 48) = 12 km/hr

questions.question: Reema types 540 words in half-hour. How many words would she type in 6 minutes?
questions.explanation:
Number of words typed in {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}hour (30 minutes) = 540
Number of words typed in 1 minute = {tex}540\over30{/tex} = 18words
So, number of words typed in 6 minutes = 18 {tex}\times{/tex}6 = 108 words

questions.question: If 56 men can do a piece of work in 42 days, how many men are required to complete it in 14 days?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of days more men will be needed to complete the work
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
56 {tex}\times{/tex} 42 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 14 (where a is the number of men required)
{tex}2352\over14{/tex} = a
168 men = a

questions.question: The scale of a map is given as 1 : 40000000. Two cities are 4 cm apart on the map. Find the actual distance between them.
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with increase in scale in map the actual distance covered will also increase 
In direct proportion the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}1\over 40000000{/tex} = {tex}4cm\over a{/tex} (where a is the actual distance covered)
40000000 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 cm = a
160000000cm = a
1600 km (1 km = 1000 m = 1000 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 cm = 100000 cm)

questions.question: 12 pipes are required to fill a tank in 2 hours 40 minutes. How long will it take if only 10 pipes of the same type are used?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of pipes more time will be taken to fill the tank
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
12 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 hours 40 mins = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the time taken to fill the tank)
12 {tex}\times{/tex} 160 min = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} a (2hr 40min = 160min)
{tex}1920min\over10{/tex} = a
192 min = a
{tex}{192\over60}hr{/tex} = a
3 hours 12 min = a

questions.question: If a box of sweets is divided among 48 children, they will get 7 sweets each. How many would each get, if the number of the children is reduced by 6?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of children each child will get more sweets
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
48 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = (48 - 6) {tex}\times{/tex}a (where a is the number of sweet each child gets)
48 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 42 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}336\over42{/tex} = a
8 sweets = a

questions.question: A batch of bottles was packed in 25 boxes with 12 bottles in each box. If the same batch is packed using 20 bottles in each box, how many boxes would be filled?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the increase in the number of bottles in each box the number of boxes to be filled will decrease
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
25 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 20 (where a is the number of boxes to be filled)
{tex}300\over20{/tex} = a
15 boxes = a

questions.question: A school has 8 periods a day each of 45 minutes duration. How long would each period be, if the school has 9 periods a day, assuming the number of school hours to be same?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the increase in the number of periods the duration of each period will decrease
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
8 {tex}\times{/tex} 45 = 9 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the length of each period)
{tex}360\over9{/tex} = a
40 minutes = a

questions.question: 120 men had food provision for 195 days. How long would the food last if there are 90 men?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of men the number of days food lasts will increase.
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
120 {tex}\times{/tex} 195 = 90 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days food will last)
{tex}23400\over90 {/tex} = a
260 days = a

questions.question: 18 men can reap a field in 35 days. Find the number of men required to reap the same field in 15 days.
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of days more men will be required to reap the field
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
18 {tex}\times{/tex} 35 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 15 (where a is the number of men required)
{tex}630\over15{/tex} = a
42 men = a

questions.question: Working for 8 hours daily, 40 people can dig the foundation of a building in 21 days. Working for 10 hours daily, if the work is to be finished in 14 days, how many people are needed to do the same work?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the increase in the number of working hours fewer people will be needed to dig the foundation.
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
8 {tex}\times{/tex} 40 {tex}\times{/tex} 21 = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} a {tex}\times{/tex} 14 (where a is the number of people needed)
{tex}6720\over140{/tex} = a
48 men = a

questions.question: 6 typists working 5 hours a day can type the manuscript of a book in 16 days. How many days will 4 typists take to do the same job each working 6 hours a day?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of typists more days will be needed to do the same job
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
6 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 16 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days needed)
{tex}480\over24{/tex} = a
20 days = a

questions.question: In an army camp, there are 800 soldiers. There is enough food for them for 60 days. If 400 more soldiers arrive at the camp, how many days will the food last?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the increase in number of soldiers the number of days food will last will decrease
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
800 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 = (800 + 400) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days food will last)
{tex}48000\over1200{/tex} = a
40 days = a

questions.question: A hostel spends Rs. 3200 as the cost of rice for 20 students for 30 days. If the hostel spends Rs. 2400 for 30 students, for how many days, will the rice last?
questions.explanation:
This is  a case of inverse proportion as with the increase in the number of students the number of days food lasts will decrease
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
20 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 = 30 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days rice last)
{tex}600\over30{/tex} = a
20 days = a

questions.question: A train can finish a journey in 10 hours, travelling at a speed of 56 km/h. If another faster train is to cover the same journey in 8 hours, what would be the average speed of the new train?
questions.explanation:
Speed and time are inversely related so with a decrease in time duration the speed has to increase to complete the journey.
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
10 {tex}\times{/tex} 56 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the average speed of the train)
{tex}560\over8{/tex} = a
70 km / hr = a

tests.name: Direct and Inverse Proportions Test 07
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 8 Direct and Inverse Proportions CBSE Online Test 07 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: 10 men working for 6 days mow an area of 5 acres. If there are 8 men working to mow 3 acres of land, how many days will it take?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of men more days will be needed to complete the work
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
10 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} a {tex}\times{/tex} 3 (where a is the number of days)
{tex}300\over24{/tex} = a
12.5 days = a

questions.question: A family of 8 people has enough stock of rice to last for 30 days. Due to the arrival of some relatives, this food was consumed in 20 days. How many guests joined the family?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the increase in more members of the family will decrease the number of days food will last
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
8 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 20 (where a is the number of guests)
{tex}240\over20{/tex} = a
12 members = a
So, the number of extra members who joined the family = 12 - 8 = 4 members

questions.question: A school hostel with 40 children has enough provisions for food to last for 15 days. If 10 children left the hostel, how many days will the provisions last now?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the decrease in the number of children the number of days food will last will increase
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
40 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = (40 - 10) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days food will last)
{tex}600\over30{/tex} = a
20 days = a

questions.question: If the cost of 5 pens is Rs.20, what will be the cost of 9 such pens?
questions.explanation:
This is the case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of pens the cost will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}5\over20{/tex} = {tex}9\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of pens)
20 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 5a
{tex}180\over5{/tex} = a
Rs 36 = a

questions.question: If the cost of 6 pens is Rs.30, what will be the cost of 10 such pens?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of pens the cost will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}6\over 30{/tex} = {tex}10\over a {/tex} (where a is the cost of pens)
30 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}300\over 6{/tex} = a
Rs 50 = a

questions.question: If the cost of 8 cans is Rs.80, what will be the cost of 10 such cans?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of cans the cost will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}8\over80{/tex} = {tex}10\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of cans)
80 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}800\over8{/tex} = a
Rs 100 = a

questions.question: If the cost of 12 apples is Rs.144, what will be the cost of 5 such apples?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with a decrease in the number of apples the cost will also decrease
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}12\over 144{/tex} = {tex}5\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of apples)
144 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}720\over 12{/tex} = a
Rs 60 = a

questions.question: If the cost of 7 oranges is Rs.28, what will be the cost of 12 such oranges?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of oranges the cost will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}7\over28{/tex} = {tex}12\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of oranges)
28 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}336\over 7{/tex} = a
Rs 48 = a

questions.question: If the cost of 9 pencils is Rs.27, what will be the cost of 15 such pencils?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of pencils the cost will also increase.
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}9\over 27{/tex} = {tex}15\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of pencils)
27 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 9 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}405\over 9{/tex} = a
Rs 45 = a

questions.question: If the cost of 11 pens is Rs.121, what will be the cost of 18 such pens?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of pens the cost will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}11\over 121{/tex}= {tex}18\over a{/tex} (where a is the cost of pens)
121 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 11 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}2178\over11{/tex} = a
Rs 198 = a

questions.question: A milk factory can fill 240 bottles in 3 hours. How many bottles will it fill in 5 hours?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in time duration more bottles can be filled.
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}240\over3{/tex} = {tex}a\over5{/tex} (where a is the number of bottles to be filled)
3 {tex}\times{/tex} a = 240 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
a = {tex}1200\over3{/tex}
a = 400 bottles

questions.question: A milk factory can fill 200 bottles in 5 hours. How many bottles will it fill in 7 hours?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of hours more bottles can be filled
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}200\over 5{/tex} = {tex}a\over7{/tex} (where a is the number of bottles)
5 {tex}\times{/tex} a = 200 {tex}\times{/tex} 7
a = {tex}1400\over5{/tex}
a = 280 bottles

questions.question: If the weight of 6 bags is 420 kg, what is the weight of 15 such bags?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of bags the weight will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}6\over420{/tex} = {tex}15\over a{/tex} (where a is the weight of bags)
420 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}6300\over6{/tex} = a
1050 kg = a

questions.question: If the weight of 5 bags is 400 kg, what is the weight of 18 such bags?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in the number of bags the weight will also increase 
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}5\over400{/tex} = {tex}18\over a{/tex} ( where a is the weight of bags)
400 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a
{tex}7200\over5{/tex} = a
1440 kg = a

questions.question: Raja covers a distance of 18 km in 1 hour, how much time will he need to travel a distance of 8 km.
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as fewer distances can be covered in less time
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}                                   
{tex}18\over 1hr{/tex} = {tex}8\over a{/tex} (where a is the time taken )
18a = 8hr
a = {tex}8\over18{/tex} hr
a = 0.44hr
a = 0.44 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 min
a = 26.66 minutes

tests.name: Direct and Inverse Proportions Test 08
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 8 Direct and Inverse Proportions CBSE Online Test 08 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A car is moving at a uniform speed of 75 km/hr. Find the time required to cover a distance of 250 km.
questions.explanation:
Speed of car = 75km/hr
Distance = 250 km
Time taken = {tex}Distance\over Speed{/tex}
= {tex}250\over75{/tex}
= 3 hours 25 minutes

questions.question: In the model of a fort, the flag is 9 m high, however, the flag of the actual ship is 12 m high. If the height of the actual fort is 28 m what is the height of the model fort.
questions.explanation:
This is a case of direct proportion as with the increase in height of fort the height of flag will also increase
In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}X\over Y{/tex}
{tex}9\over12{/tex} = {tex}a\over28{/tex}(where a is the height of model fort)
12  {tex}\times{/tex} a = 28 {tex}\times{/tex} 9
a = {tex}252\over12{/tex}
a = 21m

questions.question: A fort had provisions for food for 300 men over a period of 90 days. After 20 days, 50 men left the fort. How long would the food last at the same rate?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with decrease in number of men the food would last for more days
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
300 {tex}\times{/tex} 90 = ( 300 - 50 ) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days food will last)
{tex}27000\over250{/tex} = a
108 days = a
As 20 days have already passed, so food will last for  108 - 20 = 88 days more.

questions.question: A hostel had provisions for 75 students for 30 days. After 6 days, 15 more students come to the hostel. How long will the remaining provisions last at the same rate?
questions.explanation:
Total number of students = (75 + 15) = 90 students
Number of days left = (30 - 6) days = 24 days
For 75 students the remaining food would be continued for 24 days
For 1 student the remaining food would be continued for (24 {tex}\times{/tex} 75) days
For 90 students the remaining food would be continued for (24 {tex}\times \frac {75}{90}{/tex}) days = (24 {tex}\times \frac {5}{6}{/tex}) days = (4 {tex}\times{/tex} 5) days = 20 days

questions.question: 6 pipes are required to fill a tank in 1 hour 20 min. How long will it take if only 5 pipes of the same type are used?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of pipes the time taken to fill the tank will increase
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
6 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 hr 20 min = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the time taken to fill the tank)
6 {tex}\times{/tex} 80 min = 5a (1hr 20min = 80min)
{tex}480min\over5{/tex} = a
96min = a
{tex}96\over60{/tex}hr = a
1hr 36 min = a

questions.question: If 15 workers can build a wall in 48 hours, how many workers will be required to do the same work in 30 hours?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of working hours more workers will be required to do the work
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
15 {tex}\times{/tex} 48 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 30 (where a is the number of workers required)
{tex}\frac{720}{30}{/tex} = a
24 workers = a

questions.question: If a box of toffees is divided among 24 children, they will get 5 toffees each. How many toffees will each get if the number of children is reduced by 4?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with a decrease in the number of children the number of toffees each child gets will increase
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
24 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = (24 - 4) {tex}\times{/tex} a ( where a is the number of toffees each child gets)
120 = 20a
{tex}120\over20{/tex} = a
6 toffees = a

questions.question: A factory of socks requires 42 machines to produce a given number of socks in 63 days, how many machines are required to produce the same number of socks in 54 days?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with decrease in number of days more machines will be needed to produce the required number of socks
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
42 {tex}\times{/tex} 63 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 4 (where a is the number of machines required)
{tex}2646\over54{/tex} = a
49 machines = a

questions.question: A farmer has enough grains to feed 20 hens for a period of 6 days. How long will the food last if there were 10 more hens with him?
questions.explanation:
This is a case of inverse proportion as with the increase in the number of hens the number of days grains will last will decrease
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
20 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = (20 + 10) {tex}\times{/tex} a (where a is the number of days food will last)
{tex}120\over30{/tex} = a
4 days = a

questions.question: A car takes 2 hours to reach a destination by traveling 60 km/hr. How long will it take when the car travels at the speed of 80 km/hr.
questions.explanation:
Speed and time are inversely related as with the increase in speed the time taken to reach the destination will be less
In inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
2 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 = a {tex}\times{/tex} 80 (where a is the time taken by the car)
{tex}120\over80{/tex} = a
1.5 hours = a

questions.question: Suppose that y varies inversely as x and that y = 8 when x = 3. Form an equation connecting x and y.
questions.explanation:
Since y varies inversely as x, so in inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex}\times{/tex} y
So, x {tex}\times{/tex} y = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 8
xy = 24

questions.question: Suppose that y varies inversely as x and that y = 8 when x = 3. Calculate the value of y when x = 10.
questions.explanation:
As y varies inversely as x. So in inverse proportion, the value of constant is given by x {tex} \times {/tex} y
So, 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 10 {tex}\times{/tex} y 
{tex}\frac{24}{10}{/tex} = y
{tex}\frac{12}{5}{/tex} = y
{tex}2\frac{2}{5}{/tex} = y

questions.question: If y varies directly as x and given y = 9 when x = 5, find the equation connecting x and y.
questions.explanation:
As y varies directly as x. So in direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{9}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{9}{5}{/tex}
y = {tex}\frac{9}{5x}{/tex}

questions.question: If y varies directly as x and given y = 9 when x = 5, find the value of y when x = 15.
questions.explanation:
As y varies directly as x. So in direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{5}{9}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{15}{y}{/tex}
9 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 5y
{tex}\frac{135}{5}{/tex} = y
27 = y

questions.question: If y varies directly as x and given y = 9 when x = 5, find the value of x when y = 6.
questions.explanation:
As y varies directly as x. In direct proportion, the value of constant is given by {tex}\frac{x}{y}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{5}{9}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{x}{6}{/tex}
9x = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
x = {tex}\frac{30}{9}{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{10}{3}{/tex}
x = {tex}3\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Factorise: 12a2b + 15ab2
questions.explanation:
12a2b + 15ab2
by finding commom factor
3ab(4a + 5b)

questions.question: Factorise: 6xy - 4y + 6 - 9x
questions.explanation:
6xy - 4y + 6 - 9x
By grouping
6xy - 4y - 9x  + 6
2y (3x - 2) -3 (3x -2)
(3x - 2)(2y - 3)

questions.question: Factorise: x2 + 8x + 16
questions.explanation:
x2 + 8x + 16
By substituting, we get,
= x2 + 4x + 4x + 16
By grouping
= x(x + 4) + 4(x + 4)
= (x + 4)(x + 4)
= (x + 4)2

questions.question: Solve: -20x4 {tex}\div{/tex} 10x2
questions.explanation:
-20x4 {tex}\div{/tex} 10x2
{tex}\frac{-20x^4}{10x^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{-20}{10}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{x^4}{x^2}{/tex}
-2 {tex}\times{/tex} x4 - 2
-2x2

questions.question: Find the common factors of 12x, 36.
questions.explanation:
the common factors of 12x, 36
12x = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} x
36 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
common factors are = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
= 12

questions.question: When we factorise an expression, we write it as a ________ of factors.
questions.explanation: A factor is a number or quantity that when multiplied with another produces a given number or expression.

questions.question: Factorise: a4 - b4
questions.explanation:
a4 - b4
= [(a)2]2 - [(b)2]2
by using property (a2 - b2)
= (a2 - b2)(a2 + b2)
By factorise
= (a + b)(a - b)(a2 + b2)

questions.question: Factorise: p4 - 81
questions.explanation:
p2 - 81
= [(p)2]2 - [(3)2]2
by using property (a2 - b2)
= (p2 - 32)(p2 + 32)
By factorise
= (p - 3)(p + 3)(p2 + 32)
= (p - 3)(p + 3)(p2 + 9)

questions.question: Divide the given polynomial by the given monomial: (5x2 - 6x) {tex}\div{/tex} 3x
questions.explanation:
(5x2 - 6x) {tex}\div{/tex} 3x
{tex}\frac{5x^2 - 6x}{3x}{/tex}
x{tex}\frac{5x - 6}{3x}{/tex}
by divide
{tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}(5x - 6)

questions.question: Divide the given polynomial by the given monomial: (3y8 - 4y6 + 5y4) {tex}\div{/tex} y4
questions.explanation:
(3y8 - 4y6 + 5y4) {tex}\div{/tex} y4
{tex}\frac{(3y^4 - 4y^2 + 5)}{y^4}{/tex} (by findig common factor)
by cancelling y4
(3y4 - 4y2 + 5)

questions.question: Divide as directed: 5(2x + 1) (3x + 5) {tex} \div {/tex} (2x + 1)
questions.explanation:
5(2x + 1) (3x + 5) {tex} \div {/tex} (2x + 1)
5(2x + 1) (3x + 5) / (2x + 1)
By cancelling (2x + 1)
5(3x + 5)

questions.question: Divide as directed: 26xy (x + 5) (y - 4) {tex} \div {/tex} 13x (y - 4)
questions.explanation:
26xy (x + 5) (y - 4) {tex} \div {/tex} 13x (y - 4)
{tex}\frac{26xy (x + 5) (y - 4)}{13x (y - 4)}{/tex}
by division
2y(x + 5) [26xy (x + 5) (y – 4) / 13x (y – 4)]

questions.question: Find and correct the errors in the following mathematical statements. 4(x - 5) = 4x - 5
questions.explanation:
4 (x - 5) = 4x - 5 is not correct
4 will be multiplied by both x and 5
4 (x - 5) = 4x - 20

questions.question: Find and correct the errors in the following mathematical statements. x(3x + 2) = 3x2 + 2
questions.explanation:
x(3x + 2) = 3x2 + 2 is not correct
x will be multiplied by both3 x and 2
x(3x + 2) = 3x2 + 2x

10x2 - 18x3 + 14x4
by finding commonn factor
2x2(5 - 9x + 7x2)
in suitable arrengnment

questions.question: Factorise: x2 + xy + 8x + 8y
questions.explanation:
x2 + xy + 8x + 8y
By grouping
= x(x + y) +8(x + y)
= (x + 8)(x + y)

questions.question: Factorise: 4y2 - 12y + 9
questions.explanation:
4y2 - 12y + 9
By middle split term
= 4y2 - 6y - 6y + 9
By grouping
= 2y(2y - 3) - (2y - 3)
= (2y - 3)(2y - 3)
= (2y - 3)2

questions.question: Solve: 7x2y2z2 {tex}\div{/tex} 14xyz

{tex}\frac{7x^2y^2z^2}{14xyz}{/tex}
by division

questions.question: Find the common factors of 2y, 22xy.
questions.explanation:
Factors of 2y ad 22xy are
2y = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} y
22xy = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 11 {tex}\times{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} y
the common factors of 2y, 22xy are 2 {tex}\times{/tex} y = 2y

questions.question: The ________ may be numbers, algebraic variables or algebraic expressions.
questions.explanation: A factor of a number or quantity that when multiplied with another produces a given number or expression.

questions.question: Factorise: x4 - (y + z)4

options.html: (x - y - z)(x + y + z){tex}\left[ {{x^{{2}}} + {{ }}{{\left( {y + z} \right)}^{{2}}}} \right]{/tex}

questions.explanation:
x4 - (y + z)4
= ((x)2)2 - ((y)2)2
= [(x)2 + (y + z)2][(x)2 - (y + z)2]
= (x - y - z)(x + y + z){tex}\left[ {{x^{{2}}} + {{ }}{{\left( {y + z} \right)}^{{2}}}} \right]{/tex}

questions.question: Which of the following is the common factor of 21 x2y and 35 xy2?
questions.explanation: 7xy

questions.question: Divide the given polynomial by the given monomial: 8(x3y2z2 + x2y3z2 + x2y2z3) {tex}\div{/tex} 4x2y2z2
questions.explanation:
8(x3y2z2 + x2y3z2 + x2y2z3) {tex}\div{/tex} 4x2y2z2
= {tex}\frac{8(x^3y^2z^2 + x^2y^3z^2 + x^2y^2z^3)}{4x^2y^2z^2}{/tex}
= 8x3y2z2{tex}\frac{(x + y + z)}{4x^2y^2z^2}{/tex}
= 2(x + y + z)

questions.question: Which of the following is quotient obtained on dividing –18 xyz2 by –3 xz?
questions.explanation: 6 Yz

= {tex}\frac{52pqr(p + q)(q + r)(r + p)}{104pq(q + r)(r + p)}{/tex}
By cancelling
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}r(p + q){/tex}

questions.question: Divide as directed: 20(y + 4)(y2 + 5y + 3) {tex}\div{/tex} 5(y + 4)

20(y + 4)(y2 + 5y + 3) {tex}\div{/tex} 5(y + 4)
by dividing
= {tex}\frac{20(y + 4)(y^2 + 5y + 3)}{5(y + 4)}{/tex}
by dividing
4(y2 + 5y + 3)

questions.explanation:
24xy2z3 by 6yz2
= {tex}\frac{24xy^2z^3}{6yz^3}{/tex}
by dividing
= 4xyz

questions.question: Find the common factors of 14pq, 28p2q2.
questions.explanation:
14 pq = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} p {tex}\times{/tex} q
28p2q2 =2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} p {tex}\times{/tex} p {tex}\times{/tex} q {tex}\times{/tex} q
common factors are 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} p {tex}\times{/tex} q = 14pq

questions.question: An irreducible factor is a factor which cannot be expressed further as a ________ of factors.
questions.explanation: We know an irreducible factor is the product of the factors.

questions.question: Factorise: x4 - (x - z)4
questions.explanation:
x4 - (x - z)4
(x2)2 - ((x - z)2)2
= (x2 + (x - z)2)(x2 - (x - z)2)
= (x2 + x2 + 2xz + z2)(x + x - z)(x - x + z)
= z(2x - z)(2x2 - 2xz + z2)

questions.question: Factorise: a4 - 2a2b2 + b4
questions.explanation:
a4 - 2a2b2 + b4
= (a2)2 - 2a2b2 + (b2)2
= (a2 - b2)2
= (a - b)2(a + b)2

questions.question: Divide the given polynomial by the given monomial: (x3 + 2x2 + 3x) {tex}\div{/tex} 2x
questions.explanation:
(x3 + 2x2 + 3x) {tex}\div{/tex} 2x
{tex}\frac{x(x^2 + 2x + 3)}{2x}{/tex}
by division
{tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(x2 + 2x + 3)

questions.question: Divide the given polynomial by the given monomial: (p3q6 - p6q3) {tex}\div{/tex} p3q3
questions.explanation:
(p3q6 - p6q3) {tex}\div{/tex} p3q3
{tex}\frac{p^3q^6(p^6 - q^3)}{p^3q^3}{/tex}
by dividing
p3 q3 (q3 - p3) / p3 q3
q3 - p3

questions.question: Divide as directed: x(x + 1)(x + 2)(x + 3) {tex}\div{/tex} x(x + 1)
questions.explanation:
x(x + 1)(x + 2)(x + 3) {tex}\div{/tex} x(x + 1)
by division
= {tex}\frac{x(x + 1)(x + 2)(x + 3)}{x(x + 1)}{/tex}
= (x + 2)(x + 3)

questions.question: Divide as directed: (y2 + 7y + 10) {tex} \div {/tex} (y + 5)
questions.explanation:
(y2 + 7y + 10) {tex} \div {/tex} (y + 5)
= {tex}\frac{(y^2 + 7y + 10)}{(y + 5)}{/tex}
by the middle split term
= {tex}\frac{(y^2 + 5y + 2y + 10)}{(y + 5)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{y(y + 5)+ 2(y + 5)}{(y + 5)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(y + 5)(y + 2)}{(y + 5)}{/tex}
= (y + 2)

questions.question: Find and correct the errors in the mathematical statements. 5y + 2y + y – 7y = 0
questions.explanation:
5y + 2y + y – 7y = 0 is an incorrect statement
(5 + 2+ 1  – 7)  y 
(8 - 7) y
1y
therefore 5y + 2y + y – 7y = y

questions.question: Find and correct the errors in the mathematical statements. 3x + 2x = 5x2
questions.explanation:
3x + 2x = 5x2 is an incorrect statement
in addition, variables will not multiplied
3x + 2x = 5x

questions.question: Factorise: 22y - 33z
questions.explanation:
22y - 33z
by finding common factors
11 (2y - 3z)

questions.question: Factorise: ax + bx - ay - by
questions.explanation:
ax + bx - ay - by
By grouping
= ax + bx - ay - by
= x(a + b) -y(a + b)
= (a + b)(x - y)

questions.question: Factorise: a2 - 2ab + b2 - c2
questions.explanation:
a2 - 2ab + b2 - c2
= (a - b)2 - c2 by using property a2 - b2
= (a - b - c)(a - b + c)

questions.question: Find the common factors of 2x, 3x2 and 4.
questions.explanation: The common factors of 2x, 3x2 and 4 are 1 as all the numbers are not factors of each other.

questions.question: In factorisation by regrouping, we should remember that any regrouping (i.e., rearrangement) of the terms in the given expression may not lead to ________.
questions.explanation: In mathematics, factorization (or factoring is the decomposition of an object (for example, a number, a polynomial, or a matrix) into a product of other objects, or factors, which when multiplied together give the original.

questions.question: Factorise: p2 + 6p + 8
questions.explanation:
p2 + 6p + 8
by middle split term
p2 + 4p + 2p +8
p(p + 4) + 2(p + 4)
(p + 2)(p + 4)

questions.question: Factorise: {tex}{q^{\text{2}}}-{\text{ 1}}0q + {\text{ 21}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}{q^{\text{2}}}-{\text{ 1}}0q + {\text{ 21}}{/tex}
q2 - 7q - 3q + 21
q (q - 7 ) -3 (q - 7 )
(q - 3 ) (q - 7 )

questions.question: Divide: (10x - 2x) {tex} \div {/tex} 5
questions.explanation:
(10x - 2x) {tex} \div {/tex} 5
By finding a common factor
= {tex}\frac{5(2x - 5)}{5}{/tex}
By dividing
= 2x - 5

questions.question: Divide: (10x - 25) {tex} \div {/tex} (2x - 5)
questions.explanation:
(10x - 25) {tex} \div {/tex} (2x - 5)
By finding a common factor
= {tex}\frac{5(2x - 5)}{(2x - 5)}{/tex}
by dividing
= 5

questions.question: Divide as directed: (m2 - 14m - 32) {tex}\div{/tex} (m + 2)
questions.explanation:
(m2 - 14m - 32) {tex}\div{/tex} (m + 2)
By the middle split term
= {tex}\frac{m^2 - 16m + 2m - 32}{m + 2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{m(m - 16) + 2(m - 16)}{m + 2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(m - 16)(m + 2)}{m + 2}{/tex}
= (m - 16)

questions.question: Divide as directed: (5p2 - 25p + 20) {tex}\div{/tex} (p - 1)
questions.explanation:
(5p2 - 25p + 20) {tex}\div{/tex} (p - 1)
by middle split term
= {tex}\frac{(5p^2 - 20p - 5p + 20)}{(p - 1)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{5p(p - 4) - 5(p - 4)}{(p - 1)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(5(p - 1)(p - 4))}{(p - 1)}{/tex}
= 5(p - 4)

questions.question: Find and correct the errors in the following mathematical statements. (2x)2 + 4(2x) + 7 = 2x2 + 8x + 7

(2x)2 + 4(2x) + 7 = 4x2 + 8x + 7

questions.question: Find the common factors of 6abc, 24ab2 and 12a2b
questions.explanation:
6abc, 24ab2 and 12a2b
6abc = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} a {tex}\times{/tex} b {tex}\times{/tex} c

questions.question: Factorise: q2 - 10q + 21

questions.explanation:

By dividing

questions.question: Divide: 9x2y2(3z - 24) {tex}\div{/tex} 27xy(z - 8)
questions.explanation:
9x2y2(3z - 24) {tex}\div{/tex} 27xy(z - 8)
= {tex}\frac{27x^2y^2(z - 8)}{27xy(z - 8)}{/tex}
by cancelling (z - 8) both sides
= {tex}\frac{27x^2y^2}{27xy}{/tex}
= xy

questions.question: Divide as directed: 4yz(z2 + 6z - 16) {tex}\div{/tex} 2y(z + 8)
questions.explanation:
4yz(z2 + 6z - 16) {tex}\div{/tex} 2y(z + 8)
= {tex}\frac{4yz(z^2 + 8z - 2z - 16)}{2y(z + 8)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4yz(z(z + 8) - 2(z + 16)}{2y(z + 8)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4yz(z + 8)(z - 2)}{2y(z + 8)}{/tex}
by cancelling (z + 8) both sides
= {tex}\frac{4yz(z - 2)}{2y}{/tex}
= 2z(z - 2)

questions.question: Divide as directed: 5pq(p2 - q2) {tex}\div{/tex} 2p(p + q)
questions.explanation:
5pq(p2 - q2) {tex}\div{/tex} 2p(p + q)
= {tex}\frac{5pq(p + q)(p - q)(p + q)}{2p(p + q)}{/tex}
by cancelling (p + q) both sides
= {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}q(p - q)

= 2y(1 + 4y2)(1 - 4y2)
= 2y(1 + 4y2)(1 - 2y)(1 + 2y)

questions.explanation: The point P (-8, 1) lie in 2nd quadrant as the X value is negative and Y value is positive.

tests.name: Introduction to Graphs Test 03

General Instructions:

questions.question:
Given in the table the cost of a kind of fruit juice, use the graph to find how much of juice we can purchase for Rs 900.

Quantity of juice (in litre) 5 10 15 20
Cost (in Rs) 500 1000 1500 2000
questions.explanation:
Analysing the graph you can find out the ratio of cost vs ltr of juice as
Cost / juice = {tex}\frac{500}{5}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1000}{10}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1500}{15}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2000}{20}{/tex} = 100. So Juice cost is Rs.100 per ltr.
So we can buy 9 ltr of juice by Rs. 900 i.s {tex}\frac{900}{100}{/tex} = 9 ltrs

questions.question:
Given in the table the cost of math books, find the cost of 7 books by using a graph.

Number of books 2 4 6 8 10
Cost (in Rs) 100 200 300 400 500
questions.explanation:
As per the data given, cost /no. of books = {tex}\frac{100}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{200}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{300}{6}{/tex} = 50 .
Hence, the cost of 1 book is Rs.50.
So for  7 books cost will be 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 50 = 350

questions.question:
Shown in the table the interest s on some deposits for a year, use the graph to find that how much money should be deposited to get an interest of Rs 100

Amount deposited (in Rs) 1000 2000 3000 4000 5000
Simple interest (in Rs) 40 80 120 160 200
questions.explanation:
As per the data given, Amount deposited / interest = {tex}\frac{1000}{40}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2000}{80}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3000}{120}{/tex} = 25
Hence we have to deposit Rs. 25 for getting interest for each Rs.1.
So for getting Interested in Rs.100 we have to deposit 100 {tex}\times{/tex} 25 = 2500

questions.question:
Represented in the table the distance covered by a person on a scooter moving continuously by the speed of 30 km /hr, use the graph to find the distance covered in {tex}2\frac{1}{2}{/tex} hours.

Time (in hours) 1 2 3 4 5
distance (in km) 30 60 90 120 150
questions.explanation:
The scooter moves in 1 hr = 36 km
So, It can move in 2.5hr = 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 = 75 km
As the graph shown scooter moves 60 km in 2 hr and 90 km in 3 hr. So it will be in the middle of 60 and 90 point i.e. 75 Km for 2.5 hr

questions.question:
Shown in the table the distance covered by a bike rider with a continuous speed of 40 km /hr, use the graph to find the time to cover the distance of 140 km.

Time (in hours) 1 2 3 4 5
distance (in km) 40 80 120 160 200
questions.explanation:
As per the data, the bike rider covers each 40 km per hour. So he will travel  140 km in {tex}\frac{140}{40}{/tex} = 3.5 hr
As in graph we can see at 3 hr bike covers 120 km and at 4 hr it covers 160 hr. So to cover 140 Km it would be in between 3 and 4 hr, i.e 3.5 hr

questions.question:
Shown in the table side of the square and its perimeter, find the perimeter of the square whose side is 4.5 cm using a graph.

Side of a square (in cm) 1 2 3 4 5
Perimeter (in cm) 4 8 12 16 20
questions.explanation:
the ratio of the perimeter of the square to side = perimeter/side = {tex}\frac{4}{1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{8}{2}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{12}{3}{/tex} = 4
Hence perimeter = 4 {tex} \times {/tex} side . as we can see in graph perimeter 16 when the side is 4 and perimeter is 20 when the side is 5. So for side 4.5 and perimeter will be in the middle of 16 and 20, i.e. 18.  Or, the perimeter of side 4.5 cm  id 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4.5 = 18 cm

questions.question: If the 4-digit number 2X Y7 is exactly divisible by 3, then which of the following is the least value of (X + Y)?
What is the coordinates of point E?
questions.explanation: coordinates of point E is  (5, -9) as the point touches 5 value right from 0 and 9 value down from 0.

questions.question: What is the coordinates of point F?
questions.explanation: coordinates of point F is  (5, -4) as the point touches 5 value right from 0 and 4 value down from 0.

questions.question: What is the coordinates of point G?
questions.explanation: coordinates of point G is  (10,4 ) as the point touches 10 value right from 0 and 4 value top from 0.

questions.question: What is the coordinate of point H.
questions.explanation: coordinates of point H is (0,0) as the point is at 0 point so X = 0 and also Y=0

questions.question: What is the coordinates of point I?
questions.explanation:
coordinates of point I is (3 , 4)
To locate point I move 3 units right on the X axis and 4 units up on the Y axis
The X - Coordinate always comes first , followed by the Y - coordinate.

questions.question: What is the coordinates of point J?
questions.explanation: coordinates of point J is  (-3, -10) as the point touches 3 value left from 0 and 10 value down from 0.

questions.question: What is the coordinates of point K.
questions.explanation: coordinates of point N is  (-7,2)) as it touhes 7 point from 0 following X and 2 point from 0 following Y. And as the point is in 2nd quadrant so  X  will be negative and Y value will be positive

questions.question: What is the coordinates of point L.
questions.explanation:
coordinates of point L is  (-7,-7) as it touhes 7 point from 0 following X axis and 7 point from 0 following Y axis. And as the point is in 3rd quadrant so both X and Y value will be negative.
Or as the X point is left side of 0 so it is negative X and Y point is down side from 0 so it is negative Y.

questions.question: What is the coordinates of point M.
questions.explanation: coordinates of point M is (10, 2) as it touhes 10 points from 0 following X axis and 2 point from 0 following Y axis. And as the point is in 1st quadrant so both X and Y value will be positive. Or as X point is on the right side of 0 so it is positive X and Y point is top side of 0 so it is also positive Y

questions.question: What is the coordinates of point N.
questions.explanation: coordinates of point N is  (-3, -8 ) as it touhes 3 point from 0 following X axis and 8 point from 0 following Y axis. And as the point is in 3rd quadrant so both X and Y value will be negative. Or as the X point is left side of 0 so it is negative X and Y point is down side from 0 so it is negative Y

questions.question: On which axis does the point (0, -8) lie?
questions.explanation: The point (0, -8) lie on Y-axis as the X value is 0 of the point.

questions.question: On which axis does the point (4, 0) lie?
questions.explanation: Here x = 4, y = 0 (origin) so point (4,0) lies on x-axis.

questions.question: On which axis does the point (0, 10) lie?
questions.explanation: Here x = 0 (origin), y = 10. So point (0,10) lies on y-axis.

questions.question: On which axis does the point (2, 0) lie?
questions.explanation: Here x = 2 and y = 0 (origin). So point (2,0) lies on x -axis.

questions.question: If M is a number such that M ÷ 5 gives a remainder of 1, then which of the following is the one’s digit of M?
Substitute digits for the letter N to make the following Addition problem true.
questions.explanation: On adding 7 and 5 you get 12. One gets carried over to tens place. 8 and 1 make 9. Answer shows 5. Only 6 added to 9 would give 15.

questions.question: Substituting digits for the letter A to make the following Addition problem true.
questions.explanation: On adding 5 8 7 we get 20. 2 gets carried over. 2 3 9 8 added gives 22.

questions.question: Substitute digits for the letter A to make the following Multiplication problem true.
questions.explanation: 4 times A is 8 means A can be 2 or 7. A times A in second step is 4. Thus A is 2.

questions.question: Substitute digits for the letters A and B to make the following Multiplication problem true.
questions.explanation:
In the solution when we add 7+7+2 we get A which is equal to 16 so A= 6 and 1 will be the carry over.  Thus A is 6.
Now in first step the multiplication of B and  A which is equal to 6 is giving a number whose ones digit is B again. This is possible when
B = 2, 4, 6 or 8.
In case B = 4, the product {tex}B\times6=\;4\times6\;=\;24,{/tex}now 2 will be carry over for the next step 
we have {tex}4\times7+2=30{/tex} , hence the zero in between 7 and B and 3 will be carry over for the next step
now we have {tex}4\times1+3=7{/tex}
so value of B= 4

questions.question: Substitute digits for the letters P and Q to make the following Multiplication problem true.
questions.explanation:
In the solution when we add P + Q = P that means  Q = 0
now {tex}3\times P{/tex} gives the number whose one's digit is also P this is possible only when P = 5
Therefore, the value of P = 5 and Q = 0

questions.question: By which of the following number 225 is divisible? 2, 3, 4, and 6
questions.explanation: It's digit sum = 9. So, it is divisible by 3

questions.question: By which of the following number 168 is divisible? 2, 4, 6, and 10
questions.explanation: Divisible by 2 because it is even. Divisible by 4 as 68 is divisible by 4. Divisible by 6 as divisible by 2 and 3 both. Not divisible by 10 as no zero in units place.

questions.question: The number 477 is divisible by each of the following numbers except: 3, 6, 9
questions.explanation: 4 + 7 + 7 = 18. Divisible by 9 and 3. Number ending in 7 cannot be divisible by 6.

questions.question: If a number is divisible by 9, then it is also divisible by which number?
questions.explanation: 3 is a factor of 9.

questions.question: Which number is divisible by 8?
questions.explanation: Last 3 digits divisible by 8.

questions.question: By which of the following number 9042 is not divisible? 2, 3, 6, and 9
questions.explanation: Sum of digits not divisible by 9.

questions.question: Which of the numbers is divisible by 10?
questions.explanation: Because it ends in digit zero.

questions.question: Identify the missing digit in the number 234,4_6, if the number is divisible by 4.
questions.explanation: Last two digits number must be divisible by 4. Only 1 3 5 7 9 can be possible.

questions.question: Determine if 48 is divisible by 2, 3 and 5.
questions.explanation: By 2 because even. By 3 as 4 + 8 = 12 divisible by 3. Not by 5 as does not end in zero.

questions.question: Which of these numbers is divisible by 6?
questions.explanation: Divisible by 2 and 3 both.

questions.question: Write in generalised form: 25
questions.explanation:
Here tens digit is 2 and unit digit is 5
So, the generalised form is the tens digit {tex}\times{/tex} 10 + unit digit
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 + 5

questions.question: Write in the usual form: 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 5 + 6
questions.explanation:
10 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 50
50 + 6 = 56

questions.question: If the division N {tex} \div {/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 3, what might be the ones digit of N?
questions.explanation: Any digit is completely divided by 5 if it's unit digit is 5 or 0 If remainder is 3 then the unit digit must be either 0 + 3 = 3 or 5 + 3 = 8

questions.question: If the division N {tex} \div {/tex}2 leaves a remainder of 1, what might be the one’s digit of N?

options.html: The one’s digit must be 1, 3, 5, 7 or 9.
The one’s digit must be 0, 2, 4, 6 or 8.
The one’s digit must be 5 or 0.

questions.explanation: Only odd numbers on division by 2 leave remainder 1.

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in following :-
questions.explanation: 1 + B is 8 so B = 7. B + A gives 1 in units digit. Thus A has to be 4.

questions.question: The number 21436587 is divisible by ________.
questions.explanation: Sum of digits is divisible by 9.

questions.question: Write in the generalised form: 73
questions.explanation:
The generalised form of any two-digit number is
10 {tex}\times{/tex} tens digit + ones digit
i.e. 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 + 3

questions.question: Write in the usual form: 100 {tex} \times {/tex} 7 + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 1 + 8
questions.explanation:
100 {tex}\times{/tex} + 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 + 8
700 + 10 + 8
718

questions.question: If the division N {tex}\div{/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 1, what might be the one’s digit of N?
questions.explanation:
A number is divisible by 5 when its ones digit is 0 or 5.
so if a division N {tex}\div{/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 1 then the digit on ones place might be 5 + 1 = 6

questions.question: If the division N {tex} \div {/tex} 2 leaves no remainder (i.e., zero remainder), what might be the one’s digit of N?

options.html: The one’s digit must be 1, 3, 5, 7 or 9.
The one’s digit must be 0, 2, 4, 6 or 8.
The one’s digit must be 5 or 0.

questions.explanation: Only the even numbers divided by 2, don't leave the remainder. Therefore, N maybe 0, 2, 4, 6, 8.

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in following:-
questions.explanation:
When A is added to B, it gives 9 that is a number whose ones place is 9.Sum can be 9 only as summation of two single digits cannot be 19. So no carry generated.
In the next step, A + 2 = 0
It is possible if A = 8.
Therefore, 2 + 8 = 10 and 1 will be carried forward for the next step.
1 + 1 + 6 = 8. Therefore, value of A = 8.
When A is added to B, it gives 9.
A + B = 9
8 + B = 9
Therefore, value of B = 1
{tex}\begin{gathered} \underline {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\;128} \\ { + 681} \end{array}} \hfill \\ 809 \hfill \\ \end{gathered} {/tex}

Thus value of A and B is 8 and 1 respectively.

questions.question: Write in generalised form: 85
questions.explanation:
The general form of a two digit number is 10 {tex}\times{/tex} a + 1 {tex}\times{/tex} b
Therefore, 85 = 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 8 + 5

questions.question: Write in the usual form: 100 {tex} \times {/tex} a + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} c + b
questions.explanation: As a is multiplied by 100, c is multiplied by 10 and b by 1. Therefore a, c, b will come at hundreds, tens and ones place respectively. So, the number is acb.

questions.question: If the division N {tex} \div {/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 4, what might be the one’s digit of N?
questions.explanation: We know for a number to be divisible by 5 should have 0 or 5 at ones place. If the remainder is 4 than the ones digit of N must be either 0 + 4 = 4 or 5 + 4 = 9. Therefore, the answer is either 4 or 9.

questions.question: Suppose that the division N {tex} \div {/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 4, and the division N {tex} \div {/tex} 2 leaves a remainder of 1. What must be the one’s digit of N?
questions.explanation:
If N {tex}\div{/tex} 5 leaves remainder 4, the ones digit of N should be 4 or 9. 
If N {tex}\div{/tex} 2 leaves remainder 1, the ones digit of N should be 1, 3, 5, 7 or 9.
The common digit in both cases is 9. 
Therefore, ones digit of N is 9.

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in following :
questions.explanation:
When 3is multiplied with B it gives a number whose onesplace is B again. So, B must be 5 or 0.
Let B = 5
First step: 5 × 3 = 15
1 will be carried forward. Therefore, (A × 3) + 1 = CA. This is not possible for any number.
Therefore, value of B has to be 0 only.
If B = 0, then there will be no carry. So we get A × 3 = CA. 
When a number is multiplied with 3, its ones place should be the number itself. That is possible only for A = 0 or 5. But A cannot be 0 as it has to be two digit numbers. Therefore, the value of A is 5. Thus we get the following
{tex}\begin{gathered} \underline {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {50} \\ { \times 3} \end{array}} \hfill \\ 150 \hfill \\ \end{gathered} {/tex}
The value of A,  B and C is 5, 0 and 1 respectively.

questions.question: Find Q in the addition.
questions.explanation: The addition of Q and 3 is giving 1i.e, a number who's ones  digit is 1 which is possible if Q = 8. Now 8 + 3 = 11. Therefore,  1 + 1 + Q = 0 i. e, a number whose ones digit is 0 whicheans the number should be 10. So, 1 + 1 + Q = 10. Therefore, the value of Q = 8.

questions.question: The number 2146587 is divisible by ________.
questions.explanation: Sum of digits of 2146587 = 2 + 1 + 4 + 6 + 5 + 8 + 7 = 33,  which is divisible by 3. Therefore,  2146587 is divisible by 3.

questions.question: Write in the generalised form: 128
questions.explanation:
General form of a three digit number is 100 {tex} \times {/tex} a + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} b + 1 {tex} \times {/tex} c
Therefore, general form of 128 = 100 {tex} \times {/tex} 1 + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 2 + 1 {tex} \times {/tex} 8

questions.question: Write in the usual form: 100 {tex} \times {/tex} 7 + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 5 + 6
questions.explanation: 100 {tex} \times {/tex} 7 + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 5 + 6 = 700 + 50 + 6 = 756

questions.question: If the division N {tex} \div {/tex} 5 leaves a remainder of 0, what might be the one’s digit of N?
questions.explanation: If a number is completely divisible by 5, it should have either 0 or 5 at its ones digit.

questions.question: If 21y5 is a multiple of 9, where y is a digit, what is the value of y?
questions.explanation:
If a number is a multiple of 9, the sum of its digits must be divisible by 9. 
Sum of digits of 21y5 = 2 + 1 + y + 5 = 8 + y. 
Hence, 8 + y should be a multiple of 9. 
Next multiple of 9 is 9. So, 8 + y = 9
So, the value of y = 1.

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in following:
questions.explanation:
When 5 is multiplied with B it gives a number whose ones place is B again. So, B must be 5 or 0.
Let B = 5
First step: 5 × B = 5 × 5 = 25
2 will be carried forward. Therefore, (A × 5) + 2 = CA. This is possible for number A = 2 or 7.
The multiplication is as given below.
25 × 5 =125
75 × 5 = 375
Let B = 0
First step: 5 × B = 5
5 × 0 = 0
There will not be any carry in this case.
In the next step, 5 × A = CA
This can happen only when the value of A is 5 or 0.
However, A cannot be 0 as AB is two digit number. Therefore, the value of A is 5.
50 ×   5 = 250
Therefore, the value of A,  B and C are 5, 0 and 2 respectively.

questions.question: Find A and B in the addition. A + A + A = BA
questions.explanation:
Here,  A + A + A = BA as the sum of 3 ones digit numbers is a two-digit number so the value of A will be greater than 3. 
Putting the value of A = 4,
4 + 4 + 4 = 12 which do not satisfy the equation. 
Putting the value of A = 5, 
5 + 5 + 5 = 15, which satisfies the equation. 
Therefore, A= 5 and B = 1.

questions.question: Write in generalised form: 425
questions.explanation:
General form of a three digit number is 100 {tex} \times {/tex} a + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} b + 1 {tex} \times {/tex} c
Therefore, 425 will be written as 100 {tex} \times {/tex} 4 + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 2 + 5

questions.question: Write in the usual form: 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 6 + 7
questions.explanation: 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 6 + 7 = 60 + 7 = 67

questions.question: If 31z5 is a multiple of 9, where z is a digit, what is the value of z?
questions.explanation: For a number to be multiple of 9, the sum of its digit must be divisible by 9. Here,  3 + 1 + 5 = 9. So,  to make 31z5 divisible by 9 we will add either 0 or 9. Therefore, the value of z is either 0 or 9.

questions.question: Find the values of the letters in the following:
questions.explanation:
When 6 is multiplied with B, it gives a number whose ones place is B. It is possible only if B = 0, 2, 4, 6 or 8.
B = 0;
The product will be 0 in this case so it is not possible.
B = 2;
B {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 12 and 1 will be carried forward for the next step.
6A + 1 = BB = 22. Then integer value of A is not possible.
B = 6;
B {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 36 and 3 will be carried forward for the next step.
6A + 3 = BB = 66. Then integer value of A is not possible.
B = 8;
B {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 48 and 4 will be carried forward for the next step.
6A + 4 = BB = 88.
6A = 84.
A = 14
But A is single digit number.
Then value of A is not possible.
B = 4;
B {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 24 and 2 will be carried forward for the next step.
6A + 2 = BB = 44.
6A = 42.
A = 7
The multiplication is given below
74 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 444
Thus integer value of A and B is 7 and 4 respectively.

questions.question: Find the value of A & B from the following? 3A + 25 = B2
questions.explanation:
Here,  A = 5 + 2 = 7
As 7 + 5 = 12. So, 1 will be carried over. 
Now,  1 + 3 + 2 = B
B = 6
So, A = 7 and B = 6

questions.question: If the three-digit number 24x is divisible by 9, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation:
For a number to be divisible by 9, the sum of its digits must be divisible by 9.
Here, 2 + 4 = 6. Next multiple of 9 is 9.
Therefore, we will add 9 -  6 = 3. So,  the value of x = 3.

questions.question: If 21z5 is a multiple of 9, where z is a digit, find the value of z?
questions.explanation:
For a number to be divisible by 9, the sum of its digits must be divisible by 9
Here, 2 + 1 + 5 = 8
Next multiple of 9 is 9. So, we will add 9 - 8 = 1. Therefore, the value of z = 1.

questions.question: Write the following in the usual form: 100 {tex} \times {/tex} 8 + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 6 + 1 {tex} \times {/tex} 7

questions.question: Write the following in the usual form: 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 9 + 1 {tex} \times {/tex} 0
questions.explanation: 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 9 + 1 {tex} \times {/tex} 0 = 90 + 0 = 90

questions.question: Write the following in the usual form: 100 {tex} \times {/tex} 9 + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 8 + 1 {tex} \times {/tex} 7
questions.explanation:
100 {tex} \times {/tex} 9 + 10 {tex} \times {/tex} 8 + 1 {tex} \times {/tex} 7
= 900 + 80 + 7 = 987
